五稜郭公園.fee: 免費（五稜郭塔大人 1,000 円）
五稜郭公園.address: 北海道函館市五稜郭町44
五稜郭公園.access_train: 15-minute walk from Hakodate Streetcar "Goryokaku Koen Mae"
五稜郭公園.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes by car from "Hakodate Station"
五稜郭公園.desc:
Goryokaku Park is a designated Special Historic Site featuring a unique star-shaped Western-style fortification. From late April to early May, the park bursts into bloom with around 1,600 Somei Yoshino cherry trees, transforming this solemn site of the Bakumatsu era into a vibrant tapestry of colors.

The highlight is the panoramic view from the Goryokaku Tower. Standing 107 meters high, visitors can take in the stunning sight of the perfectly shaped, colorful floral carpet surrounded by the old moat, creating an impressive sense of space that beautifully showcases the grandeur of spring cherry blossoms in the northern regions.

The park's terrain is completely flat, and the pathways along the moat are well-maintained, making it very accessible for elderly visitors and strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, enchanting night illuminations enhance the ethereal reflections. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, making it the ideal time for photography and capturing the perfect mirror image of this historic city.
五稜郭公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of a giant pink star formed by 1,600 cherry blossoms at Hakodate's historic star-shaped fortress.

松前公園.types: about 250 varieties including Somei Yoshino, Nandenzakura, and Yaezakura
松前公園.hours: 24 小時開放（松前城資料館 09:00～17:00）
松前公園.fee: 免費（資料館大人 360 円）
松前公園.address: 北海道松前郡松前町松城
松前公園.access_train: Approximately 90 minutes by bus from JR 'Kikonai Station'
松前公園.access_car: About a 2-hour drive from Hakodate city center
松前公園.desc:
Matsumae Park, built on the historic site of Matsumae Castle from the Edo period, is one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. With around 10,000 cherry trees of 250 different varieties blooming in succession, the park boasts a month-long flowering period, painting the majestic ancient castle walls and restored wooden towers in early summer colors.

The most iconic view here is the classical dialogue between the 'Tenshukaku, Matsumae Shrine, and the vibrant sea of flowers.' Colorful blooms line the gentle gravel paths, with brilliant Shidarezakura and Nandenzakura cascading down, creating a serene scene that perfectly embodies the tranquil historical Zen spirit of the ancient castle.

The park's terrain is mostly flat, and the old pathways have been well-maintained, making it very accessible for wheelchair users and strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, the famous dreamlike illuminations at night enhance the ethereal reflections. Early mornings on weekdays are the least crowded, making it the perfect time for capturing the park's timeless beauty.
松前公園.tagline: Discover the only Edo castle site in Hokkaido, where Matsumae Castle meets a sea of 10,000 cherry blossoms.

二十間道路櫻並木.address: 北海道新ひだか町静内田原
二十間道路櫻並木.access_train: Take a shuttle bus from JR Tomakomai Station to Shizunai Station, then transfer to a bus for about 15 minutes.
二十間道路櫻並木.access_car: About a 40-minute drive from Hidaka Tokka IC on the Hidaka Expressway.
二十間道路櫻並木.tagline: Experience a breathtaking 7-kilometer avenue lined with 3,000 cherry blossoms in Shizunai Town.
二十間道路櫻並木.desc:
Nijukken Road Sakura Namiki is a stunning 7-kilometer tree-lined avenue named for its width of exactly 20 ken (approximately 36 meters), and it ranks among Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Every year in early May, the towering 3,000 giant Oyamazakura trees planted neatly along both sides of the road burst into a vibrant pink spectacle.

The most captivating visual appeal lies in the "massive, three-dimensional colorful carpet that seems to stretch infinitely along the road." Whether you're driving slowly or stopping by the roadside, you'll be surrounded by the vivid, deep pink feather-like leaves. The uniformly shaped tree canopies intertwine in the sky, creating a spacious and visually striking atmosphere.

As this location is essentially an open public roadway, please be cautious of passing vehicles while walking and searching for the perfect photo angle. This world-class destination often sees a surge of traffic during peak bloom on weekends, so it's highly recommended to visit on weekday mornings before dawn for the best lighting effects.

圓山公園.address: 北海道札幌市中央区大通西28丁目
圓山公園.access_train: 5-minute walk from Enryaku Park Station on the Tozai Line
圓山公園.access_car: Best accessed by subway (located in Sapporo city)
圓山公園.desc:
Enryaku Park is Kyoto's oldest designated urban park, celebrated as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From late March to early April, over 600 cherry trees, including Somei Yoshino and Shidarezakura, burst into vibrant bloom, transforming this sacred area near Yasaka Shrine into a breathtaking tapestry of colors.

The park's centerpiece is the iconic Gion Shidarezakura, a massive weeping cherry tree that stands as a stunning pink torch. When the evening lights illuminate it, the scene becomes expansive and poetic, framed beautifully by the vintage pond and drifting cherry blossom rafts.

The park's flat terrain and well-paved paths make it incredibly accessible for seniors and strollers alike. During the cherry blossom festival, the lively Gion Yozakura stalls offer a delightful experience. For the best tranquility, visit early in the morning before dawn to capture the serene lake surface and perfect reflections of the blooming flowers.
圓山公園.tagline: Discover Kyoto's historic urban oasis, where the iconic Gion Shidarezakura blooms under the night sky.

莫埃來沼公園.address: 北海道札幌市東区モエレ沼公園1-1
莫埃來沼公園.access_train: About 25 minutes by bus from the Toho Line "Kansaitsu Higashi Station"
莫埃來沼公園.access_car: Approximately 30 minutes drive from downtown Sapporo
莫埃來沼公園.tagline: Step into the geometric oasis crafted by master sculptor Noguchi Isamu, where glass pyramids meet a valley of cherry blossoms.
莫埃來沼公園.desc:
莫埃來沼公園 is a stunning modern landscape art park that has transformed a former waste disposal site into a perfect embodiment of geometric beauty. On the eastern side of the park, the "Sakura no Mori" features around 1,900 Oyamazakura cherry trees, painting the lush fields with vibrant colors in early May.

The park's most striking modern composition is the breathtaking "giant glass pyramid, pure white hidden play structures, and a century of pink foliage" all in one frame. Stroll along the smooth central path, flanked by flourishing cherry trees embracing the steel structures, and climb up to the high lookout point for a panoramic view of nature harmoniously blending with modern architecture.

The park's vast interior is gently undulating, with fully accessible, flat pathways, making it ideal for renting bikes at the entrance for easy exploration. The cherry blossom area, nestled among the shady groves, offers a cool and comfortable walking experience, perfect for families planning a half-day eco-friendly outing on a weekday morning.

北海道神宮.types: Ezo cherry, Somei Yoshino cherry, Yaezakura, plum
北海道神宮.count: approximately 1,100 trees (the simultaneous bloom of cherry and plum is a highlight)
北海道神宮.address: 北海道札幌市中央区宮ケ丘474
北海道神宮.access_train: 15-minute walk from Maruyama Park Station on the Tozai Line
北海道神宮.access_car: about a 15-minute drive from downtown Sapporo
北海道神宮.desc:
Hokkaido Jingu, nestled within Maruyama Park, is a world-class shrine with over a century of history and deep cultural significance. Due to the unique climate of the northern region, every year in early May, 1,400 cherry trees and 250 plum trees miraculously bloom at the same time, adorning the sacred grounds with their beauty.

The epitome of Japanese aesthetic is found in the dialogue between the wooden shrine, ancient torii gate, and the surrounding flowers. The elegant Somei Yoshino and Yaezakura cherry blossoms embrace rustic stone lanterns and towering ancient trees, creating a serene atmosphere that perfectly embodies the tranquil history of this ancient shrine, illuminated by dappled sunlight filtering through the leaves.

The path to the main hall is completely flat, with well-maintained gravel walkways, making it very accessible for seniors and strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, the shrine offers its specialty, freshly baked “Rokkatei Hanjou” mochi and limited-edition goshuin. Visiting in the early morning on weekdays is ideal, as there are fewer visitors, allowing for a peaceful and ethereal experience.
北海道神宮.tagline: Experience the sacred beauty of Hokkaido Jingu, where 1,400 cherry and plum blossoms bloom in harmony.

旭山公園.hours: 24 小時開放（賞櫻期夜間有點燈）
旭山公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Asahikawa Station towards Asahiyama Zoo for about 40 minutes.
旭山公園.access_car: Approximately a 20-minute drive from the Asahikawa North IC on the Dōō Expressway.
旭山公園.tagline: Ascend Asahikawa's heights and feast your eyes on a breathtaking panorama of the Ishikari Plain and 3,000 wild cherry blossoms.
旭山公園.desc:
Asahiyama Park is a vast natural park located right next to Asahiyama Zoo, nestled at the foot of Asahiyama Mountain. Due to its high latitude inland location, the blooming season here is one of the latest in all of Japan, with wild Oshima cherry blossoms reaching their peak from early to mid-May, painting the lush green hills in vibrant colors.

The visual highlight is the "three-dimensional floral wall view created by the hilly terrain and the expansive Ishikari mountain range." Stroll along the winding paths to the summit, where colorful blossoms intertwine with towering coniferous forests. From the highest observation deck, you can take in the entire floral carpet and simultaneously gaze over the vast plain.

The park's well-maintained trails are gently undulating, making it perfect for family picnics and leisurely strolls. The elevated terrain offers little shade, so it's highly recommended to bring sun protection when visiting for flower viewing and photography. For the best experience, visit on a weekday morning to capture the ethereal light of the open fields.

弘前公園.types: over 50 varieties including Somei Yoshino, Shidarezakura, and Yaezakura
弘前公園.fee: 大人 310 円（本丸/北之郭區域）
弘前公園.address: 青森県弘前市下白銀町1
弘前公園.access_train: About 15 minutes by loop bus from JR Hirosaki Station
弘前公園.access_car: About 20 minutes from the Tohoku Expressway Owani-Hirosaki IC
弘前公園.desc:
Hirosaki Park, built on the historic site of Hirosaki Castle, is recognized as Japan's top cherry blossom viewing spot and is part of the country's famous cherry blossom hundred selections. From late April to early May each year, over 2,600 ancient cherry trees of about 50 varieties burst into bloom, enveloping the entire historic castle walls.

The most stunning visual highlight is the legendary "sakura no juutan" (cherry blossom raft). After the peak bloom, the fallen petals blanket the calm old moat, creating a pink river that covers the water's surface without a ripple, blending motion and stillness in a magnificent display.

The park features completely flat terrain, and the pathways along the moat are well-maintained, making it very accessible for seniors and strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, enchanting illumination events at night enhance the ethereal reflections. Early morning visits on weekdays see the fewest crowds, providing the perfect opportunity to capture stunning photos of this historic castle.
弘前公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking pink river of cherry blossoms at Hirosaki Castle's Edo-era grounds.

蘆野公園.address: 青森県五所川原市金木町芦野
蘆野公園.access_train: Get off at Tsugaru Railway "Ashi no Koen Station" for direct access
蘆野公園.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes from Goshogawara Kita IC on the Tsugaru Expressway.
蘆野公園.tagline: Discover the hidden gem of Goshogawara: a vintage train journey through a tunnel of cherry blossoms.
蘆野公園.desc:
Ashi no Koen, located in the hometown of Dazai Osamu, Kanagi-machi, is a scenic park surrounding Ashi no Lake, recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From late April to early May each year, the park comes alive with around 1,500 cherry trees, including Somei Yoshino and Shidarezakura, creating a vibrant floral carpet that adorns the gentle lakeside paths.

The park's visual highlight is the "romantic duo of vintage railways and colorful flower carpets." Stroll leisurely along the stepped pathways, and as the orange-bodied retro Tsugaru Railway train slowly approaches Cherry Blossom Station, it perfectly frames the surrounding walls of blossoms, blending dynamic movement with nostalgic charm.

The park is open for free exploration, but some observation bridges have limited width, so please avoid blocking pathways and do not cross the railway's yellow line. As a hidden treasure for train enthusiasts, train schedules are infrequent, so be sure to check the latest timetable before your visit. Arriving in the early morning offers the best chance to capture stunning photos of the train amidst the blooming flowers.

十和田市官廳街通.address: 青森県十和田市西十二番町
十和田市官廳街通.access_train: About 35 minutes by bus from 'Nanbu-Towada Station'
十和田市官廳街通.access_car: Approximately 30 minutes drive from 'Hyakushaku Road' via Towada Hyakushaku IC
十和田市官廳街通.tagline: Experience Japan's scenic corridor, a stunning mile-long avenue lined with majestic pines and vibrant cherry blossoms.
十和田市官廳街通.desc:
Towada City Kanchogai Street is a central scenic corridor that runs through the heart of the city, recognized as one of Japan's top 100 roads. From late April to early May, this straight 1.1-kilometer road transforms into a colorful floral pathway, flanked by 156 towering pines and 165 Yoshino cherry trees.

The highlight here is the "lush green pines, pink cherry blossoms, and modern art sculptures creating a stunning backdrop." Stroll along the smooth central walkway, where the lush branches intertwine overhead, perfectly framing the large, colorful outdoor installations of the Towada City Museum of Contemporary Art nestled among the trees, showcasing refined modern aesthetics.

Located in the bustling city center, the spot offers excellent access and amenities, making it very stroller and wheelchair-friendly. During cherry blossom season, the municipal building's observation deck opens for free, allowing visitors to enjoy a panoramic view of the expansive, colorful floral carpet. Arriving early on weekdays before dawn provides the best opportunity to capture pristine street scenes and exceptional urban lighting for photography.

世界第一櫻花街道.address: 青森県弘前市十腰內（岩木山周邊）
世界第一櫻花街道.access_train: Take a bus from JR Hirosaki Station to Kuroki-daira, about 50 minutes.
世界第一櫻花街道.access_car: About a 30-minute drive from Hirosaki city center.
世界第一櫻花街道.tagline: Experience a breathtaking 20-kilometer cherry blossom corridor at the foot of Iwaki Mountain.
世界第一櫻花街道.desc:
The Sekai Daiichi Sakura Kaido (世界第一櫻花街道) is located at the base of the sacred Iwaki Mountain. Over the years, local residents have come together to carefully cultivate and protect an impressive 6,500 Ooyama cherry trees along the mountain road, creating a world-class corridor that stretches for 20 kilometers from late April to early May.

The most striking visual appeal lies in the "meandering mountain road adorned with a three-dimensional band of deep pink blossoms." Due to the significant elevation changes, the vibrant, full deep pink flower clusters bloom progressively with the mountain slopes, creating a stunning contrast against the majestic, snow-capped peak of Iwaki Mountain in the distance. The scene offers a harmonious blend of strength and softness, with a vast and powerful spatial perspective.

As this attraction is essentially an open public roadway, and some sections are narrower, please be cautious of passing vehicles while driving slowly or stopping for photos. The mountain foothills can feel slightly cool, so be sure to bring a warm jacket for your photo sessions. Visiting on a clear morning with good visibility will ensure the highest color saturation.

合浦公園.address: 青森県青森市合浦1丁目
合浦公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Aomori Station for about 20 minutes to Gappo Park Mae.
合浦公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Aomori Chuo IC on the Aomori Expressway.
合浦公園.tagline: A coastal oasis of cherry blossoms and sea breezes awaits you at Gappo Park!
合浦公園.desc:
Gappo Park is located on the eastern side of Aomori City and is recognized as one of Japan's top 100 urban parks. This rare coastal cherry blossom haven allows you to step right onto the beach. From late April to early May, the park bursts into life with around 600 cherry trees, including Somei Yoshino and Shidarezakura, creating a vibrant natural spectacle.

The visual highlight is the breathtaking blend of the 'unstoppable view of Mutsu Bay, sandy beaches, and colorful flower walls.' Pink flower ribbons bloom along the gentle coastline. As the sea breeze sweeps across the water, pink petals dance through the air, falling onto the beach and showcasing the unique beauty of this port city.

The park's pathways are well-maintained and completely flat, making it very accessible for strollers and seniors. There are also popular night market experiences with food stalls within the park. Visiting during sunset on a clear day is particularly romantic, as the golden hues of the setting sun cast a warm glow over the sea and the vibrant flower fields before you.

北上展勝地.types: About 150 varieties including Somei Yoshino and Yamazakura
北上展勝地.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Kitakami Station or take a boat (during cherry blossom season)
北上展勝地.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from Tohoku Expressway Kitakami Etsuko IC
北上展勝地.desc:
Kitakami Tenshochi is located by the banks of the Kitakami River, ranking alongside Hirosaki Park and Kakunodate as one of the top three cherry blossom spots in Tohoku, and is included in Japan's top 100 cherry blossom sites. Every year from mid to late April, the 2-kilometer-long straight path is lined with approximately 10,000 ancient Somei Yoshino cherry trees, planted during the Taisho era, all blooming in unison to create a stunning pink tunnel.

The most iconic scene online features the "clear Kitakami River, traditional sightseeing carriages, and the colorful foliage in perfect harmony." Stroll along the well-maintained, gentle gravel path, where the sound of vintage carriage hooves echoes through the full blossom tunnel, while hundreds of koi flags flutter above the river, creating a majestic atmosphere.

The area is extensive and completely flat, making it perfect for a leisurely hike or a scenic boat ride. During the cherry blossom festival, enchanting night illuminations are held, and visiting early in the morning on weekdays offers the least crowds. At this time, sunlight filters through the treetops, casting a beautiful glow on the floral walls, making it ideal for capturing fresh, airy photographs.
北上展勝地.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of cherry blossoms along the Kitakami River's scenic corridor.

高松公園.address: 岩手県盛岡市高松1丁目
高松公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR 'Morioka Station' for about 15 minutes to 'Takamatsu Ike-guchi'.
高松公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from 'Morioka IC' on the Tohoku Expressway.
高松公園.tagline: Discover the breathtaking beauty of Takamatsu Park, where cherry blossoms and majestic Iwate Mountain create a stunning backdrop.
高松公園.desc:
Takamatsu Park (Takamatsu no Ike) is a sprawling waterfront park built around the Edo period's South Nanbu feudal lord's water control dam, Takamatsu Pond. It is recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Every year, from mid to late April, around 800 Yoshino cherry trees bloom along the lakeshore, transforming the water's edge into a vibrant tapestry of colors.

The visual highlight is the magnificent Iwate Mountain, the lakeshore, and the colorful floral walls creating a stunning mirror effect. Pink and pure white flower bands stretch along the waterfront path, and when the calm, mirror-like surface of the water perfectly reflects the majestic 'Nanbu Fuji' snow-capped peak and the colorful forest, the scene feels expansive and poetic.

The park's interior is flat, with well-maintained paths that are very wheelchair and stroller-friendly. You can rent paddle boats to enjoy the lake, admiring the three-dimensional colorful flower blanket cascading down the shore from the water's perspective. Set aside an hour for a leisurely stroll, ideally in the early morning when crowds are minimal, allowing you to capture perfect reflections and the fresh air of the fields.

石割櫻.count: 1 tree (approximately 400 years old, national natural monument)
石割櫻.hours: 24 小時開放（位於盛岡地方裁判所內）
石割櫻.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from JR Morioka Station
石割櫻.access_car: Recommended to walk or use nearby parking at the court.
石割櫻.tagline: Discover a miracle at the Morioka District Court, where a 400-year-old cherry blossom thrives in a granite crevice.
石割櫻.desc:
Ishiwari Zakura is located in the courtyard of the Morioka District Court, a national natural monument and a renowned cherry blossom wonder. This massive Edo-higan cherry tree, over 400 years old, grows in a 21-meter-long granite crevice, bursting into vibrant pink blooms every mid-April.

The most striking aspect of this living beauty lies in the "stark contrast between the hard, massive rock and the gentle cherry blossoms." The sturdy, ancient trunk rises majestically from the cracked white stone, while the lush, colorful flower clusters envelop the solemn court building, showcasing the refined historical landscape of Japan.

Conveniently located in the heart of Morioka, the site features well-maintained protective fencing, making it easy and comfortable to stroll around. As a single, extraordinary giant tree attraction, a leisurely walk and photo session will take about twenty minutes. For the best light and a chance to capture stunning, unobstructed views, visit early in the morning before dawn.

盛岡城跡公園.access_train: Take the JR 'Morioka Station' and ride the 'Dendenmushi' loop bus for about 10 minutes.
盛岡城跡公園.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from the Morioka IC on the Tohoku Expressway.
盛岡城跡公園.tagline: Experience the majestic stone walls and vibrant cherry blossoms at this historic castle site.
盛岡城跡公園.desc:
盛岡城跡公園, built on the site of the Edo period's Morioka Castle, is recognized as one of Japan's top 100 castles. Every year from mid to late April, around 250 cherry trees, including Somei Yoshino and Shidarezakura, burst into bloom, adding a splash of color to the grand granite stone walls.

The park's visual highlight is the unique contrast of the sturdy stone walls and the delicate blossoms. Strolling along the well-maintained paths, you'll witness the pink and deep purple flower clusters beautifully complementing the ancient, robust white stone structures, showcasing the distinctive charm of this northern castle.

Built on a hilly terrain, some paths have inclines, so wearing good non-slip sports shoes is highly recommended for flower viewing. The park features spacious areas and a large lawn, making it a comfortable retreat during the summer. Early mornings on weekdays are the least crowded, perfect for capturing the historical beauty of this ancient castle.

小岩井農場一本櫻.count: 1 tree (classic composition with Iwate Mountain in the background)
小岩井農場一本櫻.address: 岩手県岩手郡雫石町丸谷地36-1
小岩井農場一本櫻.access_train: About a 35-minute bus ride from JR Morioka Station towards Koiwai Farm.
小岩井農場一本櫻.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from the Morioka IC on the Tohoku Expressway.
小岩井農場一本櫻.tagline: A century-old cherry blossom stands proud at the foot of majestic Iwate, capturing the beauty of green pastures and a single grand sakura.
小岩井農場一本櫻.desc:
Koiwai Farm's Ipponzakura is located beside a historic, expansive private farm. This solitary, century-old Edo-higan cherry tree stands tall in the midst of vast green pastures. From late April to early May, it reaches its peak bloom, transforming into a stunning postcard-worthy scene known throughout Japan.

The most breathtaking view is the perfect harmony of the ‘Residual Snow Iwate Mountain’ as a backdrop, the wide fields, and the solitary giant tree. The majestic ‘Nanbu Fuji’ volcano rises in the background, while the endless green grass stretches at your feet. The proud pink canopy blooms magnificently in this grand space, creating an awe-inspiring atmosphere.

To protect the pasture ecosystem and tree roots, strict no-entry barriers surround Ipponzakura. Visitors can take photos from designated viewing areas along the dedicated road. The elevated terrain is mostly exposed with strong winds, so be sure to prepare for windy conditions. The clear morning light best illuminates the pink canopy, showcasing its vibrant colors at their peak.

嚴美溪.types: Somei Yoshino cherry, Edo Bihankakura cherry
嚴美溪.address: 岩手県一関市厳美町字滝ノ上
嚴美溪.access_train: Take a bus for about 20 minutes from JR "Ichinoseki Station" to "Genbikei" Station.
嚴美溪.access_car: About a 10-minute drive from "Tohoku Expressway" Ichinoseki IC.
嚴美溪.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of cherry blossoms and majestic cliffs at the stunning Genbikei Gorge.
嚴美溪.desc:
Genbikei is a remarkable gorge carved by the Iwai River over the years, famous for its steep cliffs and unique pothole formations. From mid to late April, around 200 Somei Yoshino cherry trees bloom in unison along the riverbanks, transforming this natural wonder into a vibrant and lively spectacle.

The most visually stunning moment comes from the dialogue between the "crystal-clear stream, the peculiar rock formations, and the pink flower walls." Standing on the Tenkou Bridge, you can witness the rushing stream creating white splashes as it weaves through the blooming floral canopy. Additionally, the famous local delicacy "Kugokudango," delivered across the river by a ropeway, offers a classic cultural experience during your flower viewing.

The gorge's walking trail is flat and well-maintained, making it perfect for a leisurely family hike along the riverside. Since many of the main viewpoints are located on open rocky plateaus, the summer midday sun can be intense, so it's highly recommended to bring sun protection and visit in the morning for the most comfortable experience and the best photographic opportunities.

船岡城址公園.fee: 免費（纜車大人往返 500 円）
船岡城址公園.address: 宮城県柴田郡柴田町船岡中央2-1
船岡城址公園.access_car: 20-minute drive from Shiroishi IC on the Tohoku Expressway
船岡城址公園.tagline: Ride the vintage slope car for breathtaking views of Shiroishi River's cherry blossoms and a vibrant floral tapestry.
船岡城址公園.desc:
Funakoshi Castle Ruins Park is built on the historical site of the Shibata clan's castle from the Edo period, and along with the nearby Shiroishi River embankment, it is recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From early to mid-April, the entire hillside is enveloped by around 1,300 stunning Somei Yoshino cherry trees, transforming the landscape into a magnificent, colorful floral carpet.

The most iconic experience here is riding the "slope car" (スロープカー). Visitors can hop on the small monorail that gently climbs the steep slope, with both sides of the window framed by dense walls of pink blossoms. At the summit, you’ll find a towering 24-meter statue of the Peace Kannon, offering expansive panoramic views.

Thanks to the seamless connection via the Shibata Chizakura Bridge to the Shiroishi River embankment, it's perfect for a full-day cherry blossom viewing itinerary. The hilly terrain includes some steps and slopes, so wearing comfortable running shoes is highly recommended. Early morning visits on weekdays are the best time to capture the stunning scenery without the crowds.

白石川堤一目千本櫻.address: 宮城県柴田郡大河原町・柴田町
白石川堤一目千本櫻.access_train: About a 3-minute walk from JR "Ogawara Station" or "Funaboko Station"
白石川堤一目千本櫻.access_car: During cherry blossom season, traffic can be heavy, so taking the JR is highly recommended.
白石川堤一目千本櫻.desc:
Shiroishigawa Tei Ichimoku Senbonzakura stretches along the Shiroishi River for 8 kilometers, earning its place among Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From early to mid-April, around 1,200 cherry trees, primarily ancient Somei Yoshino, burst into bloom, transforming the gentle riverbanks into an endless, vibrant floral carpet.

The most breathtaking view features the "majestic Zao mountain range, the clear Shiroishi River, and a spectacular display of cherry blossoms" all in one frame. Standing on either Kijinshi Dam or Shibata Senbonzakura Bridge, the mirror-like water perfectly reflects the distant white snow-capped mountains and the nearby pink flower wall, creating an awe-inspiring sense of space.

The riverside promenade is completely flat, making it very friendly for strollers and joggers alike. During the cherry blossom festival, you can enjoy a traditional "hanami yakata-bune" boat ride on the river, and trains on the JR Tohoku Main Line slow down as they pass through this scenic area. For the best mirror-like reflections, visit on weekday mornings.
白石川堤一目千本櫻.tagline: Experience a breathtaking 8-kilometer cherry blossom corridor against the majestic backdrop of the Zao mountain range.

榴岡公園.address: 宮城県仙台市宮城野区榴ケ岡
榴岡公園.access_train: Just a short walk from JR Sendai Line 'Tsurugawa Station'
榴岡公園.access_car: Approximately a 20-minute drive from 'Sendai Miyagi IC' on the Tohoku Expressway.
榴岡公園.tagline: Step into the historic garden of the Date clan and witness 300 stunning weeping cherry trees.
榴岡公園.desc:
榴岡公園 is a renowned cherry blossom viewing spot introduced by Date Tsunamura, the fourth lord of the Sendai domain, who brought rare cherry varieties from Kyoto. Every year from mid to late April, over 300 weeping cherry trees burst into bloom, transforming this urban oasis into a vibrant spectacle of color and life.

The park's core visual feature is the elegant blend of weeping cherry trees, lush green lawns, and the vintage wooden historical exhibition hall. The pink floral walls undulate with the natural hills, beautifully contrasting with the preserved Edo period 'Old Army Infantry Barracks,' creating a rich historical ambiance.

Conveniently located near the city center with completely flat terrain, the main walking paths are well-maintained, making it very friendly for strollers and elderly visitors. The park features a large grassy plaza, bustling with vendors during the cherry blossom festival, making it perfect for a comfortable family picnic on a sunny morning.

西公園.address: 福岡県福岡市中央区西公園
西公園.access_car: 5-minute drive from the urban expressway "Nishi Koen Ramp"
西公園.tagline: Discover the serene oasis of Sendai, where vintage steam trains meet blooming cherry blossoms.
西公園.desc:
Nishi Koen is nestled along the clear waters of Hirose River, making it the oldest traditional nature park in Sendai. From early to mid-April, the park's expansive pathways and flower fields come alive with hundreds of Shidarezakura cherry trees, painting the bustling urban area with a gentle early summer hue.

The park's most iconic scene features the dynamic contrast of a vintage C60 steam locomotive against a backdrop of full-bloom cherry blossoms. The sturdy black train stands quietly on the grassy field, surrounded by vibrant pink floral walls, creating a beautifully harmonious visual experience.

The park's walking paths are well-maintained and accessible, making it perfect for travelers looking to enjoy a leisurely morning stroll. During the cherry blossom festival, lively night illuminations and food stalls bring a festive atmosphere. Visiting on weekday mornings offers the least crowds, ideal for capturing the steam train in a picturesque dialogue with the blooming flowers.

鹽竈神社.types: Shiogama cherry, Somei Yoshino cherry, Shidare cherry
鹽竈神社.count: about 300 trees (including the national natural monument Shiogama cherry)
鹽竈神社.access_train: 15-minute walk from JR Senseki Line 'Hon-Shiogama Station'
鹽竈神社.access_car: About a 10-minute drive from 'Rifu-naka IC' on the Sanriku Expressway
鹽竈神社.tagline: Discover the sacred beauty of Japan's first shrine, where the rare Shiogama cherry blossoms bloom.
鹽竈神社.desc:
Shiogama Jinja is a historic shrine with over 1,200 years of heritage, revered as the first shrine of Oshu and designated as an Important Cultural Property. From mid-April to early May, the shrine's majestic vermillion halls and ancient torii gates are surrounded by around 300 cherry trees of 40 different varieties, creating a breathtaking display of sacred beauty.

The highlight is the rare Shiogama cherry (シオガマザクラ), a national natural monument. With 40 to 50 delicate petals resembling exquisite hydrangeas, these deep pink blossoms beautifully complement the ancient stone lanterns and the historic worship hall, embodying the serene Zen spirit unique to this shrine.

To reach the main hall, you'll need to climb 202 steep stone steps along the historical approach (Omotesando). If you prefer a gentler route, the eastern approach offers an easier path. The shrine grounds feature natural gravel pathways, so wearing comfortable walking shoes is recommended. Visiting early on weekdays allows you to fully enjoy the ethereal atmosphere of this sacred domain.

三神峯公園.types: Somei Yoshino, Shidarezakura, Satozakura (Sendai Sakura), and 45 more
三神峯公園.address: 宮城県仙台市太白区三神峯1
三神峯公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Sendai Station for about 30 minutes to "Sanjinbō Park Mae."
三神峯公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Sendai Minami IC on the Tohoku Expressway.
三神峯公園.tagline: Experience a vibrant tapestry of 48 cherry blossom varieties in the scenic hills of Sendai.
三神峯公園.desc:
Sanjinbō Park, nestled in the hilly outskirts of southern Sendai, is the city's most diverse cherry blossom oasis. Every year from mid to late April, around 750 cherry trees of 48 different varieties bloom exuberantly along the natural slopes, transforming the hillside into a colorful spectacle.

The visual highlight is the "encyclopedic density of blossoms and the layered space of a colorful floral carpet." From the delicate single-petal Somei Yoshino to the lush double-petal Yaezakura and drooping Shidarezakura, the flowers bloom in a delightful sequence. Climbing up to the grassy plaza offers stunning views of Sendai city and the Pacific Ocean, showcasing beautiful lines and perspectives.

The park features a flat and spacious terrain, well-maintained with a vast grassy meadow, making it perfect for families to enjoy leisurely strolls and picnics, while the atmosphere remains relatively tranquil for visitors. On sunny summer days, the midday sun can be quite intense, so it's highly recommended to bring sun protection. Arriving in the morning allows for capturing the most saturated and three-dimensional floral shadows in the air.

角館武家屋敷.address: 秋田県仙北市角館町表町下丁
角館武家屋敷.access_car: about 40 minutes by car from Omagari IC on the Akita Expressway
角館武家屋敷.tagline: Explore the enchanting Edo-era streets of Kakunodate, where ancient black walls meet 400 blooming cherry trees.
角館武家屋敷.desc:
Kakunodate Bukeyashiki Street is a beautifully preserved historical area that showcases the elegant residences of mid to high-ranking samurai from the Edo period, recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From late April to early May, the broad streets lined with black earthen walls come alive with around 400 ancient weeping cherry trees, brought in by the feudal lord from Kyoto, reaching their peak bloom.

The visual highlight here is the striking contrast between the "heavy black wooden walls, samurai longhouse gates, and the pink weeping cherry blossoms." Vivid pink blossoms cascade like waterfalls from the walls, hanging gracefully between the solemn black walls and the rustic moss, creating a harmonious blend of historical elegance that has been preserved since the Edo period.

The historical street is completely flat, with wide and well-maintained pathways, making it very friendly for elderly visitors and those with strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, a magical lighting event takes place at night, enhancing the ethereal reflections of the ancient walls. Early morning visits on regular days see the least crowds, making it the perfect time for photography and capturing serene, empty scenes.

檜木內川堤.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Kakunodate Station (adjacent to Bukeyashiki)
檜木內川堤.tagline: Stroll through Kakunodate's cherry blossom tunnel, a breathtaking 2-kilometer stretch of blooming Yoshino cherry trees.
檜木內川堤.desc:
Hinokinai River Promenade, located next to Kakunodate's Bukeyashiki Street, is a scenic walkway that extends along both banks of the clear Hinokinai River for 2 kilometers, recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From late April to early May each year, the towering 400 ancient Yoshino cherry trees bloom in perfect synchrony, reaching their peak beauty.

The greatest visual charm lies in the interplay of the clear stream and the pink tunnel of blossoms. As you leisurely stroll along the flat riverbank path, the lush branches overhead weave together in the sky, creating the sensation of traversing an endless colorful tunnel. When the gentle breeze blows, the vibrant sea of flowers contrasts beautifully with the distant snow-capped peaks, creating a majestic atmosphere.

The riverside walkway is completely flat and well-maintained, making it very friendly for strollers and joggers alike, and it is open to the public free of charge all day. It can be easily linked with the Bukeyashiki old street for a well-planned day trip. It is highly recommended to visit in the early morning, when you can capture stunning reflections of the clear water and the perfect cluster of blossoms.

千秋公園.address: 秋田県秋田市千秋公園1-39
千秋公園.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Akita Station
千秋公園.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from Akita Chuo IC on the Akita Expressway
千秋公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of cherry blossoms at the historic site of Kubota Castle.
千秋公園.desc:
Senboku Park is built on the historic grounds of Kubota Castle, once home to the Satake clan during the Edo period, and is recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From mid to late April, hundreds of Somei Yoshino cherry trees and Edo-honkan sakura burst into bloom, adorning the majestic earthen mounds and restored wooden guardhouses with vibrant colors of early summer.

The park's core feature lies in its "classic scenery of the retro moat, robust castle walls, and blooming flowers." Colorful blossoms flourish along the gentle waterways, and when the tranquil water perfectly reflects the white-walled, tiled-roofed classical arched bridge of Gozukike and the vibrant foliage, the scene opens up, showcasing elegant Japanese aesthetics and the grandeur of the Satake family.

The park's terrain is mostly gentle hills, with well-maintained main paths that make for a comfortable stroll. Within the park, you'll find the famous tea house, Sen-an, and during the cherry blossom festival, numerous vendors set up, making it perfect for travelers seeking a leisurely morning historical walk. Early mornings on weekdays are the least crowded, ideal for capturing stunning reflections in photos.

真人公園.address: 秋田県横手市増田町増田字真人岱
真人公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Jūmonji Station for about 20 minutes to "真人公園前."
真人公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Jūmonji IC on the Yuzawa Yokote Road.
真人公園.tagline: Discover the timeless beauty of 真人公園, where 2,000 cherry blossoms create a stunning mirror with the iconic Taiko Bridge.
真人公園.desc:
真人公園 is a beautifully preserved classical stroll garden from the Taisho era, renowned for its breathtaking views of Mount 真人. It is listed among Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From late April to early May, around 2,000 Somei Yoshino and mountain cherry trees bloom spectacularly around the expansive pond, 真人沼.

The visual highlight is the "ultimate classical waterside pond, the vermilion Taiko Bridge, and the mirror-like interplay of flowers." Colorful blossoms line the gentle gravel paths, and when the calm, mirror-like water perfectly reflects the vintage wooden bridge and vibrant foliage, the scene opens up, exuding an elegant Eastern Zen and Taisho romance.

The park features flat terrain and well-maintained pathways, making it very accessible for seniors and strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, traditional local folk activities take place, showcasing the unique local culture. Early mornings see the fewest crowds, making it the best time to capture stunning reflections in the sunlight.

本莊公園.hours: 24 小時開放（賞櫻期夜間點燈）
本莊公園.address: 秋田県由利本荘市本荘字荒町
本莊公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Ugo-Honjo Station
本莊公園.access_car: Approximately a 10-minute drive from Honjo IC on the Nihonkai-Tohoku Expressway
本莊公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of cherry blossoms reflecting on ancient castle moats.
本莊公園.desc:
Honjo Park is built on the historic site of Honjo Castle, once home to the Honjo clan during the Edo period. It stands as a quintessential cherry blossom oasis in the Dewa region. Each year, from mid to late April, around 1,000 stunning Somei Yoshino cherry trees bloom vibrantly around the remnants of the old castle walls, painting the ancient ruins in the colors of early summer.

The park's core feature is the harmonious interplay of the old castle's dry moat, tranquil ponds, and colorful flower walls. Pink flower walls stretch along the gentle waterways, and when the calm water perfectly reflects the restored historical gates and vibrant foliage, the scene feels expansive and refreshing, showcasing the grandeur of this historic city.

The park's flat terrain and well-maintained paths make it ideal for families to stroll leisurely while savoring history. The main cherry blossom viewing routes are very stroller and elderly-friendly. Set aside an hour for a leisurely walk, especially in the early morning when the crowds are minimal, making it the perfect time for capturing stunning photographs of this historic castle's picturesque landscape.

霞城公園.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Yamagata Station
霞城公園.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from Yamagata North IC on the Yamagata Expressway
霞城公園.tagline: Experience a breathtaking sea of cherry blossoms at the historic site of Yamagata Castle.
霞城公園.desc:
Kajo Park is built on the historic grounds of Yamagata Castle, once home to the feudal lord of the Yamagata domain, and is listed among Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From mid to late April, the park bursts into bloom with around 1,500 Somei Yoshino cherry trees, adding a layer of pink to the majestic stone walls and reconstructed wooden castle tower.

The park's core feature is its classical scenery, showcasing the retro castle moat, sturdy stone walls, and cherry blossoms. Colorful blooms line the gentle waterways, and when the tranquil water perfectly reflects the white walls and tiled roofs of the Higashi Otemon gate alongside the full cherry blossom canopy, the passing trains on the Ou Main Line create an elegant scene of Japanese aesthetic beauty.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained stone paths that are very friendly for elderly visitors and strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, enchanting nighttime illuminations enhance the ethereal reflections. For the best experience, visit early in the morning on weekdays when the crowds are minimal, perfect for capturing stunning photos of this historic castle.

烏帽子山公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR "Akayu Station"
烏帽子山公園.access_car: About a 10-minute drive from "Tōhoku Chūō Expressway" Nanyo Takahata IC
烏帽子山公園.tagline: Ascend to breathtaking views and witness Japan's largest stone torii and a thousand weeping cherry blossoms.
烏帽子山公園.desc:
Ubozuyama Park is nestled in the mountains behind Akayu Onsen, recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots and affectionately known as the starting point of the "Oshimazakura Corridor." From mid to late April each year, the slopes burst into color with around a thousand Edo-higan and weeping cherry trees, transforming the lush green hills into a vibrant spectacle.

The park's most iconic feature is the "dialogue between Japan's largest stone torii and the pink weeping cherry blossoms." Ancient trees twist and turn, with vivid pink blooms cascading like waterfalls from the rocky cliffs, beautifully contrasting with the rustic Ubozuyama Hachiman Shrine, showcasing a refined aesthetic of historical elegance.

The hilly terrain requires some climbing to reach the torii and observation deck, so wearing good non-slip sports shoes is highly recommended for cherry blossom viewing. While strolling, you can enjoy a panoramic view of the entire onsen town. Early mornings on weekdays are particularly serene, making it the perfect time for capturing the timeless beauty of the scenery.

天童公園.count: about 2,000 trees (famous for the Ningen Shogi event)
天童公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Tendo Station to the base of the mountain
天童公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Yamagata Expressway Yamagata North IC
天童公園.tagline: Experience the enchanting cherry blossom carpet at Tendo Park, where tradition meets nature in a breathtaking display.
天童公園.desc:
Tendo Park, located at the summit of Maizuru Mountain, offers stunning views of the Zao mountain range. Every year from mid to late April, the park bursts into bloom with an impressive 10,000 cherry blossom trees, creating a spectacular and vibrant floral tapestry that is among the best in Yamagata Prefecture.

The highlight of the season is the limited-time "Ningen Shogi" event. On a giant chessboard in the expansive lawn, armored players act as chess pieces, set against a backdrop of vibrant red and gold flower walls. This dynamic scene beautifully embodies traditional folk aesthetics.

While you can drive directly to the mountaintop parking lot, strict traffic regulations are enforced during the event, so it's advisable to use the shuttle service. The mountaintop plaza is flat and spacious, equipped with accessible pathways. For the best experience, visit on a clear morning when the light enhances the beauty of both the cultural event and the breathtaking cherry blossom views.

三春瀧櫻.types: Weeping Cherry (one of Japan's top three cherry blossoms)
三春瀧櫻.count: 1 tree (a giant ancient tree over 1,000 years old)
三春瀧櫻.fee: 大人 300 円（僅觀覽期收費）
三春瀧櫻.address: 福島県田村郡三春町滝
三春瀧櫻.access_train: About 15 minutes by special bus "Takizakura-go" from JR Miharu Station
三春瀧櫻.access_car: About 15 minutes drive from the Ban'etsu Expressway Miharu-Shinmachi IC
三春瀧櫻.tagline: Experience Japan's majestic cherry blossom, a breathtaking spectacle of a thousand-year-old weeping sakura.
三春瀧櫻.desc:
Miharu Takizakura, located in Miharu Town, is a legendary Edo-higan weeping cherry tree over a thousand years old, renowned as one of Japan's top three cherry blossoms. Each year, from mid to late April, this towering giant, standing at 13.5 meters and spanning 25 meters wide, transforms into a stunning natural masterpiece that captivates all who visit.

The most striking visual experience comes from the "expansive pink waterfall cascading through the rice fields." The thick, ancient trunk rises majestically, while countless slender branches cascade down like a waterfall, adorned with lush, deep pink blossoms. This display showcases the ultimate beauty of nature and the passage of time.

To protect the delicate roots of this ancient tree, a well-maintained wooden walkway and fence have been constructed around the sakura, allowing visitors to enjoy the view from all angles. During cherry blossom season, enchanting night illuminations are held. It is highly recommended to arrive early at 6 AM when the park opens to capture the ethereal morning mist in the air.

花見山公園.types: Somei Yoshino cherry, Higan cherry, Weeping cherry (mixed colors)
花見山公園.fee: 免費（建議現場捐贈環保協力金）
花見山公園.address: 福島県福島市渡利字八島田11
花見山公園.access_train: About 15 minutes by temporary shuttle bus from JR Fukushima Station
花見山公園.access_car: Traffic is fully controlled during cherry blossom season; private cars must park at temporary lots and transfer to buses.
花見山公園.tagline: Discover your personal paradise in Fukushima, where the breathtaking Azuma Mountains meet a vibrant sea of blossoms.
花見山公園.desc:
Hanamiyama Park is a cherished sanctuary, lovingly cultivated by local sericulture families over generations, and open to the public for free. Every year, from mid-April, the park bursts into life with over a dozen varieties of cherry and peach blossoms, along with forsythia, painting the entire valley in a riot of colors.

The visual highlight is the stunning interplay of the remaining snow on the Azuma Mountains, the bright yellow forsythia, and the expansive carpet of cherry blossoms. As you stroll along the gently sloping natural pathways, you’ll find yourself enveloped by towering walls of flowers, with the majestic snow-capped mountains in the distance contrasting beautifully with the vibrant colors surrounding you, creating a rich tapestry of space and layers.

Since the entire park retains its natural hilly landscape, expect to navigate several slopes during your walk. It’s highly recommended to wear comfortable athletic shoes for cherry blossom viewing. During the blooming season, strict traffic controls are in place, so visiting early in the morning on weekdays is best to enjoy a more serene experience.

鶴之城.fee: 免費（天守閣大人 410 円）
鶴之城.address: 福島県会津若松市追手町1-1
鶴之城.access_train: Take the city loop bus from JR Aizuwakamatsu Station to "Tsuruno-jō Entrance."
鶴之城.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from the Aizuwakamatsu IC on the Ban'etsu Expressway.
鶴之城.tagline: Experience Japan's only red-tiled castle and its breathtaking cherry blossoms in a historic setting.
鶴之城.desc:
Tsuruno-jō (Wakamatsu Castle) is located in the historic city of Aizuwakamatsu, recognized as one of Japan's top 100 castles, famous for its unique red-tiled keep. Every year from mid to late April, the park bursts into bloom with around 1,000 cherry blossoms of the Somei Yoshino variety, adding a layer of pink to the grand ancient castle walls.  
  
The main feature here is the "unique red-tiled keep, sturdy stone walls, and classical cherry blossom scenery." Vibrant blooms line the gentle waterways, and as the calm waters of the old moat perfectly reflect the white walls and red-tiled keep surrounded by cherry blossoms, fallen petals create a legendary flower raft, showcasing the majestic beauty of this historic castle.  
  
The park's terrain is completely flat, and the ancient pathways have been well maintained, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. At night, a magical lighting event enhances the reflections, adding an ethereal quality to the scene. Visiting early on weekdays means fewer crowds, making it the perfect time for capturing stunning photos of this historic landmark.

常陸風土記之丘.count: Approximately 500 trees (home to the famous shidarezakura tunnel)
常陸風土記之丘.fee: 免費（收費區域大人 310 円）
常陸風土記之丘.access_train: About 15 minutes by bus from JR Ishioka Station
常陸風土記之丘.access_car: About 15 minutes from Chiyoda Ishioka IC on the Joban Expressway
常陸風土記之丘.tagline: Discover a historic green oasis in Ishioka, where Japan's largest lion head meets a vibrant cherry blossom corridor.
常陸風土記之丘.desc:
常陸風土記之丘 is a colossal historical theme park that beautifully blends ancient reconstructed buildings with top-notch horticulture. Each year, from mid to late April, the park's famous shidarezakura (weeping cherry blossoms) corridor reaches its peak, with around 500 pink cherry trees creating a stunning tunnel that transforms the entire park into a vibrant spectacle.

The most breathtaking experience is undoubtedly the view from the largest lion head observation deck in Japan, framed by the pink shidarezakura. The towering, dark lion head structure contrasts dramatically with the cascading wall of bright pink blossoms below. As the petals fall, the ground becomes a carpet of pink, creating a striking visual impact.

The park features wide open spaces and well-maintained paths, making it very friendly for strollers and those with mobility challenges. The cherry blossom area is shaded by numerous trees, providing a cool and comfortable walking experience. During peak weekends, the park attracts many families, so it's highly recommended to visit on a weekday morning for a chance to capture serene, crowd-free scenes.

靜峰故鄉公園.fee: 免費（櫻花祭期間可能收費）
靜峰故鄉公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR "Shizuka Station"
靜峰故鄉公園.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from "Tōhoku Expressway" Naka IC
靜峰故鄉公園.tagline: Step into the realm of Yaezakura and be dazzled by a vibrant carpet of 2,000 double-flowered cherry blossoms.
靜峰故鄉公園.desc:
靜峰故鄉公園 is a beautifully designed ecological park that takes advantage of the natural hilly landscape and is listed among Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Known for its late-blooming "Yaezakura (double-flowered cherry)" trees, the blossoms here reach their peak from mid to late April, painting the hillside with a kaleidoscope of colors from around 2,000 trees.

The park's standout feature is its "dense, colorful flower wall unique to double-flowered cherry blossoms." Stroll along the well-maintained winding paths, where the lush pink and deep purple flower clusters appear particularly thick due to their numerous petals. From the summit viewpoint, you can take in the breathtaking expanse of the colorful floral carpet below.

The park offers free access for leisurely walks (some facilities may charge), featuring a large grassy area and child-friendly play equipment, making it perfect for family outings. The elevated terrain has little shade, so be sure to apply sunscreen when visiting for flower viewing and photography. Mornings on weekdays are the least crowded, providing the best lighting for stunning photos.

天平之丘公園.access_train: About a 5-minute taxi ride from JR Koganei Station
天平之丘公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from the Mibu IC on the Kita Kanto Expressway
天平之丘公園.tagline: Step into the ancient grounds of Shimotsuke Kokubunji and witness 500 majestic late-blooming 'Usuzumi Zakura' trees.
天平之丘公園.desc:
Tennpyo no Oka Park is built on the historic site of the Nara period's famous temple, Shimotsuke Kokubunji, making it one of Kanto's most iconic cherry blossom viewing spots. Here, the rare 'Usuzumi Zakura', one of Japan's three great cherry blossoms, blooms in mid-April, showcasing a stunning sea of 500 trees in full glory.

The park's core feature is the intricate blend of historical earthen fortifications, wooden longhouses, and a floral tapestry that feels like a natural extension of the landscape. Delicate pink and pure white flower walls stretch along the ancient stone paths and moss-covered steps, while the tranquil pond reflects historical monuments and colorful foliage, creating a spacious and serene atmosphere.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained paths that are very friendly for seniors and strollers. There’s a traditional farmhouse café and a large lawn area, making it a perfect spot for a comfortable early spring picnic while enjoying the cherry blossoms. For the best experience, visit early in the morning on weekdays when the crowds are minimal, ideal for capturing beautiful cultural scenes.

太平山.access_train: About 15 minutes by bus from JR Tochigi Station
太平山.access_car: About 15 minutes from Tochigi IC on the Tohoku Expressway
太平山.tagline: Experience the breathtaking 'Sakura Tunnel' on the scenic roads of Taiheizan, where 2,000 cherry blossoms bloom in vibrant color.
太平山.desc:
Taiheizan is renowned for its breathtaking views from the Kenshin-daira observation deck, named after the legendary warrior Uesugi Kenshin who is said to have gazed upon this landscape. From early to mid-April, the scenic road leading to the summit bursts into life with over 2,000 Somei Yoshino cherry trees, transforming into a stunning two-kilometer-long pink tunnel.

The visual allure lies in the 'undulating, colorful flower belt that winds along the mountainside, framed by the surrounding peaks.' Whether you roll down your windows for a leisurely drive or stop by the roadside, you'll be surrounded by vibrant, lush pink flower walls. Standing at the Kenshin-daira observation deck, you can take in the grand panorama of the Kanto Plain alongside the colorful blooms at your feet, creating a truly majestic scene.

As this spot is essentially an open roadway, strict traffic regulations, including one-way access, are enforced during cherry blossom season, so be sure to check for the latest announcements before you go. The main cherry blossom viewing paths are gently sloped, so wearing comfortable sneakers is recommended. Arriving at dusk offers a magical view of the plains and flower sea bathed in the warm glow of the setting sun.

赤城南面千本櫻.address: 群馬県前橋市富士見町赤城山
赤城南面千本櫻.access_train: About 50 minutes by temporary shuttle bus from Maebashi Station
赤城南面千本櫻.access_car: About 30 minutes by car from the Isesaki IC on the Kita Kanto Expressway
赤城南面千本櫻.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of cherry blossoms and canola flowers in a stunning two-kilometer tunnel.
赤城南面千本櫻.desc:
Akagi Nanmen Senbonzakura is located on the gentle slopes of Akagi Mountain's southern side, recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Every year from early to mid-April, the straight two-kilometer road bursts into color with a thousand ancient Somei Yoshino cherry trees lining both sides, painting the vast highlands with vibrant early spring hues.

The most striking feature is undoubtedly the "pink cherry blossom tunnel framed by a blanket of yellow canola flowers." Strolling along the central pathway, the lush, colorful flower walls rise almost to eye level, harmonizing beautifully with the 150,000 blooming canola flowers at your feet, showcasing a refined ecological beauty.

The scenic road is well-maintained and gently undulating, making it perfect for leisurely drives or family outings. The highland area can be breezy and slightly cool in the mornings and evenings, so be sure to bring a warm jacket for your photo sessions. For the best visibility, visit on a clear morning when the colors of the cherry blossoms and canola flowers are at their most vibrant.

櫻山公園.count: about 7,000 winter cherry trees, 3,000 Somei Yoshino cherry trees
櫻山公園.access_train: Take a taxi or temporary bus from JR Gunma-Tomioka Station
櫻山公園.access_car: Approximately 40 minutes from the Fujioka IC on the Joshinetsu Expressway
櫻山公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of winter cherry blossoms and vibrant autumn foliage at Sakurayama Park.
櫻山公園.desc:
Sakurayama Park is a nationally designated scenic spot located at the summit of Sakurayama, renowned for its unique dual-season cherry blossoms that bloom in both spring and autumn. In early April, you can marvel at 3,000 cherry trees of the Somei Yoshino variety, while an impressive 7,000 winter cherry trees bloom in late autumn, creating a rare two-season attraction in Japan.

The most breathtaking view is the stunning juxtaposition of winter cherry blossoms against the backdrop of fiery autumn leaves. From the mountaintop observation deck, you can capture the elegant white winter cherry blossom wall swaying in the breeze, alongside the deep red sea of maple leaves cascading down the hillside, all framed by distant mountain ranges—a scene that is both grand and harmonious.

As the entire park is situated in a natural hilly forest terrain, reaching the observation deck requires climbing several slopes. Be sure to wear good-quality, slip-resistant sports shoes. The highland terrain offers little shelter, so prepare for windy conditions. Visiting in the early morning allows you to photograph the ethereal morning mist that envelops the northern forest.

大宮公園.address: 埼玉県さいたま市大宮区高鼻町4
大宮公園.access_train: A 5-minute walk from Omiya Koen Station on the Tobu Noda Line
大宮公園.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from the Shuto Expressway Saitama Shintoshin Line, Nintoshin Nishi IC
大宮公園.tagline: Step into the century-old beauty of Omiya Park, where a sea of cherry blossoms and red pines create a breathtaking pink paradise.
大宮公園.desc:
Omiya Park is a vast natural park located next to the ancient Ikegawa Shrine, recognized as one of Japan's top urban parks and a cherry blossom hotspot. Each year in early April, around 1,000 cherry trees of the Somei Yoshino variety burst into bloom, enveloping the lush, historic grounds in a vibrant tapestry of colors.

The park's core feature is the harmonious dialogue between the century-old red pine forest, traditional Japanese gardens, and the stunning cherry blossom views. Colorful blooms line the gentle gravel paths, contrasting beautifully with the towering pines and the weighty, historic buildings. During the cherry blossom fall, petals drift onto the surface of the 'Funayuu Pool,' creating a spacious and serene atmosphere.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained ancient pathways, making it very accessible for elderly visitors and strollers. There are also playgrounds and a small zoo, perfect for families. Set aside an hour to stroll, and visit in the early morning right after opening for the least crowds, ideal for capturing the park's timeless beauty.

幸手權現堂櫻堤.address: 埼玉県幸手市内国府間887-3
幸手權現堂櫻堤.access_train: About 15 minutes by bus from Tobu Nikko Line "Kasukabe Station"
幸手權現堂櫻堤.access_car: About 10 minutes by car from "Ken-O Expressway" Kasukabe IC
幸手權現堂櫻堤.desc:
Kasukabe Gongen-do Sakura Embankment is a stunning one-kilometer landscape corridor that stretches along the old Gongen-do River embankment, recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Every year in early April, around a thousand ancient Somei Yoshino cherry trees burst into bloom, creating a breathtaking spectacle that harmonizes with endless fields of vibrant yellow rapeseed flowers.

The most breathtaking view is the "pink cherry blossom tunnel framed by a blanket of golden rapeseed flowers." As you stroll along the gentle ancient embankment path, you’ll find yourself under a dense canopy of cherry blossoms, with a vast, shimmering sea of rapeseed flowers on your right, creating a striking visual impact.

The riverside walkway is completely flat, making it very friendly for strollers and joggers, and it is open to the public free of charge all day. During the cherry blossom festival, lively nighttime illuminations and food stalls are set up. For those looking to avoid the crowds and capture the cleanest, most refined contrasts in their photos, local photographers strongly recommend arriving early on weekdays before dawn.
幸手權現堂櫻堤.tagline: Stroll along the historic Gongen-do Sakura Embankment, where a breathtaking one-kilometer tunnel of cherry blossoms meets a golden sea of flowers.

秩父羊山公園.fee: 免費（芝櫻祭期間大人 300 円）
秩父羊山公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from Seibu Chichibu Station on the Seibu Chichibu Line
秩父羊山公園.access_car: Approximately a 50-minute drive from Hanazono IC on the Kan-Etsu Expressway
秩父羊山公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of Shibazakura Hill, where 170,000 vibrant flowers blanket the landscape against majestic mountains.
秩父羊山公園.desc:
Yohiyama Park is perched on a hilly highland on the eastern side of Chichibu city, renowned for its world-famous "Shibazakura Hill." Due to its unique varieties and blooming period, the peak season here follows the Yoshino cherry blossoms, bursting into color from mid-April to early May. The park has meticulously cultivated up to 170,000 Shibazakura across its expansive slopes.

The most stunning sight online is the "majestic Bukōzan, the pink Shibazakura carpet, and the cosmos in a perfect frame." Stroll along the well-maintained zigzag paths, flanked by vibrant pink, sky blue, and pure white flower clusters, resembling a massive geometric carpet laid across the earth, creating a powerful visual impact.

As the main Shibazakura area is concentrated on undulating natural hills, it's highly recommended to wear non-slip casual sneakers when visiting. During the flower season, strict traffic regulations for self-driving will be enforced, and the park features a rich local produce market. Visiting on weekday mornings offers the brightest lighting, perfect for capturing the scenic beauty.

清水公園.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from 清水公園 Station on the Tobu Noda Line
清水公園.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from the Nagareyama IC on the Joban Expressway
清水公園.tagline: Step into Japan's cherry blossom paradise, where ancient temples meet a tunnel of 2,000 blooming sakura.
清水公園.desc:
清水公園 is Japan's first privately-owned park, blending rich natural ecology, cultural heritage, and large outdoor adventure facilities. It's proudly listed among Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Every year in early April, around the historic temple of 金乗院 on the park's north side, approximately 2,000 Yoshino cherry trees burst into bloom.

The visual highlight features the classic scene of the sturdy wooden sanmon gate, the Nio gate, and a sea of flowers. Colorful blossoms stretch along both sides of the long gravel path, beautifully complemented by ancient stone lanterns. This interplay of movement and stillness perfectly embodies the tranquil historical Zen essence and the passage of time unique to the temple.

The park offers free admission (some facilities may charge), with flat, well-paved grounds that are very stroller and elder-friendly. There’s a large grassy plaza within the park, making it an ideal spot for a comfortable cherry blossom picnic in early spring. Visiting on weekday mornings ensures the least crowds, perfect for capturing stunning cultural landscapes and pristine views.

本土寺.count: about 100 trees (famous for hydrangeas and autumn leaves, but the weeping cherry is also beautiful in spring)
本土寺.access_train: 10-minute walk from JR Joban Line 'Kita-Kogane Station'
本土寺.tagline: Visit the 'Temple of Flowers' in Matsudo City, where five-story pagodas, moss, and a thousand Yoshino cherry trees await.
本土寺.desc:
Hondoji is a renowned ancient temple with over 700 years of history, belonging to the Nichiren sect. It is affectionately called the 'Temple of Flowers' due to the abundance of blooms throughout its grounds. From late March to early April, the expansive area bursts into color as thousands of Somei Yoshino and weeping cherry trees bloom in unison, adorning this sacred space.

The most iconic view features the striking vermilion five-story pagoda, the vintage wooden sanmon gate, and the perfect harmony of blooming flowers. Vibrant pink and pure white blossoms cascade along the ancient stone-paved approach, beautifully contrasting with the moss and historic stone monuments, creating a serene scene that embodies the unique tranquility of the temple.

The temple grounds are flat and compact, making it easy to stroll around. A visit typically takes about 40 minutes. During the flower season, the temple collects a small offering fee and offers limited edition omamori. Early mornings on weekdays provide the most ethereal atmosphere, allowing you to enjoy a pristine environment with excellent lighting and reflections for photography.

新宿御苑.types: Somei Yoshino, Yaezakura, Shidarezakura, and about 70 other varieties
新宿御苑.access_train: 5-minute walk from Shinjuku Gyoenmae Station or Shinjuku Sanchome Station
新宿御苑.access_car: Alcoholic beverages are prohibited; public transportation is recommended.
新宿御苑.tagline: Discover a vibrant oasis of cherry blossoms in the heart of Tokyo.
新宿御苑.desc:
Shinjuku Gyoen is a sprawling royal garden that beautifully blends traditional Japanese aesthetics, expansive English lawns, and refined French geometric design. With around 1,000 cherry trees of 70 different varieties blooming in succession, the flowering season lasts for two months, painting the bustling urban landscape in vivid colors.

The visual highlight is the dialogue between the modern Shinjuku skyline, the classic NTT building, and the cherry blossoms. Colorful blooms line the gentle gravel paths, and when the tranquil pond of the Japanese garden perfectly reflects the wooden longhouses, historical monuments, and vibrant foliage, it showcases an elegant Japanese aesthetic.

The park's terrain is completely flat, and the ancient pathways are well-maintained, making it very wheelchair and stroller-friendly. During the cherry blossom season, entry to the park is by online reservation only. The area is highly accessible, ideal for independent travelers. Arrive early on weekdays to capture stunning cultural landscapes.

千鳥之淵.address: 東京都千代田区三番町先
千鳥之淵.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from Kudan-shita Station or Hanzomon Station on the subway
千鳥之淵.access_car: Located in the city center, taking the subway is highly recommended.
千鳥之淵.tagline: Experience a dazzling night of lights and a breathtaking pink flower carpet at Chidorigafuchi.
千鳥之淵.desc:
Chidorigafuchi is a globally renowned hotspot located next to the Imperial Palace's Kitanomaru Park, famous for its top-tier night illuminations and riverside cherry blossom views. From late March to early April, the cherry blossoms reach their peak, with towering ancient trees bursting into bloom along the moat, transforming this sacred area into a vibrant spectacle.

The most iconic scene features the "ultimate night LED lighting, water corridor, and cherry blossoms in a mirrored reflection." Standing on the specially set central observation platform, you can witness the stunning pink flower forest illuminated by colorful lights, perfectly contrasting with the calm water surface. As a gentle breeze ripples across the water, the scene becomes truly magical.

The pathways are well-maintained and gently sloping, making it very friendly for seniors and leisurely travelers. During the night illumination period, lines can be quite long, so it's recommended to visit early on weekdays as soon as the gates open to capture the pristine beauty and perfect reflections. Please remember to maintain silence and follow etiquette while taking photos.

目黑川.hours: 24 小時開放（夜間有點燈活動）
目黑川.address: 東京都目黒区青葉台1丁目
目黑川.access_train: Just a short walk from Nakameguro Station on the Tokyu Toyoko Line/Subway
目黑川.access_car: Parking is extremely difficult due to crowds; taking the train is recommended.
目黑川.desc:
Meguro River is a famous urban river flowing through Setagaya, Meguro, and Shinagawa, and it stands as one of Japan's top romantic cherry blossom viewing spots. From late March to early April, the 4-kilometer-long gentle pathways on both sides burst into bloom with 800 towering Somei Yoshino cherry trees, transforming into a pink tunnel.

The most stunning sight is the "dual romance of chic residences, a grand red bridge, and pink flower rafts." As you leisurely stroll along the smooth riverside path, the lush branches intertwine above you. After the peak bloom, the falling petals blanket the water's surface, turning the river into a pink conveyor belt, creating a striking visual impact.

Located in a bustling urban area, the site offers excellent transportation and accessibility, making it very stroller and wheelchair friendly. During the cherry blossom festival, the famous pink lanterns illuminate the night, drawing large crowds. For the cleanest and most refined views of the cherry blossom rafts without tourists, it’s highly recommended to arrive early on weekdays before dawn.
目黑川.tagline: Stroll through the heart of Meguro's trendy district, surrounded by a breathtaking carpet of pink cherry blossom petals.

三溪園.address: 神奈川県横浜市中区本牧三之谷58-1
三溪園.access_train: Take a bus from JR "Yokohama Station" for about 35 minutes to "Sankeien Entrance"
三溪園.access_car: About a 5-minute drive from "Shuto Expressway" Honmoku IC
三溪園.tagline: Step into Yokohama's designated national treasure garden and capture the beauty of the ancient three-tiered pagoda and 300 Yoshino cherries.
三溪園.desc:
Created by entrepreneur Hara Sankei, this expansive garden is a source of pride for Yokohama as a nationally designated scenic spot. From late March to early April, the historical buildings and old folk houses, relocated from Kyoto and Kamakura, are surrounded by up to 300 Somei Yoshino and Yamazakura cherry trees, showcasing a deep Eastern aesthetic.

The most iconic view here is the "Old Tomyoji Three-Tiered Pagoda framed by blooming flowers." Vivid clusters of blossoms embrace ancient moss-covered structures and the tranquil surface of a large pond. When the lights illuminate the garden at night, it transforms into a breathtaking ink wash painting, rich in atmosphere.

The garden is vast and filled with historical relics, so it's recommended to allocate at least 1.5 hours for a leisurely visit. The main waterside paths are flat, making them very accessible for strollers and seniors. It's highly recommended to visit on a weekday morning to fully enjoy the unique tranquility and excellent lighting for photography.

三浦海岸.types: Kawazu cherry blossoms (early-blooming, mid-February to early March)
三浦海岸.address: 神奈川県三浦市南下浦町菊名
三浦海岸.access_train: Arrive at Keikyu Kurihama Line's Miura Kaigan Station
三浦海岸.access_car: Approximately 20 minutes from Sawara IC on the Yokohama-Yokosuka Road
三浦海岸.tagline: Discover the breathtaking beauty of Miura Coast's early cherry blossoms and vibrant landscapes.
三浦海岸.desc:
Miura Kaigan, located along the southern coast of Kanagawa Prefecture, is a premier destination for early cherry blossoms in the Kanto region. Thanks to its unique warm climate, the blooming period here is one of the earliest in all of Japan, from mid-February to early March, with blossoms bursting along the road from Keikyu Miura Kaigan Station to Komatsui Park.

The most breathtaking view features the iconic "Keikyu red train, a blanket of yellow rapeseed flowers, and the pink Kawazu cherry blossoms" all in one frame. Stroll along the gentle pathways, where vibrant, deep pink cherry blossom walls are within reach, and as the vintage Keikyu train passes by, the scene combines both dynamic movement and nostalgic charm.

The road is completely flat and well-maintained, making it perfect for a leisurely morning walk without any burdens. During the cherry blossom festival, the area around the station will be illuminated with enchanting lights, and limited-edition local products will be available for purchase. Aim to visit on a clear morning for the best visibility, when the colors of the cherry blossoms and rapeseed flowers are at their most vibrant.

縣立三池公園.types: about 78 varieties including Somei Yoshino, Kanzakura, and Yaezakura
縣立三池公園.address: 神奈川県横浜市鶴見区三ツ池公園1-1
縣立三池公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Tsurumi Station for about 15 minutes to Miike Park North Gate.
縣立三池公園.access_car: About a 10-minute drive from Kanagawa Route 7 Yokohama North Line Kogane Ikuta IC.
縣立三池公園.tagline: Discover a breathtaking oasis of cherry blossoms in Yokohama's southern suburbs, featuring three stunning ponds and 78 varieties.
縣立三池公園.desc:
Kenritsu Miike Park is an ecological leisure oasis cleverly nestled in a natural valley, named for its three large water retention ponds, and is recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From mid-March to mid-April each year, around 16,000 cherry trees of 78 different varieties burst into vibrant bloom, adorning the hillside with a kaleidoscope of colors.

The most stunning spectacle online is the layered beauty of the 'Miike waterfront, classical wooden bridges, and a three-dimensional pink flower carpet.' From the delicate single-petal Yoshino cherry to the lush double-petal Yaezakura and Yokohama Hizakura, the blooming periods overlap beautifully. When the calm water perfectly reflects the blossoms, the aesthetic lines are simply breathtaking.

The park offers free and open walking paths, with flat and well-planned terrain that is very friendly for strollers and elderly visitors. There is a large grassy plaza, perfect for families planning a full-day picnic and outdoor fun. Visiting early on weekdays ensures fewer crowds, making it ideal for capturing that perfect mirror-like reflection.

高田城址公園.count: approximately 4,000 trees (one of Japan's Three Great Night Sakura)
高田城址公園.hours: 24 小時開放（夜間點燈至 21:00 或 22:00）
高田城址公園.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from Echigo Tokimeki Railway "Takada Station"
高田城址公園.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from the Hokuriku Expressway Joetsu IC
高田城址公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking cherry blossom spectacle at Takada Castle Ruins Park, a true springtime wonder!
高田城址公園.desc:
Takada Castle Ruins Park is built on the site of the former Takada Castle, established during the Edo period. It is recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots and is also celebrated as one of the "Three Great Night Sakura" alongside Ueno Park and Hirosaki Park. Every year from early April to mid-April, around 4,000 cherry trees of the Somei Yoshino variety burst into bloom, adding vibrant colors to the majestic remnants of the old castle.

The park's core feature is its classic scenery, showcasing the retro castle moat, sturdy earthworks, and the three-story turret. The colorful blossoms bloom alongside the gentle waterway, and when the calm water perfectly reflects the turret and the full canopy of cherry blossoms, the scene transforms at night with 3,000 snow lanterns illuminating the area, creating a spacious and harmonious atmosphere.

The park's terrain is completely flat, and the ancient pathways are well-maintained, making it very friendly for seniors and those with strollers. The surrounding area is also highly functional, perfect for a leisurely drive. Set aside an hour to stroll, as the lake is calmest in the early morning, making it the ideal time to capture ethereal, Zen-like reflections.

大河津分水.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Echigo Line "Bunsui Station"
大河津分水.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from "Nakanoshima Mitsuke IC" on the Hokuriku Expressway
大河津分水.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of cherry blossoms along the historic banks of the Shinano River.
大河津分水.desc:
Ōkawazu Bunsui is a remarkable flood control project created to manage the waters of Japan's longest river, the Shinano. It is also recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From early to mid-April, thousands of ancient Somei Yoshino cherry trees burst into bloom along the long embankment road, creating a spectacular sight.

The most breathtaking view features the grand modern dam, the wide river, and a stunning display of cherry blossoms all in one frame. Stroll along the smooth riverbank path, where the lush, colorful flower walls rise almost to eye level, beautifully contrasting with the tranquil water surface, showcasing the harmony between nature and modern architecture.

The well-maintained roads and completely flat terrain make it perfect for a leisurely riverside hike or a scenic drive. During the cherry blossom festival, the famous traditional event “Oiran Dochu” takes place, creating a vibrant atmosphere. Visiting on a clear morning is ideal for capturing the expansive, colorful carpet of blossoms.

松川公園.hours: 24 小時開放（賞櫻遊覽船約 09:30～17:00）
松川公園.fee: 免費（遊覽船大人 1,800 円）
松川公園.address: 富山県富山市本丸至七軒町（松川沿岸）
松川公園.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Toyama Station
松川公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Toyama IC on the Hokuriku Expressway
松川公園.tagline: Experience the enchanting cherry blossom tunnel along the Matsukawa River in Toyama's urban oasis.
松川公園.desc:
