Matsukawa Park is a winding waterfront oasis in the heart of the city, nestled along the old Jintsu River (now Matsukawa) and recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Each year, in early April, around 700 Yoshino cherry trees burst into bloom along the riverbanks, their branches extending towards the water, transforming the area into a stunning pink blossom tunnel.

The visual highlight is the charming interplay of the retro Matsukawa sightseeing boats, flowing water, and the mirrored pink flower walls. As you stroll along the gentle riverside path, you can watch traditional wooden boats gliding through the carpet of falling cherry blossoms, creating a harmonious blend of tranquility and the refined urban landscape of Hokuriku.

The riverside promenade is completely flat and easily accessible, making it perfect for travelers seeking a leisurely morning walk. For those looking to capture the perfect reflection of the cherry blossoms and boats without ripples, experienced photographers highly recommend visiting on weekday mornings when the water is at its calmest.

朝日町舟川（春之四重奏）.types: Somei Yoshino cherry, tulips, rapeseed flowers
朝日町舟川（春之四重奏）.address: 富山県下新川郡朝日町舟川新
朝日町舟川（春之四重奏）.access_train: About 10 minutes by temporary shuttle bus from Tomari Station
朝日町舟川（春之四重奏）.access_car: About 5 minutes from Hokuriku Expressway Asahi IC
朝日町舟川（春之四重奏）.tagline: Experience a breathtaking symphony of cherry blossoms, rapeseed flowers, and tulips beneath the Northern Alps.
朝日町舟川（春之四重奏）.desc:
Asahi-machi Funagawa is a renowned destination in Japan, where nature and top-tier agricultural landscapes blend perfectly. From early to mid-April, 280 cherry trees of the Somei Yoshino variety bloom along the banks of Funagawa, harmonizing with a vibrant array of tulips and yellow rapeseed flowers planted by local farmers to match the flowering season.

The most stunning visual experience comes from the "fourfold interpretation of snow-capped peaks, pink cherry blossoms, yellow rapeseed flowers, and red and yellow tulips." Standing amidst the gently sloping fields, you’ll gaze at the majestic snow-covered Hakuba and Asahi mountains in the background, while your feet rest upon a vivid, multi-colored carpet of flowers, creating an unparalleled visual impact.

This area features a rustic agricultural landscape, with natural dirt paths for walking. It is highly recommended to wear comfortable, dirt-resistant running shoes when visiting for cherry blossom viewing. The best time to capture the snow-capped peaks at their most visible is on clear, windless mornings, when the vibrant colors of the earth are at their most vivid and rich.

富山縣水墨美術館.count: several massive weeping cherry trees in the garden
富山縣水墨美術館.fee: 免費（庭園部分；進入展覽廳需收費）
富山縣水墨美術館.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from Toyama Chihou Tetsudo City Tram "Toyama Totteran Mae"
富山縣水墨美術館.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Toyama IC on the Hokuriku Expressway
富山縣水墨美術館.tagline: Step into a minimalist Japanese garden and capture the beauty of two century-old weeping cherry trees against a pure white backdrop.
富山縣水墨美術館.desc:
Toyama Ken Suiboku Bijutsukan is a cultural haven that showcases the elegance of Eastern ink wash aesthetics. In early April, the "Edo Higan Weeping Cherry" trees in the museum's expansive courtyard reach their peak bloom, transforming into a breathtaking sea of vibrant pink from two trees that are decades old.

The most iconic composition features a dialogue between the minimalist white walls, lush green lawns, and graceful weeping cherry trees. Delicate pink blossoms cascade from the sky like a waterfall, perfectly contrasting with the museum's solid, pure white Japanese-style longhouse architecture, embodying the pinnacle of Japanese refined gardening aesthetics and serene Zen spirit.

The park's terrain is completely flat, and the walking paths are well-maintained, making it very friendly for older visitors and those who prefer a leisurely pace. After enjoying the cherry blossoms in the garden, be sure to step inside to appreciate the ink wash art exhibitions. Mornings on weekdays are the least crowded, allowing you to capture the rich textures of the old building and create stunning, story-filled photographs.

兼六園.types: about 40 varieties including Somei Yoshino, Higan, and Kōkaen chrysanthemum cherries
兼六園.hours: 07:00～18:00（賞櫻高峰期夜間點燈免費開放至 21:30）
兼六園.fee: 大人 320 円（夜間點燈期間免費）
兼六園.access_train: About 15 minutes by Kōkaen shuttle bus from JR Kanazawa Station
兼六園.access_car: About 20 minutes from Kanazawa Morimoto IC via the Hokuriku Expressway
兼六園.desc:
兼六園, alongside Mito Kairakuen and Okayama Korakuen, ranks as one of Japan's three great gardens, perfectly embodying the Edo period's "strolling" landscape style. It's also recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From early to mid-April, the garden bursts into color with around 400 ancient cherry trees and chrysanthemum cherries, creating a vibrant spectacle.

The most iconic view features the landmark Hizaki Stone Lantern, the Rainbow Bridge, and the cherry blossoms reflecting in the tranquil waters. Colorful blooms line the gentle gravel paths, and when the calm surface of Kasa no Ike mirrors the historical treasures, ancient wooden Tsurugi pines, and full cherry blossom canopies, the space feels expansive and exudes an elegant Zen atmosphere.

The garden's flat terrain and well-maintained pathways make it very accessible for seniors and strollers. During cherry blossom season, the garden offers special "limited-time free entry" and evening illumination events, which are extremely popular. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, making it the best time to capture the serene essence of this classical garden.
兼六園.tagline: Explore the stunning beauty of Kōka's iconic garden, where cherry blossoms and serene landscapes await.

能登鹿島站.count: about 100 trees (forming a cherry blossom tunnel on both sides of the platform)
能登鹿島站.address: 石川県鳳珠郡穴水町曽福
能登鹿島站.access_train: Arrive at Noto Kashima Station on the Noto Railway
能登鹿島站.access_car: Approximately 5 minutes drive from the Noto Chuo Expressway's Koshinohara IC
能登鹿島站.tagline: Experience the enchanting cherry blossom tunnel at Noto Kashima Station!
能登鹿島站.desc:
Noto Kashima Station, located along the Noto Peninsula's Nanao Bay, is famously known as the "Noto Cherry Blossom Station" due to its platform flanked by dense cherry blossoms. Each year, from mid-April, hundreds of Yoshino cherry trees bloom spectacularly, transforming the area into a breathtaking pink tunnel.

The ultimate visual delight is the "romantic encounter of the vintage local train and the pink floral wall." Standing on the safe observation platform, as the brightly painted Noto Railway train slowly enters the cherry blossom platform, the train is enveloped by the vibrant floral wall, creating a dynamic and nostalgic scene perfect for photos.

This spot is an operational public railway station, so please be mindful of platform safety and avoid crossing the yellow line while exploring and capturing angles. As a hidden gem for train enthusiasts, train frequencies are limited, so be sure to check the timetable before your visit. Early morning visits offer the best chance to capture ethereal landscapes.

足羽川櫻並木.count: approximately 600 trees (one of the largest cherry blossom tunnels in Japan, stretching 2.2 kilometers)
足羽川櫻並木.address: 福井県福井市足羽川沿い
足羽川櫻並木.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from JR Fukui Station
足羽川櫻並木.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from Fukui IC on the Hokuriku Expressway
足羽川櫻並木.tagline: Experience a breathtaking 2.2-kilometer cherry blossom tunnel in the heart of Fukui City.
足羽川櫻並木.desc:
The Ashu River Cherry Blossom Avenue stretches along both banks of the Ashu River in central Fukui, boasting one of Japan's premier wooden cherry blossom viewing corridors, recognized as one of Japan's top 100 cherry blossom spots. Each year, in early April, the banks come alive with the stunning bloom of around 600 ancient Somei Yoshino cherry trees, creating a spectacular sight.

The most captivating feature is the "endless, dual-row cherry trees forming a massive pink tunnel." Stroll along the smooth riverbank path, where the lush branches intertwine above, making you feel as if you're walking through an infinite pink corridor, with an expansive sense of space and striking visual impact.

The riverside walkway is completely flat and well-maintained, making it very stroller and jogger-friendly, and it is open to the public free of charge all day. During the cherry blossom festival, a famous nighttime illumination show takes place. For those looking to capture the pristine beauty of the cherry blossom tunnel without tourists, experienced photographers strongly recommend visiting on weekday mornings before dawn.

丸岡城.fee: 免費（進入天守閣大人 450 円）
丸岡城.address: 福井県坂井市丸岡町霞町1-59
丸岡城.access_train: Take a bus from JR Fukui Station for about 40 minutes to Maruoka Castle Station.
丸岡城.access_car: About a 5-minute drive from Maruoka IC on the Hokuriku Expressway.
丸岡城.tagline: Experience Japan's oldest surviving castle with a breathtaking sea of 400 cherry blossoms.
丸岡城.desc:
Maruoka Castle, built during the Tensho era, is one of Japan's top 100 castles and boasts the only remaining wooden castle tower in Hokuriku, renowned nationwide. Every year from early to mid-April, around 400 Somei Yoshino cherry trees burst into bloom, enveloping the majestic ancient castle walls and surrounding hills.

The core feature here is the "national treasure-level grand keep, sturdy stone walls, and classical cherry blossom scenery." Colorful blooms flourish along the gentle waterways, while the rustic white-walled castle tower is surrounded by a vibrant carpet of flowers in various shades, perfectly embodying the unique historical charm of this ancient site.

The castle grounds are situated on natural hills, and reaching the keep requires climbing a steep set of ancient stone steps. It is highly recommended to wear non-slip athletic shoes when visiting for cherry blossom viewing. During the cherry blossom festival, a magical illumination event takes place at night, capturing the old architecture and colorful foliage, while early morning visits on regular days offer a serene experience.

新倉山淺間公園.count: about 650 trees (classic composition of Mount Fuji, the five-story pagoda, and cherry blossoms)
新倉山淺間公園.address: 山梨県富士吉田市新倉3353-1
新倉山淺間公園.access_train: 20-minute walk from Fujikyu Railway's Shimoyoshida Station
新倉山淺間公園.access_car: Traffic restrictions apply during cherry blossom season; shuttle buses are required.
新倉山淺間公園.tagline: Experience breathtaking views of Mount Fuji, a five-story pagoda, and a sea of cherry blossoms in one iconic frame.
新倉山淺間公園.desc:
Shinakurayama Asama Park, nestled on the slopes of Shinakurayama, is a beautifully preserved historical shrine and a world-renowned cherry blossom viewing spot. From early to mid-April, the approach to the summit's Chūrei Tower is lined with around 600 stunning Somei Yoshino cherry trees, transforming the mountainside into a vibrant tapestry of color.

The most breathtaking view online is undoubtedly the legendary "iconic frame of Mount Fuji, the vermilion five-story pagoda, and a full bloom of cherry blossoms." Standing at the famous observation deck at the summit, you'll be greeted by the majestic, snow-capped peak of Mount Fuji in the background, the traditional wooden Chūrei Tower in the midground, and a cascading wall of pink blossoms at your feet, creating a powerful visual impact.

To reach the renowned Chūrei Tower observation deck, you'll need to climb the historic 398-step "Sakuya Hime Stairs," which requires a bit of stamina. It's highly recommended to wear proper athletic shoes. During the cherry blossom season, strict visitor limits are enforced. For the best chance to capture the ethereal beauty, visit early on weekdays.

山高神代櫻.types: Edo-higan cherry (one of Japan's Three Great Cherry Blossoms)
山高神代櫻.count: 1 tree (approximately 2,000 years old, the oldest cherry blossom in Japan)
山高神代櫻.address: 山梨県北杜市武川町山高2763
山高神代櫻.access_train: About a 15-minute taxi ride from JR Hinoharu Station
山高神代櫻.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from the Suwa IC on the Chuo Expressway
山高神代櫻.tagline: Witness the ancient majesty of Japan's oldest cherry blossom, surrounded by a sea of vibrant canola flowers.
山高神代櫻.desc:
Yamakakajindai Zakura is located within the Shissouji temple grounds. This legendary Edo-higan cherry tree is believed to be between 1,800 and 2,000 years old, making it one of Japan's oldest "Three Great Cherry Blossoms." Each year, it reaches its peak bloom in early April, transforming into a breathtaking spectacle with its massive trunk measuring 11.8 meters in circumference.

The most stunning view features the "snow-capped Kaikomagatake in the background, a golden blanket of canola flowers, and the dialogue of time with the ancient tree." The rustic stone lanterns and historic old temple create a majestic setting, where the towering tree stands tall amidst the lush pink blossoms and the vibrant yellow canola fields at its feet, creating a dramatic contrast.

To protect the delicate roots of this 2,000-year-old giant, a well-maintained gravel path and protective fencing have been established around the tree, making it very friendly for elderly visitors. The scenic area is well-preserved, offering a cool and comfortable stroll. It is highly recommended to visit in the early morning when the sunlight captures the timeless beauty of this cultural landmark.

身延山久遠寺.count: 2 massive ancient trees (approximately 400 years old) and hundreds of others nearby
身延山久遠寺.address: 山梨県南巨摩郡身延町身延3567
身延山久遠寺.access_train: About 15 minutes by bus from JR Minobu Station
身延山久遠寺.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes by car from Minobu-san IC on the Chubu Expressway
身延山久遠寺.tagline: Explore the historic heart of Nichiren Buddhism, where ancient cherry trees whisper tales of devotion.
身延山久遠寺.desc:
身延山久遠寺 is the first major temple of the Nichiren sect, founded by Saint Nichiren. From late March to early April, hundreds of cherry blossoms bloom around the majestic shrine and towering vermilion five-story pagoda, including two massive weeping cherry trees that are over 400 years old.

The most iconic view features a classic dialogue between the "grand five-story pagoda, ancient moss, and cascading pink weeping cherry blossoms." Vivid flower clusters pour down like a waterfall from the heights, beautifully contrasting with ancient stone lanterns. Under the shade of towering cedar trees in the deep mountains, this scene embodies a serene and spiritual Eastern aesthetic.

The path from the main gate to the main hall is a natural gravel approach, featuring a steep historical stone staircase known as "Otoko-zaka" with 287 steps. For those with limited stamina, a comfortable slope elevator or the Minobu-san cable car is available. Visiting in the early morning on weekdays offers a quieter experience, allowing you to enjoy the pure morning bells and perfect lighting for photography.

高遠城址公園.types: Takato Shidarezakura (unique variety, deeper pink color)
高遠城址公園.address: 長野県伊那市高遠町東高遠
高遠城址公園.access_train: About 25 minutes by bus from JR Ina City Station
高遠城址公園.access_car: About 30 minutes by car from Ina IC on the Chuo Expressway
高遠城址公園.desc:
Takato Jōshi Kōen is built on the ruins of Takato Castle, established by the Takato clan during the Edo period. It ranks among Japan's top three cherry blossom spots alongside Hirosaki and Yoshinoyama, and is featured in the top 100 cherry blossom sites in Japan. From early to mid-April each year, the park bursts into bloom with an impressive 1,500 unique Takato Shidarezakura trees, revered as the 'World's Best Cherry Blossom.'

The most stunning visual highlight is the legendary 'Retro Sakura Cloud Bridge, Old Moat, and Pink Cherry Blossom Cloud' all in one frame. This variety of cherry blossom has slightly smaller blooms and a deep pink hue. When the lush flower clusters nearly obscure the ancient stone walls and wooden arch bridge, the scene delivers a powerful visual impact, creating a grand atmosphere.

Since the main cherry blossom areas are concentrated around the historical castle's fortifications and steep slopes, it is highly recommended to wear comfortable, non-slip running shoes when visiting. During cherry blossom season, surrounding roads can experience severe traffic congestion. Arriving early on weekdays is the best way to capture the ethereal beauty of the landscape in the crisp morning air.
高遠城址公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of 1,500 Takato Shidarezakura, the 'World's Best Cherry Blossom' in a historic castle park.

松本城.fee: 免費（進入天守閣大人 700 円）
松本城.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from JR Matsumoto Station
松本城.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from Matsumoto IC on the Nagano Expressway
松本城.tagline: Experience the majestic black castle and illuminate your night with 300 cherry blossoms in full bloom.
松本城.desc:
Matsumoto Castle, built during the Eisho era, is one of Japan's four national treasures, renowned for its striking black-and-white "Crow Castle" appearance. Every year from early to mid-April, the famous old moat surrounding the castle bursts into life, showcasing around 300 cherry blossom trees, including Somei Yoshino and Edo-higan, adorning the historic castle walls.

The core feature is the "national treasure black donjon, vermilion gates, and classic autumn foliage scenery." Colorful blooms flourish along the gentle waterways, and when the calm water perfectly reflects the majestic national treasure castle tower, the distant snow-capped Northern Alps, and the full cherry blossom canopy, the nighttime illumination event enhances the ethereal beauty of the reflections.

The park's terrain is completely flat, and the ancient pathways are well-maintained, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. However, the stairs inside the national treasure donjon are quite steep, so please be cautious during your visit. Set aside an hour to stroll, as early mornings on weekdays have the fewest crowds, making it the perfect time to capture the historic castle in its mirror-like splendor.

根尾谷淡墨櫻.types: 淡墨櫻 (one of Japan's three great cherry blossoms)
根尾谷淡墨櫻.count: 1 tree (approximately 1,500 years old, blooms pink to white, and fades to ink color)
根尾谷淡墨櫻.hours: 24 小時開放（賞櫻期夜間點燈至 21:00）
根尾谷淡墨櫻.address: 岐阜県本巣市根尾板所字上段495
根尾谷淡墨櫻.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from Tarumi Station on the Tarumi Railway
根尾谷淡墨櫻.access_car: Approximately a 40-minute drive from the Tokai Kanjo Expressway, Yamagata IC
根尾谷淡墨櫻.tagline: Witness the breathtaking transformation of a 1,500-year-old sacred tree, from white to delicate ink hues.
根尾谷淡墨櫻.desc:
根尾谷淡墨櫻, alongside Miharu Takizakura and Yamagata Jindai Zakura, is celebrated as one of Japan's three great cherry blossoms and is designated as a national natural monument. Every year in early April, this majestic tree, nestled in a serene valley and over 1,500 years old, bursts into bloom with a stunning vitality that is unmatched across Japan.

The core visual highlight is the miraculous transformation of the blossoms from pure white to elegant ink hues as they fall. At peak bloom, the tree showcases translucent white flower clusters, seamlessly transitioning to a delicate ink color reminiscent of traditional Japanese ink wash. The lush blooms intertwine with the ancient, robust trunk, exuding a profound sense of Eastern history, Zen spirit, and sacred energy.

To protect the delicate roots of this ancient tree, a well-maintained wooden walkway and fence surround it, making it very accessible for the elderly and those with strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, strict traffic regulations are enforced on the surrounding roads. For the best experience, visit early in the morning before dawn to enjoy the ethereal landscape unique to this sacred tree.

新境川堤.address: 岐阜県各務原市那加門前町周辺
新境川堤.access_train: Get off at Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line 'Shimin Koen Mae Station' for easy access.
新境川堤.access_car: Approximately a 10-minute drive from 'Tōkai Hokuriku Expressway' Gifu Kakamigahara IC.
新境川堤.tagline: Stroll along Kakamigahara's scenic riverside and be awed by the cherry blossom tunnel of Hyakujurozakura by the grand red bridge.
新境川堤.desc:
Shinkyogawa Tei is a scenic riverside park named after the Hyakujurozakura, gifted by the kabuki actor Ichikawa Hyakujuro, and is listed among Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Every year, from early April, the banks of the winding Shinkyogawa in the city center burst into bloom with 1,200 massive Somei Yoshino cherry trees, transforming into a breathtaking pink tunnel.

The highlight is the "romantic duality of the vintage grand red bridge, the flowing cherry blossom ribbon, and the pink flower rafts." As you leisurely stroll along the smooth riverside path, the lush branches intertwine above, and the calm water perfectly reflects the grand red bridge and colorful flower balls, showcasing a refined urban aesthetic.

The riverside walking path is completely flat and well-paved, making it perfect for families to enjoy leisurely walks, bike rides, and picnics. During the cherry blossom festival, enchanting night illuminations are held. For the best chance to capture the perfect "upside-down cherry blossom" reflection without ripples, seasoned photographers highly recommend visiting on weekday mornings.

飛驒一之宮水無神社.count: 1 tree (approximately 1,100 years old, resembling a sleeping dragon)
飛驒一之宮水無神社.address: 岐阜県高山市一之宮町石原
飛驒一之宮水無神社.access_train: About a 1-minute walk from JR Takayama Main Line 'Hida Ichinomiya Station'
飛驒一之宮水無神社.access_car: Approximately a 20-minute drive from 'Chubu Jukan Expressway' Takayama IC
飛驒一之宮水無神社.tagline: Step into the sacred realm of Hida's Ichinomiya Shrine, where wooden halls meet the timeless beauty of the ancient 'Waryu Zakura.'
飛驒一之宮水無神社.desc:
Mizuno Shrine is an ancient shrine steeped in history, dedicated to protecting the Hida region. From mid to late April, the 'Waryu Park,' adjacent to the main shrine, showcases an ancient Edo-period cherry tree known for its dragon-like form, bursting into bloom and adorning the solemn sacred grounds with the vibrant colors of spring.

The most representative aspect of Japanese aesthetics here is the dialogue between the 'over 1,100-year-old designated natural monument Waryu Zakura and the ancient torii gate.' This majestic tree, with its soft pink blossoms cascading down, harmonizes beautifully with the rustic stone lanterns and wooden shrine buildings, perfectly embodying the tranquil historical Zen spirit unique to this ancient site.

The terrain around the shrine and park is flat and spacious, with well-maintained ancient stone paths, making it easy for families and seniors to stroll leisurely. During the cherry blossom season, exquisite sakura-themed limited edition goshuin stamps are available. Visiting on weekday mornings is ideal, as fewer worshippers allow you to capture the ethereal morning light that fills the air.

河津櫻並木.types: 河津櫻 (early blooming cherry blossoms, early February to early March)
河津櫻並木.count: approximately 850 trees (about 8,000 in total throughout the town)
河津櫻並木.hours: 24 小時開放（櫻花祭期間點燈至 21:00）
河津櫻並木.address: 静岡県賀茂郡河津町浜
河津櫻並木.access_train: Just a short walk from 伊豆急行 "河津駅"
河津櫻並木.access_car: About 80 minutes from "Tōmei Expressway" Numazu IC via the Izu Skyline.
河津櫻並木.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of 河津櫻 along a stunning four-kilometer stretch of vibrant canola flowers.
河津櫻並木.desc:
河津櫻並木, located in the warm southern region of the Izu Peninsula, is a world-renowned destination and the epitome of early cherry blossoms in Japan. Thanks to its unique subtropical climate, the flowers bloom exceptionally early, from mid-February to early March, with around 800 河津櫻 trees bursting into full bloom along the banks of the 河津川 for a spectacular four kilometers.

The most breathtaking sight is the stunning contrast of a "full blanket of yellow canola flowers against a tunnel of dense deep pink cherry blossoms." Stroll leisurely along the gentle riverside promenade, where you are enveloped by a thick canopy of cherry blossoms overhead, while to your right lies an expansive field of golden flowers, creating a sense of openness and a striking visual impact.

The riverside walkway is completely flat and well-maintained, making it very friendly for leisurely strolls and for those accompanying elderly visitors. During the cherry blossom festival, a magical multi-colored lighting display illuminates the area at night. If you want to avoid the overwhelming crowds and capture the cleanest, most refined shots, experienced photographers strongly recommend visiting on weekday mornings before dawn.

富士靈園.count: approximately 8,000 trees (cherry blossoms with the magnificent view of Mount Fuji)
富士靈園.address: 静岡県駿東郡小山町用沢232
富士靈園.access_train: About 25 minutes by temporary bus from JR Gotemba Station
富士靈園.access_car: About 20 minutes drive from Tomei Expressway Gotemba IC
富士靈園.tagline: Experience a breathtaking cherry blossom corridor at the foot of Mount Fuji, a must-see in Japan!
富士靈園.desc:
富士靈園, located on the expansive slopes of Mount Fuji, is a stunning destination that beautifully blends modern geometric gardening aesthetics with the wonders of nature. It is recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From early to mid-April, the massive central avenue running through the park and the sloping sides burst into bloom with around 1,000 cherry trees of the Somei Yoshino variety.  

The most striking visual highlight is the "symmetrical central staircase corridor framed by the majestic Mount Fuji." From the highest viewpoint, you can gaze down at the colorful flower walls that extend infinitely along the slopes, perfectly juxtaposed against the conical silhouette of the snow-capped mountain, creating a breathtaking three-dimensional landscape.  

The main cherry blossom viewing paths and walking trails are well-paved and equipped with comfortable gentle slopes, making it very family-friendly for visitors of all ages. Given the serene and sacred nature of the environment, please remember to maintain silence and etiquette while enjoying the cherry blossoms. The best visibility is in the clear mornings, perfect for capturing the stunning scenery.

日本平.address: 静岡県静岡市清水区草薙
日本平.access_train: About a 35-minute bus ride from JR Shizuoka Station on the Nihondaira Line
日本平.access_car: About a 25-minute drive from Shizuoka IC or Shimizu IC on the Tomei Expressway
日本平.tagline: Experience breathtaking views of Mount Fuji, Shimizu Port, and 2,000 vibrant cherry blossoms at this national treasure.
日本平.desc:
Nihondaira is a solitary hilltop at an elevation of 307 meters, designated as a national scenic spot. From late March to early April each year, around 2,000 Somei Yoshino cherry trees bloom spectacularly, painting the natural landscape of Suruga Bay in a riot of colors.

The most stunning sight here is the "360-degree unobstructed view of the ocean, snow-capped Mount Fuji, and pink cherry blossoms all in one frame." Standing on the observation deck of the avant-garde wooden structure, Nihondaira Dream Terrace, you can take in the majestic snow-capped mountains, the emerald bay, and the vibrant floral carpet right before your eyes, creating a truly grand atmosphere.

The mountaintop area is spacious and flat, fully accessible, making it very friendly for strollers and elderly visitors with mobility issues. You can take the Nihondaira Ropeway directly to Kunozan Toshogu Shrine. Early mornings offer the clearest air, making it the perfect time to capture the stunning views of Mount Fuji framed by cherry blossoms for your photography.

山崎川四季之路.hours: 24 小時開放（鼎橋周邊有點燈）
山崎川四季之路.address: 愛知県名古屋市瑞穂区山崎川沿い
山崎川四季之路.access_train: 15-minute walk from Meitetsu Sakura-dori Line 'Mizuho Ward Office Station'
山崎川四季之路.access_car: No dedicated parking available; taking the subway is recommended.
山崎川四季之路.tagline: Discover a serene riverside oasis in Nagoya, where cherry blossoms bloom in timeless elegance.
山崎川四季之路.desc:
Yamazaki River 'Shiki no Michi' is an urban ecological corridor stretching along both banks of the Yamazaki River, recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From late March to early April, the breathtaking display of 500 ancient Somei Yoshino cherry trees bursts into bloom along the 2.5-kilometer stone-paved banks, radiating classic charm.

The visual highlight is the 'classic wooden Kanae Bridge, where gentle streams meet a mirror of pink blossoms.' Delicate pink flower walls sway gently with the breeze, and as the morning light filters through the trees, it dances on the flowing water and the cherry blossom-covered rafts, creating a harmonious blend of tranquility and refined waterside horticultural beauty.

The riverside promenade is completely flat and well-maintained, making it very accessible for seniors and strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, certain sections are illuminated with a magical light display at night. For the perfect, ripple-free water mirror and pristine sky, seasoned photographers highly recommend visiting on weekday mornings.

五條川.address: 愛知県岩倉市下本町下市場周辺
五條川.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from Meitetsu Inuyama Line 'Ikakra Station'
五條川.access_car: During the cherry blossom season, extensive traffic controls are in place, so taking the train is recommended.
五條川.tagline: Experience a breathtaking 14-kilometer cherry blossom corridor and the traditional 'Nonbori-sai' festival.
五條川.desc:
Gojo River is a stunning 14-kilometer cherry blossom viewing spot that stretches across Ikakra City and Okuchi Town, proudly listed among Japan's top cherry blossom sites. From late March to early April, the banks of this gentle river come alive with 1,400 ancient Somei Yoshino cherry trees, all blooming in unison, creating a mesmerizing pink corridor.

The most iconic sight here is the traditional craft of the koi nobori, beautifully juxtaposed against the vibrant pink flower wall. Watching artisans wash colorful koi flags in the clear stream while being enveloped by the cascading cherry blossom tunnel above creates a unique cultural landscape found nowhere else in Japan.

The riverside pathway is well-maintained and completely flat, making it a comfortable and leisurely route for long-distance walking. Although the cherry blossom festival attracts large crowds, visiting on weekday mornings allows you to easily avoid the rush, making it the perfect time to capture the refreshing beauty of the landscape.

岡崎公園.count: about 800 trees (the most popular night sakura in the Tokai region)
岡崎公園.access_train: 15-minute walk from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line 'Higashi-Okazaki Station'
岡崎公園.access_car: About 10 minutes by car from 'Okazaki IC' on the Tomei Expressway
岡崎公園.tagline: Experience the birthplace of Tokugawa Ieyasu, surrounded by 800 cherry blossoms and Japan's finest night sakura.
岡崎公園.desc:
Okazaki Park is built on the historic site of Okazaki Castle, the birthplace of Tokugawa Ieyasu from the Edo period, and is listed among Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From late March to early April, around 800 Somei Yoshino cherry trees bloom in unison, enveloping the majestic stone walls and the old moat, creating a breathtaking scene around this historical monument.

The most stunning sight is the renowned night illumination, celebrated as the best in the Tokai region. Standing by the tranquil inner moat, you'll be captivated by the vibrant red and pink blossoms illuminated by thousands of snow cave lanterns, perfectly framed by the wooden keep and the sturdy stone walls, offering a grand and expansive view.

The park's terrain is mostly gentle hills, with well-maintained main paths that make for a comfortable stroll. Inside, you'll find the Mikawa Samurai History Museum, ideal for history enthusiasts exploring at their own pace. For photographers seeking a serene reflection of the keep without crowds or ripples, visiting on weekday mornings is highly recommended.

三多氣.count: Approximately 500 trees (the reflections of the terraced fields and cherry blossoms are stunning)
三多氣.address: 三重県津市美杉町三多気
三多氣.access_train: About a 10-minute bus ride from JR Ise-Otsu Station
三多氣.access_car: Approximately 1 hour drive from Kusu IC on the Ise Expressway
三多氣.tagline: Discover a hidden mountain village where history meets breathtaking cherry blossoms.
三多氣.desc:
Santaki, nestled in the valley before Shinpo-in, is a beautifully preserved rural gem, recognized as a national scenic spot and one of Japan's top cherry blossom locations. Each year, in mid-April, along the 1.5-kilometer natural slope, 500 ancient mountain cherry trees reach their peak bloom.  
  
The most stunning sight is the "reflected mirror" of the retro thatched-roof farmhouse, irrigated terraced fields, and mountain cherry blossoms. Standing at a special photo spot, the tranquil, water-filled rice paddies perfectly mirror the lush green mountains, ancient stone walls, and pink cherry blossoms, creating a breathtaking ink wash painting effect.  
  
Due to the steep terrain of this preserved area, the paths are mostly natural dirt roads and gravel slopes. It is highly recommended to wear professional non-slip sports shoes when visiting for cherry blossom photography. Driving is the most convenient way to get there. The calm, windless mornings provide the best conditions for capturing stunning water mirror scenes.

名花之里.types: Kawazu cherry, weeping cherry, Somei Yoshino cherry
名花之里.count: approximately 300 trees (combined with large-scale winter illuminations)
名花之里.fee: 大人 2,500 円（含園內 1,600 円消費券）
名花之里.address: 三重県桑名市長島町駒江漆畑270
名花之里.access_train: About 10 minutes by direct bus from Kintetsu Nagashima Station
名花之里.access_car: About 10 minutes by car from Nagashima IC on the Tomei-Hanshin Expressway
名花之里.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of Japan's largest nighttime illuminations and mirrored cherry blossoms.
名花之里.desc:
名花之里 (Nabana no Sato) is a massive landscape park renowned worldwide for its spectacular nighttime illuminations and greenhouse gardens. From late February's Kawazu cherry blossoms to early April's Yoshino and weeping cherries, the park bursts into bloom. The famous maple corridor and the area around the large pond reach their peak, showcasing a vibrant tapestry of colors.

The highlight that captivates visitors is undoubtedly the "ultimate nighttime illuminations, water corridor, and the mirrored pink flower wall." Standing by the tranquil waterside pond, the vivid flower balls illuminated by stunning multicolored lights contrast beautifully with the distant lit landmarks. As a gentle breeze ripples across the water, the scene transforms into a magical fantasy.

The park is thoughtfully designed with fully accessible pathways, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. The best time to visit is around 4 PM, allowing you to capture the stunning sunset colors and the striking visuals of the initial illuminations at night.

海津大崎.count: about 800 trees (spanning 4 kilometers along Lake Biwa)
海津大崎.hours: 24 小時開放（賞櫻期禁止私家車進入，需搭接駁車）
海津大崎.address: 滋賀県高島市マキノ町海津
海津大崎.access_train: Approximately a 30-minute walk from JR Makino Station or take a shuttle bus.
海津大崎.access_car: One-way traffic restrictions are in place on weekends during cherry blossom season; taking the train is recommended.
海津大崎.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of cherry blossoms at Kaizu Osaki, a must-visit gem by Lake Biwa.
海津大崎.desc:
Kaizu Osaki, located along the steep cliffs of the northern shore of Lake Biwa, is one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots, renowned for its stunning views in the Kinki region. From early to mid-April, the 4-kilometer stretch of road lined with over 800 towering Somei Yoshino cherry trees, planted during the Taisho era, bursts into a spectacular bloom.

The most breathtaking scene unfolds as you witness the "unmatched emerald Lake Biwa, dramatic cliffs, and a pink cherry blossom tunnel" all in one frame. As you leisurely drive along the gentle lakeside road, you'll find yourself under a dense canopy of blossoms. When the lake is calm, the horizon blends seamlessly with the sky, creating an awe-inspiring sense of space.

Since this spot is essentially an open public road with some narrow sections, please be mindful of safety while driving slowly or walking to find the perfect angle for your photos. On certain weekends during the cherry blossom season, vehicle restrictions are in place. For the best reflections, arrive early on weekdays before dawn when the lake is at its calmest.

彥根城.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from JR Hikone Station
彥根城.access_car: Approximately a 10-minute drive from the Meishin Expressway Hikone IC
彥根城.tagline: Experience the majestic Hikone Castle, where cherry blossoms light up the night sky.
彥根城.desc:
Hikone Castle, built during the Keicho era, is a magnificent fortress that has preserved its Edo-period architecture and is one of Japan's national treasures. Every year, from early to mid-April, around 1,100 cherry blossoms of the Somei Yoshino variety burst into bloom along the expansive moat, beautifully framing the ancient castle walls.

The core features include the national treasure of the white-topped main keep, the scenic Genkyu-en garden, and the classical views of cherry blossoms. Colorful blooms line the gentle waterways, and when the still waters of the pond perfectly reflect the towering keep, the vintage wooden bridges, and the full cherry blossom canopy, the night illuminations create an exquisite display of Japanese aesthetics.

The castle is situated on natural hills, and reaching the main keep requires climbing a steep set of ancient stone steps, so wearing non-slip athletic shoes is recommended. The flat terrain of Genkyu-en garden is perfect for leisurely strolls. During the illumination festival, enchanting lights are showcased at night. Visiting on weekday mornings offers the most tranquil experience.

豐公園.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from JR Nagahama Station
豐公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from the Hokuriku Expressway Nagahama IC
豐公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of cherry blossoms by Lake Biwa at this historic site!
豐公園.desc:
Hō Park is built on the historic grounds of Nagahama Castle, originally established by Toyotomi Hideyoshi, and is recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Its standout feature is its proximity to the vast eastern shores of Lake Biwa. Every year in early April, around 600 stunning Somei Yoshino cherry trees bloom around the reconstructed wooden keep, creating a magnificent spectacle.

The park’s highlight is the breathtaking view from the top of the keep, where you can gaze down at the sprawling pink blossoms that blanket the ground, merging beautifully with the expansive lake and mountain scenery in the distance, showcasing a grand aesthetic of nature.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained ancient pathways, making it very accessible for seniors and strollers. There is a large grassy plaza within the park, which is renowned for its stunning sunset views, earning a place among Japan's top sunset spots, especially for capturing the enchanting night cherry blossoms. Visiting on weekday mornings offers the least crowds, ideal for capturing pristine views of the castle.

醍醐寺.types: weeping cherry, Somei Yoshino cherry, mountain cherry, double-flowered cherry
醍醐寺.fee: 大人 1,000 円（春季特別拜觀）
醍醐寺.address: 京都府京都市伏見区醍醐東大路町22
醍醐寺.access_train: 10-minute walk from Daigo Station on the Tozai Line
醍醐寺.access_car: 15-minute drive from Uji Higashi IC on the Keiji Bypass
醍醐寺.desc:
Daigo-ji is a world heritage temple founded by the revered monk Rigen. Renowned for its collection of over a thousand ancient weeping cherry trees and Somei Yoshino cherry blossoms, it has long been celebrated as the "Flower of Daigo." Each year, from mid-March to early April, the sacred grounds burst into a riot of colors, enchanting visitors with their beauty.

The most iconic view features the National Treasure Five-Story Pagoda, the Sanboin Main Gate, and the cascading pink weeping cherry blossoms, creating a stunning dialogue across time. The vibrant blooms seem to pour down from the sky like a waterfall, harmonizing with Kyoto's oldest wooden pagoda, which dates back to the Edo period, exuding a profound sense of historical tranquility.

The temple grounds are spacious, with well-maintained pathways for visitors. During peak season, strict crowd control measures are in place within the Rinpokan and Sanboin to ensure safety. The paths are mostly natural gravel, so comfortable, non-slip shoes are recommended. Arriving early allows you to enjoy the ethereal light for photography as soon as the gates open.
醍醐寺.tagline: Discover the legendary cherry blossoms of Daigo, where history meets breathtaking beauty.

哲學之道.address: 京都府京都市左京区若王子神社至銀閣寺
哲學之道.access_train: Take city bus 5 or 17 from JR Kyoto Station to "Ginkakuji-michi."
哲學之道.access_car: Traffic around the area is restricted and parking is extremely limited; taking the bus is recommended.
哲學之道.tagline: Stroll along the Philosopher's Path, where a breathtaking tunnel of cherry blossoms awaits you.
哲學之道.desc:
The Philosopher's Path is a scenic walkway along the Biwa Canal, named after the philosopher Nishida Kitaro who often strolled here. It is recognized as one of Japan's top 100 roads. From late March to early April, the 2-kilometer stretch is transformed into a stunning pink tunnel as the cherry blossoms (Somei Yoshino) burst into bloom.

The highlight of this experience is the "romantic duality of clear canal waters, classic wooden bridges, and floating pink petals." As you leisurely walk along the smooth stone path, the lush branches intertwine above, creating a canopy. After the peak bloom, the fallen petals blanket the water's surface, turning the canal into a mesmerizing pink ribbon.

The flat riverside path is conveniently located near Ginkaku-ji and Honen-in, making it perfect for travelers seeking a carefree morning stroll. During the cherry blossom festival, crowds can be overwhelming, so if you want to capture the perfect reflection of the "sakura" without ripples, it’s highly recommended to visit on a weekday morning before dawn.

平野神社.types: Sakigake Sakura and about 60 other varieties
平野神社.fee: 免費（夜間點燈可能收費）
平野神社.address: 京都府京都市北区平野宮本町1
平野神社.access_train: Take city bus 205 from JR Kyoto Station to "Kinugasa Koumae."
平野神社.access_car: About a 30-minute drive from Meishin Expressway Kyoto Minami IC.
平野神社.tagline: Discover the sacred realm of Heian-kyo, where rare cherry blossoms bloom in a breathtaking spectacle.
平野神社.desc:
Hirano Jinja is a prestigious shrine, classified as a Kanpei Taisha, renowned since the Heian period for its rare cherry blossom varieties. From mid-March to mid-April, the shrine's majestic vermilion halls and ancient torii gates are surrounded by around 400 cherry trees, showcasing up to 60 different species in a stunning floral display.

The highlight features include the "Vermilion Great Torii, historic worship hall, lantern-lined corridors, and classical flower scenery." Especially noteworthy is the limited-bloom landmark "Sakigake Sakura," with its vibrant pink blooms beautifully framed by the ancient wooden shrine. As hundreds of snow lanterns illuminate the scene at night, it reveals an exquisite aesthetic of traditional Japanese religious beauty.

The shrine grounds are completely flat, and the ancient gravel paths are well-maintained, making it easy to stroll around—perfect for seniors and those with strollers. During the cherry blossom season, a grand "Sakura Matsuri" festival and portable shrine procession take place. Visiting on weekday mornings offers a quieter experience, ideal for capturing the serene cultural landscape of this sacred space.

仁和寺.types: Omuro cherry (late-blooming cherry), Somei Yoshino cherry
仁和寺.count: About 200 Omuro cherry trees and several hundred surrounding trees
仁和寺.fee: 大人 500 円（特別入山料）
仁和寺.address: 京都府京都市右京区御室大内33
仁和寺.access_train: Arrive at Kyofu Electric Railway (Arashiyama Line) 'Omuro Ninnaji Station'
仁和寺.access_car: Approximately a 40-minute drive from 'Meishin Expressway' Kyoto Minami IC
仁和寺.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of the late-blooming Omuro cherry blossoms against the backdrop of a national treasure five-story pagoda.
仁和寺.desc:
仁和寺 is a world heritage Zen temple founded by Emperor Uta, perfectly preserving the grandeur of the Omuro Palace. Known for its unique late-blooming variety, the Omuro cherry blossoms (オムロザクラ), this spot showcases around 200 magnificent trees that bloom in mid-April, creating a stunning sea of pink blossoms.

The visual highlight is the breathtaking view of the low Omuro cherry blossoms against the majestic five-story pagoda. Stroll along the wooden corridor or gentle pathways, where the cherry trees are at eye level, making you feel enveloped in a vibrant pink floral wall, perfectly framed by the imposing pagoda in the background.

The temple's layout is well-planned, with some historical steps leading to the main hall, but the main cherry blossom viewing paths are very friendly for seniors. The autumn and winter months can feel a bit chilly, and visitors are required to remove their shoes when entering the halls. It's highly recommended to visit early on a weekday morning as soon as the gates open to capture the ethereal Zen atmosphere in the air.

平安神宮.count: about 300 trees (including stunning reflections in the garden)
平安神宮.address: 京都府京都市左京区岡崎西天王町97
平安神宮.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from Higashiyama Station on the Tozai Line
平安神宮.access_car: Approximately a 20-minute drive from Kyoto Higashi IC on the Meishin Expressway
平安神宮.tagline: Experience the timeless beauty of cherry blossoms at Heian Shrine's enchanting gardens.
平安神宮.desc:
Heian Jingu is a magnificent shrine commemorating the millennium of the Heian capital's relocation, perfectly recreating the grandeur of the Heian era's court. Nestled within the expansive 10-acre garden designed by Ogawa Jihei, the 'Shin'en' features around 150 stunning 'Yaebeni Shidarezakura' cherry trees that reach their peak in early April, exuding classical elegance.

The visual highlight is the breathtaking reflection of the 'Kyokushin Kōbikan,' the wooden bridge of Taiheikaku, against the tranquil pink blossoms mirrored in the serene 'Shihō Pond.' As vibrant pink flower clusters cascade from the sky like a waterfall, their perfect reflection on the calm pond creates a rich tapestry of beauty, showcasing the refined aesthetics of traditional Japanese architecture.

The pathways within the garden are flat and well-planned, making them very accessible for seniors and strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, the garden hosts Japan's premier 'Shidarezakura Concert,' which is immensely popular. For the best experience, visit early in the morning right after opening to enjoy the serene atmosphere of this historic garden.

萬博紀念公園.fee: 大人 260 円（公園入園料）
萬博紀念公園.address: 大阪府吹田市千里万博公園1-1
萬博紀念公園.access_train: Easily accessible via the Osaka Monorail at "Banpaku Kinen Koen Station."
萬博紀念公園.access_car: About a 5-minute drive from the Chugoku Expressway's Chugoku Suita IC.
萬博紀念公園.tagline: Explore the historic site of the 1970 World Expo, home to the iconic Tower of the Sun and 5,000 cherry blossoms.
萬博紀念公園.desc:
Banpaku Kinen Koen is a sprawling urban oasis built on the former site of the 1970 World Expo, recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From late March to early April, the area around the Natural Cultural Park's "Higashido" and the Japanese garden bursts into bloom with approximately 5,000 Somei Yoshino cherry trees, showcasing nine different varieties.

The park's visual centerpiece is the striking contrast between the avant-garde Tower of the Sun, lush green fields, and a vibrant pink floral carpet. Pink flower trails stretch along the main avenue, and as the morning sunlight filters through the treetops onto the open path, it frames the magnificent Tower of the Sun designed by Taro Okamoto, embodying refined modern aesthetics.

The park's terrain is mostly flat, with well-maintained, accessible pathways and wooden walkways, making it very friendly for seniors and those with strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, the park features a nighttime illumination show and numerous food stalls. On weekdays, the park opens at 9:30 AM, making it the perfect time for photography and capturing the pristine autumn scenery.

勝尾寺.count: About 8,000 trees (blooming later due to higher elevation)
勝尾寺.address: 大阪府箕面市粟生間谷2914-1
勝尾寺.access_train: About 35 minutes by bus from Chihon Chuo Station on the subway
勝尾寺.access_car: About 10 minutes by car from the Minoh Hōkō IC on the Shin-Meishin Expressway
勝尾寺.tagline: Discover the serene beauty of Katsuouji Temple, where late-blooming cherry blossoms and ancient traditions intertwine.
勝尾寺.desc:
Katsuouji Temple, nestled in the mountains of Minoh City, is a millennium-old sanctuary renowned for its prayers for victory and its famous "Katsuon Daruma" dolls. Being a high-altitude spiritual site, the blooming season here is later than in the plains, reaching its peak in mid-April when thousands of elegant weeping cherry trees and local sakura burst into bloom.

The core feature is the dialogue between the misty waterside scenery and the historical presence of the Daruma. Pink and pure white blossoms spread along the shores of Kannon Pond and the sloping hills. When the pond releases a mystical white mist, it creates a stunning backdrop with countless red Daruma hidden among the flowers, complemented by the towering pagoda, which takes on an enchanting atmosphere when illuminated at night.

As the entire temple is built along the hillside, the approach includes many steps, so wearing comfortable, non-slip sports shoes is highly recommended. The grounds are equipped with accessible pathways, and visiting on weekday mornings offers a quieter experience, making it the perfect time to capture the pristine spiritual realm and magnificent Daruma views.

姬路城.fee: 免費（天守閣大人 1,000 円）
姬路城.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from JR Himeji Station
姬路城.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from Himeji Higashi IC on the Sanyo Expressway
姬路城.desc:
Himeji Castle is Japan's first UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the twelve remaining original castles. Every year from late March to early April, the famous "San no Maru Plaza" and the old moat come alive with around a thousand cherry trees, including Somei Yoshino and Yamazakura, creating a stunning contrast against the castle's pure white walls.

The core feature here is the "pure white main keep, sturdy stone walls, and the classic cherry blossom scenery." Vibrant blooms line the gentle waterways, and when the calm moat reflects the magnificent national treasure keep and the full canopy of cherry blossoms, the scene feels expansive and balanced, showcasing the grandeur of this historic city.

The castle grounds are situated on natural hills, and reaching the main keep requires climbing a steep set of ancient stone steps, so wearing non-slip athletic shoes is recommended. The interior of the keep is well-preserved. Allow 1.5 hours for your visit, and if you go early on weekdays, you can avoid the crowds and capture the perfect mirror-like shots of this historic gem.
姬路城.tagline: Experience the majestic Himeji Castle, surrounded by a sea of cherry blossoms in full bloom.

夙川公園.address: 兵庫県西宮市夙川・結善・南郷町
夙川公園.access_train: Easily accessible from Hankyu Kobe Line 'Shukugawa Station' or JR 'Sakurashukugawa Station'.
夙川公園.access_car: Parking is extremely difficult; public transportation is recommended.
夙川公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of 1,600 cherry blossoms along the serene banks of Shukugawa.
夙川公園.desc:
Shukugawa Park (Shukugawa Riverside Green Space) is a sprawling urban oasis stretching 2.8 kilometers along the banks of the Shukugawa River, recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From late March to early April, the 1,600 towering Somei Yoshino cherry trees burst into bloom, transforming the area into a stunning pink tunnel.

The visual highlight not to be missed is the interplay of the clear stream, vintage stone arch bridges, and the delicate pink blossoms. The flower clusters droop gracefully towards the river, perfectly blending with the lush green grass at your feet. As the gentle breeze blows, the petals sway with the flowing stream, exuding the refined elegance unique to Nishinomiya's upscale residential area.

The riverside promenade is completely flat and well-maintained, making it very friendly for strollers and leisurely travelers. It connects easily with the nearby Kurakuen and Ashiya shopping districts. We strongly recommend visiting in the early morning when the water is calm, allowing you to capture the crystal-clear reflections of the blossoms on the water's surface.

吉野山.count: approximately 30,000 trees (divided into Shimo Senbon, Naka Senbon, Ue Senbon, and Oku Senbon)
吉野山.hours: 24 小時開放（夜間點燈至 22:00 左右）
吉野山.fee: 免費（賞櫻期間有停車收費及接駁巴士）
吉野山.address: 奈良県吉野郡吉野町吉野山
吉野山.access_train: Take the Kintetsu train to Yoshino Station, then ride the cable car or shuttle bus.
吉野山.access_car: During cherry blossom season, traffic is fully regulated; private cars must park at outer lots and transfer to shuttle buses.
吉野山.desc:
Yoshinoyama is a renowned World Heritage site famous for its stunning "Ichimoku Senbon" cherry blossoms. Each year, from early April to late April, the peak bloom period arrives, with 30,000 ancient Shiroyama cherry trees bursting into a spectacular display across the mountain, thanks to varying elevations.

The most visually striking aspect is the composition of the Kimpusen-ji Zao-do temple, deep mountain valleys, and colorful forests. From the viewpoints of Yoshimizu Shrine or Hanayagura, the pink-hued sea of blossoms cascades down the steep slopes, beautifully framed by ancient wooden temples, creating a grand sense of space that showcases the majestic aesthetics of religion.

While you can take the Yoshinoyama Ropeway to ascend the mountain, the attractions at the summit are spread out, and natural slopes connect the various shrines. Be sure to wear professional anti-slip hiking shoes. The mountain often shrouded in morning fog offers a mysterious and ethereal beauty to the blooming flowers, so choosing a clear morning with good visibility is ideal for capturing the expansive scenery.
吉野山.tagline: Experience a breathtaking tapestry of 30,000 cherry blossoms on this sacred mountain of World Heritage.

又兵衛櫻.types: Weeping cherry (famous single ancient cherry tree over a thousand years old)
又兵衛櫻.count: 1 tree (approximately 300 years old, linked to the legend of Sengoku general Goto Yabe)
又兵衛櫻.fee: 免費（需付費維護協力金 100 円）
又兵衛櫻.address: 奈良県宇陀市大宇陀本郷
又兵衛櫻.access_train: About a 20-minute bus ride from Kintetsu "Hayabihara Station" to "Dai-Uda"
又兵衛櫻.access_car: Approximately a 30-minute drive from "Meihan National Route" Shina IC
又兵衛櫻.tagline: Experience the legendary beauty of Goto Yabe's cherry blossom and vibrant rapeseed fields in Uda City.
又兵衛櫻.desc:
Yabezakura (officially known as Hongō no Takizakura) is a stunning Edo-period weeping cherry tree over 300 years old, believed to have grown on the ruins of the residence of the famous Sengoku general Goto Yabe. Each year, it reaches its peak bloom in early April, transforming into a breathtaking sight with its towering height of 13 meters and a circumference of 3 meters.

The most striking visual experience comes from the "majestic stone walls, a blanket of vibrant yellow rapeseed flowers, and the timeless dialogue with the massive trunk of this ancient tree." In front of the rustic stone wall, this solitary giant stands tall, with countless slender branches cascading down like waterfalls, adorned with lush deep pink flower clusters that showcase the ultimate beauty of life.

To protect the delicate roots of this ancient tree, a well-maintained gravel path and supportive fencing have been constructed around it, making it very friendly for elderly visitors. There is also a local specialty market nearby. As this is a singular, extraordinary giant tree attraction, it is highly recommended to visit in the early morning when the sunlight beautifully captures the essence of its historical grandeur.

和歌山城.fee: 免費（天守閣大人 410 円）
和歌山城.address: 和歌山県和歌山市一番丁3
和歌山城.access_train: Take a bus from JR Wakayama Station for about 10 minutes to 'Shiyakusho-mae'.
和歌山城.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Wakayama IC on the Hanwa Expressway.
和歌山城.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of 600 cherry blossoms at Wakayama Castle's stunning gardens.
和歌山城.desc:
Wakayama Castle, perched atop the historic Torafusu Mountain, is one of Japan's top 100 castles and a renowned stronghold of the Kishu Tokugawa clan. From late March to early April, the famous Okaguchi Gate and the scenic Nishi no Maru Garden within the castle grounds come alive with an explosion of cherry blossoms, featuring around 600 Yoshino cherry trees in full bloom.

The core attraction lies in the mesmerizing interplay of the white castle tower, the sukiya-style Koi fish pavilion, and the cherry blossom scenery. Pink flower walls bloom alongside the clear inner moat, and when the tranquil, mirror-like water perfectly reflects the historic wooden structures and the vibrant cherry blossom canopy, it creates a spacious visual experience that showcases the beauty of traditional Japanese gardens.

The castle's terrain is naturally hilly, and reaching the main tower requires climbing a steep set of ancient stone steps, so wearing non-slip athletic shoes is recommended. Inside the garden, the tea house, Akamatsu-an, offers a perfect spot to enjoy matcha while admiring the cherry blossoms. Arriving early on weekdays allows you to fully enjoy the serene atmosphere and exceptional lighting for photography in this ancient garden.

紀三井寺.count: about 500 trees (the earliest blooming cherry blossom landmark in Kansai)
紀三井寺.address: 和歌山県和歌山市紀三井寺1201
紀三井寺.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Kimiidera Station
紀三井寺.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from Hanwa Expressway Kainan IC
紀三井寺.tagline: Climb the sacred steps of Kimiidera and witness Japan's earliest cherry blossom declaration.
紀三井寺.desc:
Kimiidera (officially Kimiidera Kōkokuin) is a historic temple founded by the revered monk Kōbō Daishi, renowned for being the site of Japan's earliest cherry blossom declaration. Every late March, the approach to the main hall bursts into bloom with around 500 Somei Yoshino and Yamazakura cherry trees, creating a breathtaking spectacle.

The temple's most striking aesthetic lies in the dialogue between the Important Cultural Property gate, the 231-step 'Ketsuen-zaka' stone staircase, and the vibrant flowers. As you ascend the ancient, winding stone steps, the lush, colorful floral walls rise almost to eye level, and from the main hall's observation deck, you can enjoy stunning views of Wakayama Bay.

Although reaching the main hall requires climbing the steep historical stone steps of Ketsuen-zaka, the temple has recently installed a comfortable, high-speed slope elevator, making it very accessible for elderly visitors and those with strollers. The elevated mountain area offers distant sea views. Early mornings on weekdays provide the most ethereal atmosphere, perfect for capturing stunning cultural landscapes.

打吹公園.types: Somei Yoshino, mountain cherry, and more
打吹公園.hours: 24 小時開放（賞櫻期夜間有點燈）
打吹公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Kurayoshi Station for about 15 minutes to Meiji-cho.
打吹公園.access_car: Approximately a 40-minute drive from the Yonago Expressway, Yubara IC.
打吹公園.tagline: Discover the cherry blossom paradise at the foot of Mount Daibutsu, with 4,000 sakura in full bloom.
打吹公園.desc:
Daibutsukouen, nestled at the base of the pristine Daibutsu Mountain, is a top-tier strolling garden established during the Edo period and recognized as one of Japan's 100 best cherry blossom spots. Every year, from early April, the park bursts into life with around 4,000 Somei Yoshino and mountain cherry trees, enveloping the lush, ancient grounds in a stunning display of pink.

The park's visual highlights include the striking interplay of the vermilion Daijingu Bridge, the waterside of Hamanokawa Pond, and the pink floral walls. Vibrant blossoms line the gently winding gravel paths, and when the tranquil waters perfectly reflect the classical vermilion drum bridge and the full canopy of cherry blossoms, the expansive space radiates an elegant Eastern Zen atmosphere.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained ancient pathways, making it very accessible for seniors and strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, up to 500 vintage snow lanterns illuminate the park at night, drawing many visitors. On regular mornings, the park opens with the least crowd, allowing you to capture the ethereal Zen ambiance in the air.

久松公園.access_train: About 8 minutes by city loop bus "Kururi" from JR Tottori Station
久松公園.access_car: Approximately 10 minutes by car from Tottori IC on the Tottori Expressway
久松公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of cherry blossoms at the historic site of Tottori Castle.
久松公園.desc:
Kusumo Park is built on the former site of Tottori Castle, established by the Ikeda clan during the Edo period, and is recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Its standout feature is the unique "k巻石垣" medieval defensive structure. Every year in early April, around 1,000 Somei Yoshino cherry trees burst into bloom, surrounding the majestic ancient castle walls and the surrounding hills.

The park's core attraction lies in the classic scenery created by the "Western-style building, Jinfukaku, the sturdy stone walls, and the cherry blossoms." Colorful blooms line the gentle waterways, and when the tranquil surface of the old moat perfectly reflects the white-painted tenshu and the full cherry blossom canopy, the falling petals create a stunning scene that showcases the grandeur of this historic castle.

The castle grounds are situated on natural hills, and reaching the ruins of the tenshu requires climbing a steep set of ancient stone steps, so wearing non-slip athletic shoes is recommended. Meanwhile, the Jinfukaku and pathways below are flat and accessible. Visiting in the early morning on weekdays offers the least crowds, making it the perfect time for capturing stunning reflections of this historic site.

湊山公園.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from JR Yonago Station
湊山公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Yonago IC on the Yonago Expressway
湊山公園.tagline: Discover the serene beauty of Minato Park, where cherry blossoms meet the sunset over the tranquil Chūhai Sea.
湊山公園.desc:
Minato Park is nestled beside the historic ruins of Yonago Castle, right next to the vast coastline of the Chūhai Sea. Every year, in early April, the park's extensive pathways and the surrounding waterfront area come alive with around 450 cherry blossom trees, particularly the Somei Yoshino variety, creating a gentle spring hue against the dramatic coastal scenery.

The park's main attraction is the breathtaking interplay of the stunning Chūhai Sea views, the sunset beach, and the pink flower walls. Strips of pink blossoms stretch alongside the gentle sandy beach, and as the sea breeze blows, petals dance down to the shoreline, creating a picturesque scene with the distant Shimane mountain range.

The park features completely flat terrain and well-maintained ancient pathways, making it very accessible for seniors and strollers. With a large grassy plaza, it’s most romantic at sunset when the golden rays of the setting sun cast a warm glow over the sea. Early mornings are the quietest, offering the clearest light for photography.

松江城山公園.fee: 免費（進入登閣需另付費）
松江城山公園.access_train: About 10 minutes by "Lakeside" bus from JR Matsue Station
松江城山公園.access_car: About 10 minutes by car from Matsue Chuo IC on the San'in Expressway
松江城山公園.tagline: Experience the majestic black castle and breathtaking cherry blossoms under the stars.
松江城山公園.desc:
Matsue Jozan Park is built on the historic grounds of Matsue Castle, a former stronghold of the Matsue feudal lords during the Edo period. It ranks among Japan's top cherry blossom spots and is renowned for its black wooden castle tower, one of the twelve remaining original keep structures in the country. Each year in early April, hundreds of Somei Yoshino cherry trees burst into full bloom, creating a magnificent spectacle.

The park's core feature is the "National Treasure Black Lacquered Keep, Matsue's Moat, and the classical cherry blossom scenery." Vibrant blossoms bloom alongside the gentle waterways, and when the calm surface of the old moat perfectly reflects the towering keep, the rugged granite stones, and the expansive cherry tree forest, it showcases the ultimate in Japanese aesthetic beauty.

The castle grounds are situated on natural hills, and reaching the keep requires climbing a steep set of ancient stone steps, so wearing non-slip athletic shoes is recommended. You can also enjoy cherry blossoms from the water aboard the famous "Horikawa Sightseeing Boat." Visiting early on weekdays is the best way to avoid crowds and capture the perfect, serene historical site.

斐伊川堤櫻並木.hours: 24 小時開放（夜間有點燈活動）
斐伊川堤櫻並木.address: 島根県雲南市木次町木次
斐伊川堤櫻並木.access_train: About a 1-minute walk from JR Kikisacho Station on the Kikisacho Line
斐伊川堤櫻並木.access_car: About a 5-minute drive from the Matsue Expressway, Mikado Kikisacho IC
斐伊川堤櫻並木.tagline: Experience a breathtaking 2-kilometer cherry blossom corridor in the heart of Izumo.
斐伊川堤櫻並木.desc:
The Hiigawa River Cherry Blossom Avenue stretches along both banks of the Hiigawa River in Kikisacho, boasting one of the finest wooden cherry blossom viewing paths in the Chugoku region, recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Every year in early April, the 2-kilometer-long straight embankment road on both sides bursts into bloom with around 800 magnificent ancient Somei Yoshino cherry trees.

The most striking visual appeal lies in the "endless, double-row cherry tree tunnel of vibrant pink." Stroll leisurely along the flat riverside promenade, where the lush branches intertwine overhead against the backdrop of an expansive blue sky, perfectly complemented by the verdant grass underfoot, creating a stunning visual impact.

The riverside pathway is completely flat and well-maintained, making it very friendly for strollers and joggers, and it is open to the public free of charge all day. During the cherry blossom festival, countless vintage lanterns illuminate the area at night. For those looking to capture the pristine beauty of the tunnel without tourists, experienced photographers strongly recommend visiting on weekday mornings.

玉造溫泉.hours: 24 小時開放（夜晚有點燈映照溪水）
玉造溫泉.address: 島根県松江市玉湯町玉造
玉造溫泉.access_train: About a 15-minute walk or a bus ride from JR '玉造温泉駅'.
玉造溫泉.access_car: Approximately a 5-minute drive from the '山陰自動車道' 玉造 IC.
玉造溫泉.tagline: Stroll through the historic 玉造温泉, soaking in foot baths and a tunnel of 400 cherry blossoms.
玉造溫泉.desc:
玉造温泉 is one of Japan's top hot springs, a historic gem in Izumo. Every year in early April, the banks of the 玉湯川, which winds through the heart of the hot spring town, burst into bloom with around 400 cherry blossom trees. Their branches stretch over the river, creating a stunning pink canopy that envelops the area.

The most iconic view features a dialogue between the retro wooden bridge, the steam from the hot springs, and the vibrant pink floral wall. Colorful clusters of blossoms surround ancient stone lanterns, and when the dreamy night lights illuminate the scene, the swirling white steam dances among the pink foliage, reflecting a serene Eastern fantasy.

The riverside promenade is completely flat and well-paved, making it very friendly for strollers and seniors. Along the way, you'll find free foot baths, perfect for a day trip itinerary. We highly recommend visiting in the early morning when the mist creates an ethereal atmosphere along the riverside.

津山城.count: approximately 1,000 trees (a top cherry blossom destination in Western Japan)
津山城.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Tsuyama Station
津山城.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Tsuyama IC on the Chugoku Expressway
津山城.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of cherry blossoms at this historic castle site!
津山城.desc:
Tsuyama Castle (Tsuyama Park) is built on the former site of the Tsuyama domain lord's castle from the Edo period. It is listed among Japan's top 100 cherry blossom spots and is renowned for its stunning sea of cherry blossoms. Every year, in early April, around 1,000 Somei Yoshino cherry trees bloom spectacularly, enveloping the historic site in a breathtaking display of pink.

The most striking visual experience comes from the "restored wooden Bichu turret, the imposing stone walls, and the three-dimensional pink floral carpet." Standing at the majestic honmaru stone walls and looking up, the cascading clusters of blossoms spread wildly across the dramatic cliffs, contrasting beautifully with the white-walled castle towers, creating a sense of vastness and grandeur.

The castle's terrain is situated on natural hills, and reaching the honmaru requires climbing a steep set of ancient stone steps. It is highly recommended to wear non-slip athletic shoes when visiting for cherry blossom viewing. During the cherry blossom festival, enchanting night illuminations are held. For the best chance to capture the perfect reflection of this historic castle, visit early on weekday mornings.

後樂園.address: 岡山県岡山市北区後楽園1-5
後樂園.access_train: A 25-minute walk from JR Okayama Station or take a direct bus.
後樂園.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from Okayama IC on the Sanyo Expressway.
後樂園.tagline: Explore a timeless Edo-period garden, where cherry blossoms and Okayama Castle create a stunning classical reflection.
後樂園.desc:
Kōraku-en is a representative daimyo garden from the Edo period, meticulously crafted by Okayama's lord Ikeda Tsunamasa, and is celebrated as one of Japan's three great gardens. Each year in early April, the "Sakura Grove" on the eastern side of the garden and the hundreds of Somei Yoshino cherry trees surrounding the outer moat reach their peak bloom, adorning this historical site with classical elegance.

The visual highlight is the "view from the summit of Yuishin Mountain, overlooking the cherry grove, the clear pond, and the striking Okayama Castle." Vibrant blossoms bloom alongside gentle waterways, and when the tranquil surface of "Sawa no Ike" perfectly reflects the winding stream, thatched tea houses, and the black-gold exterior of "Ujo" castle tower, it showcases the refined beauty of Japanese aesthetics.

The garden's terrain is mostly flat, with well-maintained stone pathways and grassy areas, making it very wheelchair and stroller friendly. Especially during the night illumination period, the dreamlike quality of the water mirror becomes even more ethereal. Arriving early on weekdays allows you to capture the serene beauty of this classical garden at its best.

醍醐櫻.count: 1 tree (over 1,000 years old, selected as one of Japan's new famous trees)
醍醐櫻.fee: 免費（需支付維護協力金）
醍醐櫻.access_train: About 30 minutes by taxi from JR Mimasaka-Ochiai Station
醍醐櫻.access_car: About 30 minutes from the Ochiai IC on the Chugoku Expressway (very congested during bloom season)
醍醐櫻.tagline: Experience the awe of Japan's ancient cherry blossom, a thousand-year-old giant that captivates with its stunning mountain backdrop.
醍醐櫻.desc:
Daigo Zakura is a legendary cherry tree, believed to be over a thousand years old, named after Emperor Go-Daigo who once marveled at its beauty. This majestic Edo-period cherry tree is listed among Japan's top hundred giant trees. Each year, it reaches its peak bloom in early April, transforming into a breathtaking spectacle with its towering height of 18 meters and a width of 20 meters.

The most striking visual experience comes from the harmonious dialogue between the vast mountain ranges, expansive fields, and the ancient tree trunk. Standing proudly in the center of a spacious grassy area, the solitary giant is surrounded by lush, pale pink blossoms that resemble clouds, showcasing the ultimate beauty of life and the passage of time.

To protect the delicate roots of this ancient tree, a well-constructed fence surrounds the area. Due to its remote location and narrow scenic roads, traffic congestion can be severe during peak bloom season. The highland area tends to be slightly cool, so be sure to bring a warm jacket for your visit. The best lighting for photography is during the clear morning hours.

宮島.count: approximately 1,900 trees (a breathtaking view with the vermilion torii gate)
宮島.address: 広島県廿日市市宮島町
宮島.access_train: About a 10-minute ferry ride from JR Miyajimaguchi Station
宮島.access_car: Park at the Miyajimaguchi parking lot and take the ferry.
宮島.tagline: Step into the sacred island of Miyajima, where vibrant cherry blossoms and stunning landscapes await.
宮島.desc:
Miyajima Momijidani Park and the nearby Itsukushima Shrine are a perfect blend of natural beauty and sacred heritage, recognized as a top UNESCO World Heritage site. From late March to early April, the park and the area around the five-story pagoda burst into bloom with approximately 1,900 ancient Somei Yoshino and Oyamazakura cherry trees, showcasing nine different varieties.

The most iconic view features the vermilion wooden Momiji Bridge, the pagoda, and a sea of cherry blossoms in perfect harmony. Colorful blossoms bloom along the gentle waterfront, and as the morning light filters through the treetops, casting reflections on the tranquil water, it creates a serene scene alongside wandering deer, embodying the rich historical and Zen spirit of the area.

The park's walking paths are designed to blend with the natural terrain, making the stroll from the Miyajima ferry terminal shaded and refreshing—ideal for a leisurely day trip. Due to the popularity of this spot, it’s highly recommended to take the first ferry on weekday mornings to capture the tranquil beauty of the Momiji Bridge without the crowds.

千光寺公園.count: Approximately 1,500 trees (with views of the Seto Inland Sea and cherry blossoms)
千光寺公園.address: 広島県尾道市西土堂町19-1
千光寺公園.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from JR Onomichi Station to the ropeway station
千光寺公園.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from Onomichi IC on the Sanyo Expressway
千光寺公園.tagline: Ride the Senkoji Ropeway to the summit for breathtaking views of cherry blossoms and the Seto Inland Sea.
千光寺公園.desc:
Senkoji Park is perched on a solitary hill in the historic city of Onomichi, recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Every year in early April, 1,500 cherry trees of the Somei Yoshino variety burst into bloom along the steep slopes, transforming the natural hillside into a vibrant tapestry of colors.

The most stunning sight here is the breathtaking view of the "unstoppable Onomichi waterway, the New Onomichi Bridge, and the three-dimensional pink flower carpet" all in one frame. Take the Senkoji Ropeway to ascend slowly, feeling the refreshing sea breeze as you gaze down at the colorful carpet stretching endlessly along the valley below.

While the ropeway takes you directly to the top observation deck, the terrain is uneven with many ancient stone steps, so wearing good non-slip athletic shoes is highly recommended for cherry blossom photography. Don't miss the famous literary path and Senkoji Temple while you're here. Visiting on a weekday morning offers the best visibility and vibrant colors for your photos.

平和紀念公園.address: 広島県広島市中区中島町1-2
平和紀念公園.access_train: Take the tram from JR Hiroshima Station to "Genbaku Dome Mae"
平和紀念公園.access_car: Parking in the city center is inconvenient; public transport is recommended.
平和紀念公園.tagline: Experience the poignant beauty where history and nature intertwine beneath blooming cherry blossoms.
平和紀念公園.desc:
Heiwa Kinen Koen is a world-class historical memorial park designed by Kenzo Tange, located at the confluence of the Motoyasu River and Ota River. Every year in early April, the tranquil paths stretching for several kilometers along both banks of the river burst into a sea of cherry blossoms, with around 300 Somei Yoshino trees in full bloom.

The most striking modern composition features the World Cultural Heritage Atomic Bomb Dome, the Flame of Peace, and a wall of pink blossoms harmoniously framed together. As you stroll along the smooth wooden promenade by the water, the lush branches intertwine overhead, and the calm water beautifully reflects the remnants of historical tragedy alongside the vibrant cherry blossoms, creating a spacious atmosphere.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained walking paths that are very friendly for seniors and strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, you can take a scenic open-air boat tour. The surrounding area is highly functional, making it perfect for independent travelers to explore at their own pace. Visiting early in the morning on weekdays offers the least crowds, allowing you to capture the solemn and pristine ambiance in the air.

上野公園.hours: 24 小時開放（賞櫻期間有夜間點燈）
上野公園.address: 広島県庄原市三日市町
上野公園.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Bizen-Shobara Station
上野公園.access_car: About a 5-minute drive from the Chugoku Expressway Shobara IC
上野公園.tagline: Discover the breathtaking cherry blossoms at Ueno Park, where vibrant blooms reflect on the serene waters of Ueno Pond.
上野公園.desc:
Ueno Park is a classic Japanese strolling garden built around the expansive Ueno Pond, recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Its history dates back to the Edo period when it was established by the lord of the southern Bizen province. Every year in early April, the six hundred Yoshino cherry trees surrounding the lakeside reach their peak bloom, painting the landscape in vibrant colors.

The visual highlight is the "ultimate classical waterside pond, vermilion gourd island, and the mirrored cherry blossom scenery." Pink flower walls bloom along the gentle wooden pathways by the water, and when the tranquil, mirror-like surface perfectly reflects the vintage wooden drum bridge and the full bloom of cherry trees, the illuminated snow caves at night create an expansive visual experience.

The park's flat terrain and well-maintained paths make it very friendly for elderly visitors and strollers. It also features the famous Shobara City Library, making it perfect for a leisurely morning stroll. Early morning is the best time to capture the lake's calmness, as sunlight easily reveals the ethereal Zen-like reflections in the air.

錦帶橋.count: about 1,500 trees (classic composition with Nishiki Bridge and Iwakuni Castle)
錦帶橋.address: 山口県岩国市岩国1丁目
錦帶橋.access_train: About 20 minutes by bus from JR Iwakuni Station
錦帶橋.access_car: About 10 minutes from Iwakuni IC on the Sanyo Expressway
錦帶橋.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of a historic wooden bridge amidst a sea of cherry blossoms.
錦帶橋.desc:
Nishiki Bridge is a world-class, unique wooden five-arch bridge spanning the clear waters of Iwakuni River, recognized as one of Japan's three great bridges. From late March to early April each year, the nearby Yoshikō Park and the opposite Momijidani Park come alive with around three thousand ancient Somei Yoshino cherry trees, creating a stunning backdrop for this historic bridge.
