The core feature is the "original five-arch wooden bridge, framed by the classic scenery of the mountains and cherry blossoms." Colorful blooms line the gentle riverbank, and as the calm waters of Iwakuni River perfectly reflect the elegant lines of the bridge and the vibrant foliage, you can also catch a distant view of Iwakuni Castle atop the mountain, showcasing the ultimate in Japanese aesthetic beauty.

The parks on both sides of the river are completely flat, and the ancient pathways have been well maintained, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. The bridge itself features a special wooden staircase structure, so be mindful of your steps while walking across. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, making it the best time to capture the perfect mirror-like scenery without anyone around.

戰場之原公園.count: approximately 200 trees (popular cherry blossom spot in Shimonoseki)
戰場之原公園.address: 山口県下関市長府川端2丁目
戰場之原公園.access_train: About 20 minutes by bus from JR Shimonoseki Station to "Shimonoseki City Art Museum"
戰場之原公園.access_car: About 15 minutes drive from "Chugoku Expressway" Shimonoseki IC
戰場之原公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of cherry blossoms and tulip carpets at Shimonoseki's historic oasis.
戰場之原公園.desc:
戦場之原公園 (Janjou no Hara Park) is perched on a solitary hill in Koda Town, Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture, making it the premier cherry blossom viewing spot in the area. From late March to early April, the park bursts into bloom with hundreds of Somei Yoshino and Yamazakura cherry trees, beautifully set against the natural hilly landscape.

The park's most iconic view features the "majestic Shiroishi Chūrei Tower, the Shimonoseki skyline, and a stunning pink floral wall" all in one frame. Stroll along the smooth stone pathways, where vibrant pink blossoms intertwine with the sky. In the morning light, the contrast between the pure white and golden tulip fields at your feet creates a breathtaking scene.

The park's flat and spacious terrain includes well-maintained accessible paths and children's play areas, making it very friendly for elderly visitors and strollers. From the observation deck at the top of the hill, you can take in the entire sea of cherry blossoms, and the expansive grounds are perfect for picnics. Visiting on weekday mornings offers the least crowds, allowing you to capture the purest colors of nature's landscape.

眉山公園.hours: 24 小時開放（眉山纜車 09:00～17:30，視季節延長）
眉山公園.fee: 免費（纜車大人往返 1,030 円）
眉山公園.access_train: A 10-minute walk from JR Tokushima Station to the cable car station, then a 6-minute ride to the summit.
眉山公園.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from Tokushima Expressway's Tokushima IC.
眉山公園.tagline: Ride the Mount Bizan Ropeway to the sky and gaze over the Yoshino River, Tokushima Plain, and a sea of cherry blossoms.
眉山公園.desc:
Mount Bizan Park, perched atop the iconic Mount Bizan in Tokushima City, is a vast landscape park offering breathtaking views of the Azan mountain range and the Seto Inland Sea. Each year, in early April, the park's famous high slopes and walking paths burst into bloom with around 1,500 Somei Yoshino and mountain cherry trees in full splendor.

The most stunning sight here is the "360-degree panoramic view of mountains and sea, featuring the Tokushima skyline and a three-dimensional pink floral carpet." Standing at the cutting-edge radio tower observatory, the tranquil Yoshino River glistens like a mirror, contrasting beautifully with the colorful carpet of flowers at your feet, creating a magnificent scene.

While you can take the "Mount Bizan Aerial Cableway" directly to the summit, be prepared for a few natural stone steps between the observatories, so wearing comfortable walking shoes is recommended. At the top, you'll find a cozy observation café. The early morning air is the clearest, making it the best time to capture stunning views of the sea and the vibrant floral display.

德島中央公園.address: 徳島県徳島市徳島町城内
德島中央公園.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Tokushima Station
德島中央公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Tokushima IC on the Tokushima Expressway
德島中央公園.tagline: Explore the historic site of Tokushima Castle, surrounded by 250 stunning Yoshino cherry blossoms.
德島中央公園.desc:
Tokushima Chuo Park is built on the historic grounds of Tokushima Castle, once home to the Tokushima feudal lords, and is recognized as one of Japan's Historical Parks. Each year, in early April, around 250 cherry trees bloom vibrantly along the majestic old castle walls and the serene moat, adding a splash of color to this ancient site.

The park's highlight is the restored wooden Waki-mon gate, the nationally designated scenic garden of the former Tokushima Castle, and the classical views of cherry blossoms. The colorful blooms flourish beside gentle waterways, and when the calm water perfectly reflects the white-walled castle tower and the full cherry blossom canopy, it creates an expansive visual experience that embodies elegant Japanese aesthetics.

The park's terrain is completely flat, and the pathways along the moat are well-maintained, making it very accessible for seniors and those with strollers. The well-preserved Hachisuka family garden features a unique Muromachi period dry landscape design, which is highly recommended to visit. Early mornings on weekdays are the least crowded, making it the perfect time for capturing stunning reflections.

妙見山公園.address: 徳島県鳴門市撫養町林崎北代
妙見山公園.access_car: Approximately 10 minutes by car from the Naruto IC on the Kobe-Awaji-Naruto Expressway
妙見山公園.tagline: Experience breathtaking views of the Naruto Strait and 500 cherry blossoms at Myokenzan Park.
妙見山公園.desc:
Myokenzan Park, built on the historic site of the Muromachi period castle, Narujo (Okazaki Castle), is a green oasis that offers stunning views of the Naruto Inland Sea. Each year in early April, around the reconstructed wooden donjon, Tori no Sato, 500 cherry trees of the Somei Yoshino variety bloom in vibrant colors, painting the landscape with the hues of early spring.

The visual highlight is the harmonious interplay between the retro-style donjon, the horizon of the Ohnaruto Bridge, and the colorful floral walls. Lines of pink blossoms stretch alongside the gentle waterways, and when the calm water perfectly reflects the white-walled castle tower and the vibrant foliage, the scene feels expansive and refreshing, truly magnificent.

The park's walking paths are well-maintained, making it friendly for seniors, except for some sloped stone steps near the donjon. Myoken Shrine is also located within the park, making it ideal for travelers exploring at their own pace. Set aside an hour for a leisurely stroll, especially in the early morning when the crowds are minimal, perfect for capturing stunning outdoor shots.

川井峠.address: 徳島県美馬郡つるぎ町一宇
川井峠.access_train: Take a bus from JR 'Sedako Station' for about 50 minutes to 'Kawai'.
川井峠.access_car: Approximately a 40-minute drive from Tokushima Expressway 'Mima IC'.
川井峠.tagline: Discover the hidden beauty of Kawai Pass, where ancient cherry blossoms bloom in breathtaking splendor.
川井峠.desc:
Kawai Pass, nestled at an elevation of 700 meters in the heart of the Shikoku Mountains, is a secret cherry blossom haven lovingly cultivated by local residents over many years. Every year, from mid-April, the ancient weeping cherry trees along the winding National Route 438 burst into full bloom, with up to 20 majestic trees showcasing their beauty simultaneously.

The core visual highlight is the classic juxtaposition of an old mountain village, steep valleys, and a cascading tunnel of pink cherry blossoms. Colorful blooms cascade from the heights like a waterfall, and during peak bloom, the vibrant pinks contrast beautifully with the deep blue of the surrounding mountains, transforming the entire mountain road into a pink paradise filled with a sense of timelessness.

This serene spot, far from the hustle and bustle of urban life, is a favorite among local travelers seeking a peaceful escape. Along the road, there are several well-equipped observation points. The high-altitude area can feel cool, so be sure to bring a warm jacket for your visit. Aim for a clear morning to capture the mountains' contours and the vivid details of the colorful foliage in the best light.

西部公園.address: 徳島県徳島市不動東町4丁目
西部公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Tokushima Station for about 20 minutes to "Nishiibu Koen Mae."
西部公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from the Awa Route of the Tokushima Expressway at "Aizumi IC."
西部公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking view of the Seto Inland Sea and a vibrant carpet of 3,000 Yoshino cherry blossoms.
西部公園.desc:
Nishiibu Park is nestled on the gentle slopes of the northern side of Ishikiyo Mountain, making it one of Kagawa Prefecture's premier cherry blossom viewing spots. Every year in early April, the park bursts into bloom with up to 3,000 Somei Yoshino cherry trees, transforming the lush green hills into a sea of pink.

The visual highlight is the stunning "three-dimensional flower wall view created by the hilly terrain and the picturesque backdrop of the Seto Ohashi Bridge." Stroll along the well-maintained winding paths, where vibrant blossoms intertwine with the surrounding native broadleaf forest. At the highest viewpoint, you can take in the entire floral carpet while enjoying a panoramic view of Takamatsu Port.

Since the main viewing areas are concentrated on the sloping hills, it's highly recommended to wear comfortable walking shoes for cherry blossom photography. The park's spacious interior features enchanting night illuminations during the cherry blossom festival. Visiting early on weekdays will ensure fewer crowds, offering the best visibility and a refreshing natural atmosphere.

栗林公園.hours: 05:30～18:30（視季節變動，花季夜間有燈光秀）
栗林公園.access_train: About a 3-minute walk from JR Kuritama Park North Entrance Station
栗林公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Takamatsu Chuo IC on the Takamatsu Expressway
栗林公園.tagline: Experience the serene beauty of Kuritama Park, where cherry blossoms and tranquil reflections await.
栗林公園.desc:
Kuritama Park is a stunning Edo-period landscape garden, meticulously crafted over a century by generations of the Sanuki Takamatsu lords. It is revered as a "Special Place of Scenic Beauty" in Japan. Each year in early April, the park bursts into bloom with over 300 ancient Somei Yoshino and Edo-higan cherry trees, showcasing a classic elegance.

The park's signature aesthetic features the exquisite wooden Suketsuki Pavilion, the arched bridge over the southern lake, and the mirrored reflections of cherry blossoms. From the park's prime viewpoint, Hiraikyo, you can gaze down at the tranquil, mirror-like water that perfectly reflects the winding classical embankment, lush 300-year-old pine trees, and a vibrant pink floral wall, creating a spacious and harmonious atmosphere.

The garden's terrain is mostly flat, with well-maintained gravel paths and stone walkways, making it very accessible for seniors and strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, the park is illuminated with enchanting lights at night. Arriving early on weekdays allows you to capture the pristine beauty and perfect reflections in this sacred space.

紫雲出山.hours: 09:00～17:00（花季期間需事先預約停車位）
紫雲出山.fee: 免費（花季期間環境保全協力金約 1,000 円/車）
紫雲出山.address: 香川県三豊市詫間町大浜乙451-1
紫雲出山.access_train: Take the JR train to Takuma Station, then a community bus to "Shiunzan Trailhead" and walk for about 45 minutes.
紫雲出山.access_car: Approximately 45 minutes by car from the Takamatsu Expressway "Mitoyo Torisaka IC."
紫雲出山.tagline: Ascend to a sky-high secret and witness the breathtaking beauty of Seto Inland Sea and a sea of cherry blossoms.
紫雲出山.desc:
Shiunzan, standing at 352 meters, is a world-renowned cherry blossom viewing paradise extending into the Seto Inland Sea. From early to mid-April, the summit bursts into bloom with around 1,000 cherry trees, including Somei Yoshino, Shikizakura, and Yamazakura, transforming this mountain sanctuary into a dreamlike spectacle.

The most stunning visual experience is the "unmatched beauty of the Seto Inland Sea's many islands, lush green mountains, and a blanket of pink flowers in perfect harmony." Stroll along the gentle wooden pathways, where a dense canopy of cherry blossoms arches overhead, and the scattered islands on the azure sea resemble miniature landscapes. The breathtaking view of the sea and sky merging with the pink floral wall is truly unparalleled.

To maintain environmental quality and traffic safety during cherry blossom season, strict reservations are required for private vehicles at the summit, and visitors must transfer to a shuttle bus. The main observation trail is well-maintained, making for an easy and comfortable walk. It is highly recommended to hike on a clear morning for the best visibility and to capture the ultimate stunning scenery that will amaze your followers.

金刀比羅宮.hours: 24 小時開放（御本宮參拜至 17:00）
金刀比羅宮.address: 香川県仲多度郡琴平町892-1
金刀比羅宮.access_train: 15-minute walk from JR Kotohira Station to the approach entrance
金刀比羅宮.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from Zentsuji IC
金刀比羅宮.tagline: Climb the sacred steps of Kongoji, where history meets 3,000 Yoshino cherry blossoms.
金刀比羅宮.desc:
Kongōji is a revered shrine nestled on the slopes of Mount Shōtō, known throughout Japan as a must-visit pilgrimage site at least once in a lifetime. From late March to early April, thousands of ancient trees and Yoshino cherry blossoms bloom along the long approach and within the expansive sacred gardens, adorning this divine realm.

The core feature here is the dialogue between the majestic vermillion Asahi Shrine, rustic stone lanterns, and the cherry blossom tunnel. Colorful clusters of blossoms embrace the layered ancient wooden halls, and as the morning light filters down onto the stone steps and mossy ground, the interplay of movement and stillness beautifully captures the sacred Zen essence unique to this ancient temple.

To reach the main shrine, visitors must ascend a steep staircase of 785 steps, totaling 1,368 steps to the inner shrine. It is highly recommended to wear comfortable running shoes for cherry blossom viewing and to stay hydrated at shaded spots along the way. Early morning visits at 6 AM, right when the gates open, offer the least crowds, making it the perfect time for capturing the ethereal beauty of this sacred site.

丸龜城.fee: 園內免費（天守閣大人 200 円）
丸龜城.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes by car from Sakaide IC or Zentsuji IC
丸龜城.tagline: Experience the majestic cherry blossoms against the backdrop of Japan's highest stone walls.
丸龜城.desc:
Marugame Castle, built during the Keicho era, is one of Japan's top 100 castles, renowned for its impressive stone walls that soar up to 60 meters high. Every year in early April, around 600 stunning Somei Yoshino cherry trees bloom vibrantly, surrounding the historic castle grounds.

The highlight here is the classic view of the wooden keep, the rugged stone walls, and the cherry blossoms. The colorful blooms flourish alongside the tranquil waterways, and when the calm moat reflects the towering white-walled keep and the full canopy of cherry blossoms, the scene opens up, showcasing the grandeur of this historic site.

The castle is situated on natural hills, and reaching the keep requires climbing the steep ancient stone steps known as “Mikaeri-zaka.” It’s recommended to wear non-slip athletic shoes. During the cherry blossom festival, a magical illumination event takes place at night, and visiting early in the morning on weekdays offers the least crowds, making it the perfect time for capturing the beauty of this historic castle in its mirror-like reflection.

琴彈公園.address: 香川県観音寺市有明町
琴彈公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR 'Kannonji Station'
琴彈公園.access_car: Approximately a 12-minute drive from Ōnohara IC
琴彈公園.tagline: Discover a coastal oasis of beauty, featuring the legendary 'Kanei Tōbō' sand art and a thousand Yoshino cherries.
琴彈公園.desc:
琴彈公園 is a nationally designated scenic spot, nestled next to the pristine white sands and green pines of the Ariake coast. Each year in early April, the park, surrounded by the majestic琴彈山 and its tranquil ponds, bursts into color with over 50,000 ancient pines and 1,000 cherry blossoms, creating a vibrant tapestry of hues.

The park's most striking feature is undoubtedly the massive sand art of 'Kanei Tōbō.' From the mountaintop observation deck, you can gaze down at the enormous coin-shaped sand sculpture, beautifully complemented by the blooming pink flower balls at your feet, all set against the endless deep blue of the Seto Inland Sea. This stunning view combines a sense of grand historical significance with the breathtaking aesthetics of nature.

The park's gently rolling terrain and well-maintained pathways make it very accessible for seniors and those with strollers. There are ample grassy areas and wooden walkways by the water. Being situated on a prominent coastal elevation, the sea breeze is quite strong year-round. For the best visibility, visit on a clear morning for the most dynamic lighting for your photos.

松山城.fee: 免費（天守閣大人 520 円；纜車往返 520 円）
松山城.access_train: A 5-minute walk from Daidaido Station on the Iyotetsu tram line to the ropeway station.
松山城.access_car: Approximately a 20-minute drive from Matsuyama IC.
松山城.tagline: Experience breathtaking cherry blossoms at Matsuyama Castle, a historical gem overlooking a sea of pink blooms.
松山城.desc:
Matsuyama Castle, built during the Edo period, is one of Japan's twelve existing castles and is recognized as a top cherry blossom spot. Every year in early April, around 200 stunning Somei Yoshino cherry trees burst into bloom, surrounding the ancient lacquered walls against the steep stone walls of Katsuyama.

The core feature here is the classic view of the grand castle, sturdy stone walls, and cherry blossoms. The vibrant blooms flourish alongside the gentle waterways, and when the calm waters of the old moat perfectly reflect the white walls and tiled roofs of the castle, you can even gaze out towards the distant Dogo Plain, showcasing the grandeur of this historic city.

The castle is situated at an elevation of 132 meters on Katsuyama, easily accessible via a comfortable sightseeing gondola or chairlift. The spacious Honmaru area is flat and well-maintained, making it very friendly for elderly visitors. For the best experience, visit early in the morning on weekdays when crowds are minimal, perfect for capturing stunning photos of this historic landmark in all its mirrored glory.

道後公園.address: 愛媛県松山市道後公園
道後公園.access_train: 1-minute walk from Dogo Park Station on the Iyotetsu tram
道後公園.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes by car from Matsuyama Station
道後公園.tagline: Discover Japan's oldest hot spring town, where vintage earthworks meet the beauty of cherry blossoms.
道後公園.desc:
Dogo Park is built on the ruins of the medieval Yuzuki Castle, once guarded by the Kono clan, and is adjacent to the world-renowned Dogo Onsen. From late March to early April, over 300 cherry blossoms, including Somei Yoshino and Oshima cherry trees, burst into bloom around the double moat and earthen walls, adding vibrant colors to this historic site.

The park's core feature is the "restored wooden samurai residence, inner moat, and classical cherry blossom scenery." Pink flower walls stretch along the gentle waterways, and when the calm water perfectly reflects the historic white walls and full cherry blossom canopy, it creates an expansive view that showcases elegant Japanese aesthetics.

The park's terrain is completely flat, and the ancient pathways have been fully renovated, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. There is an observation deck for a panoramic view of Matsuyama City, perfect for travelers looking to enjoy a day trip of cherry blossom viewing and hot springs. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, ideal for capturing pristine reflections.

開山公園.address: 愛媛県今治市伯方町伊方
開山公園.access_train: Take an express bus from Imabari Station to 'Hakata Island BS', then transfer to a minibus to 'Kaisan Trailhead'
開山公園.access_car: About a 10-minute drive from the Nishiseto Expressway 'Hakata Island IC'
開山公園.tagline: Experience breathtaking views of bridges and cherry blossoms atop Hakata Island!
開山公園.desc:
Kaisan Park, perched on the summit of Hakata Island along the Shimanami Kaido, is a vast natural landscape park offering unbeatable views of the Seto Inland Sea. Each year, in early April, the park bursts into color with around 1,000 Yoshino cherry trees, transforming the entire mountaintop into a vibrant tapestry of pink and purple.

The most stunning sight here is the dramatic scene of three massive bridges, the sea blending with the sky, and a three-dimensional pink floral carpet all in one frame. From the cutting-edge wooden observation deck or the high slide, you can gaze down at the colorful blossoms contrasting with the majestic cable-stayed bridges in the distance, creating a breathtaking sense of space and grandeur.

While you can drive directly to the summit, some paths leading to the main observation area include a few natural gravel trails, so wearing comfortable athletic shoes is highly recommended for cherry blossom viewing. The elevated terrain offers little shelter and can be quite windy, so be sure to bring a warm jacket. The best visibility is during clear mornings, perfect for photography.

積善山.address: 愛媛県越智郡上島町岩城
積善山.access_train: Take a ferry from Imabari Port to "Ikijima Port."
積善山.access_car: It's recommended to rent a car or take the shuttle bus on the island (mountain road restrictions during cherry blossom season).
積善山.tagline: Experience the breathtaking pink staircase of 3,000 cherry blossoms on Ikijima's sacred mountain.
積善山.desc:
Sekizenzan is located in the heart of Ikijima, in the Seto Inland Sea, renowned for its stunning corridor of 3,000 cherry trees known as "Ikijima Sanzenbonzakura." Every year, in early April, the scenic hiking trail stretches from the water oasis at the foot of the mountain to the summit at 370 meters, where countless Somei Yoshino and mountain cherry blossoms burst into bloom.

The most striking aspect of this natural beauty lies in the "vibrant, colorful floral ribbons that wind along the mountain path, framed by breathtaking sea views." Whether you're hiking or simply stopping by the roadside, you'll be surrounded by vivid pink flower walls. At the 360-degree observation deck at the summit, the entire mountain transforms into a pink Fuji, delivering a powerful visual impact.

Reaching the summit requires climbing a genuine mountain trail with natural gravel slopes, so it's highly recommended to wear professional running shoes and a windproof, warm jacket. During the cherry blossom season, shuttle buses operate on the island. Aim for a clear, windless morning to capture the purest reflections of the sky and the magnificent floral scenery.

岩屋寺.count: about 10 trees (noted for their large, ancient trunks)
岩屋寺.address: 愛媛県上浮穴郡久万高原町七鳥1173
岩屋寺.access_train: Take a bus from JR Matsuyama Station to 'Kuwan Chugakumae', then transfer to a bus to 'Iwayaji'
岩屋寺.access_car: Approximately 1 hour drive from Matsuyama City
岩屋寺.tagline: Discover the sacred beauty of Iwayaji Temple, where ancient cherry blossoms bloom against stunning cliffs.
岩屋寺.desc:
Iwayaji is a historic temple nestled among steep, rugged cliffs, founded by the revered Hōka Sennin. Every year in early April, the pristine forest surrounding the solemn wooden main hall and narrow caves bursts into bloom with ancient mountain cherry trees, adding vibrant colors to this sacred realm in early spring.

The most iconic view here is the dialogue between the massive gray rocky cliffs, steep steps, and the colorful blossoms. Flower clusters embrace the ancient moss and towering trees at your feet, and when the afternoon mist drifts through the mountains, the sea of flowers perfectly embodies the tranquil historical Zen essence unique to this temple.

Reaching the main hall requires a steep climb along a rocky path and historical stone steps that can take about thirty minutes. It’s advisable to wear high-quality, slip-resistant hiking shoes for this trek. Early morning visits on weekdays offer the most ethereal atmosphere, making it the ideal time for capturing the ancient cultural scenery.

鏡野公園.address: 高知県香美市土佐山田町宮ノ口
鏡野公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Tosa-Yamada Station to 'Kouka Daimae,' then walk about 2 minutes.
鏡野公園.access_car: Approximately a 25-minute drive from Nankoku IC.
鏡野公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking 200-meter tunnel of cherry blossoms at this top cherry blossom spot in Japan.
鏡野公園.desc:
Kagino Park, located next to Kochi University of Technology, is a beautifully curated landscape park that blends artistic greenery with nature, recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From late March to early April, the park reaches its peak bloom, with over 600 cherry trees lining both sides of the central path that stretches 200 meters.

The park's unique feature is the dynamic contrast of the straight avenue, grassy fields, and pink tunnel. As you stroll along the smooth stone pathways, the lush blossoms overhead weave together in a breathtaking display, creating the illusion of walking through an endless colorful tunnel. During the falling cherry blossom season, the ground is carpeted in pink petals, offering a stunning visual impact.

The park's terrain is completely flat, and the well-maintained old pathways are very friendly for seniors and strollers. There is a large grassy square where vendors gather during the cherry blossom festival, making it an ideal spot for a leisurely family picnic. Visiting in the early morning on weekdays ensures fewer crowds, perfect for capturing clean shots of the historical and cultural scenery.

高知城.fee: 免費（天守閣大人 420 円）
高知城.access_train: 5-minute walk from Kochi Castle Mae Station on the tram
高知城.tagline: Experience the majestic cherry blossoms at Kochi Castle, a historic gem of Japan!
高知城.desc:
Kochi Castle, built during the Keicho era, is one of Japan's top 100 castles and is renowned for being the only castle in the country to have preserved its tenshu and the main gate structure intact, earning it the title of "existing twelve tenshu." Each year, from late March to early April, hundreds of cherry trees, particularly the Somei Yoshino variety, burst into bloom, adding vibrant colors to the ancient stone walls of this grand castle.

The main highlight is the "independent wooden tenshu, sturdy stone walls, and classical cherry blossom scenery." Colorful blossoms bloom alongside the gentle waterways, and when the tranquil surface of the old moat perfectly reflects the white walls and tiled roofs of the "Otemon" gate, the scene opens up, showcasing the grandeur of this historic castle.

The castle's terrain is situated on natural hills, and reaching the tenshu requires climbing a steep set of ancient stone steps. It is highly recommended to wear non-slip athletic shoes when coming to enjoy the cherry blossoms. The interior of the main hall is well-preserved. Allow an hour for a leisurely stroll; visiting in the early morning offers the least crowds, making it the perfect time for capturing stunning photos of this historical landmark.

牧野植物園.access_train: Take the MY bus from JR Kochi Station to 'Makino Botanical Garden Main Entrance'
牧野植物園.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from Kochi IC
牧野植物園.tagline: Step into the world of botany at Makino Botanical Garden, where giant greenhouses and native cherry blossoms await.
牧野植物園.desc:
Kochi Prefectural Makino Botanical Garden is a world-class horticultural haven dedicated to the father of Japanese botany, Tomitaro Makino. From mid-March to early April each year, the garden's natural slopes at Godaisan and the newly established Sakura Grove come alive with hundreds of locally cultivated native and Somei Yoshino cherry trees in full bloom.

The garden's core features include a cutting-edge wooden structure, a grand circular greenhouse, and the elegant interplay of vibrant flowers. Colorful blooms line the gentle gravel paths, harmonizing with the lush green canopies overhead, showcasing a seamless blend of nature and modern architecture that exudes refined modern aesthetics and a touch of wilderness.

The main walking paths within the garden are fully paved and accessible, making it stroller and elder-friendly, despite some naturally sloped areas. There is also a spacious semi-open restaurant with scenic views. Visiting on weekday mornings offers the most tranquil environment, perfect for capturing stunning landscapes and mirror-like reflections.

五台山公園.access_train: Take the MY bus from JR Kochi Station to Godaisan Observation Deck
五台山公園.access_car: Approximately a 20-minute drive from Kochi IC
五台山公園.tagline: Experience breathtaking views and vibrant cherry blossoms at this historical highland gem!
五台山公園.desc:
Godaisan Park is perched on a prominent hill in the eastern part of Kochi City, offering stunning sea views and the renowned Chikurinji Temple within a vast natural landscape park. Each year, from late March to early April, the park's highlands burst into bloom with hundreds of Somei Yoshino cherry trees and mountain cherries in full glory.

The park's main visual feature is the harmonious interplay of the expansive scenery of Urato Bay, the Kochi skyline, and a vibrant floral tapestry. Stroll along the well-maintained winding paths, where colorful blossoms contrast beautifully with the distant shimmering lake. From the highest observation deck, you can take in the breathtaking expanse of the pink floral carpet, a truly magnificent sight.

The main mountaintop area is spacious and well-developed, making it very friendly for families and seniors. You can easily connect your visit to the nearby Chikurinji Temple. Springtime on clear days offers the best visibility. Visiting early on weekdays ensures fewer crowds, allowing you to capture the most vivid and immersive floral scenes.

宿毛城跡.address: 高知県宿毛市中央2丁目
宿毛城跡.access_train: 15-minute walk from Tosa Kuroshio Railway 'Sukumo Station'
宿毛城跡.access_car: Approximately 1.5 hours by car from Shimanto-cho Chuo IC
宿毛城跡.tagline: Experience the serene beauty of cherry blossoms reflecting on Matsumaru River at the historic ruins of Sukumo Castle.
宿毛城跡.desc:
Sukumo Castle Ruins Park, built during the Sengoku period, is a historical site nestled against natural mountain landscapes, making it one of the most representative hidden cherry blossom spots in the Sukumo area. From late March to early April, around a thousand Somei Yoshino and mountain cherry trees burst into bloom along the ancient defensive earthworks and castle walls.

The park's core feature is the harmonious interplay of the old castle's dry moat, tranquil ponds, and vibrant flower walls. Rows of pink flower walls stretch along the gentle waterways, and when the calm water perfectly reflects the historic white walls and the full bloom of cherry trees, the scene feels expansive and refreshing, showcasing the unique tranquil historical Zen of the ancient castle.

To reach the main hall and the back mountain park, visitors will need to navigate some natural gravel paths and steps, so wearing comfortable, non-slip sneakers is highly recommended for cherry blossom viewing. This place is a hidden gem, relatively quiet compared to tourist hotspots. Visiting on weekday mornings offers the best light for capturing the ethereal beauty of the forest and fields.

舞鶴公園.hours: 24 小時開放（花季夜間有點燈）
舞鶴公園.address: 福岡県福岡市中央区城内1
舞鶴公園.access_train: About an 8-minute walk from Akasaka Station or Ōhori Park Station on the subway
舞鶴公園.access_car: About a 10-minute drive from downtown Fukuoka
舞鶴公園.desc:
Maihama Park is built on the historic grounds of Fukuoka Castle from the Edo period and the ancient diplomatic guesthouse, Kōrōkan. It is recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Every year, from late March to early April, around the impressive old moat and the main keep, approximately 1,000 Yoshino cherry trees burst into bloom.

The park's core feature lies in its "multi-layered pagodas, robust granite stone walls, and classical cherry blossom scenery." Vibrant clusters of flowers bloom alongside the gentle waterways, and when the calm inner moat perfectly reflects the white-walled castle towers, ancient trees hundreds of years old, and the expansive cherry blossom forest, the scene feels grand and open.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained pathways along the moat, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. During the cherry blossom festival's special night illumination, the mirror-like water reflects an ethereal beauty. Arriving early on regular days allows you to capture perfect reflections and enjoy the serene landscape without crowds.
舞鶴公園.tagline: Discover the stunning cherry blossoms at Fukuoka Castle's historic site, where nature and history unite.

小倉城.fee: 免費（天守閣大人 350 円）
小倉城.address: 福岡県北九州市小倉北区城内2-1
小倉城.access_train: A 15-minute walk from JR "Kokura Station"; a 10-minute walk from JR "Nishi-Kokura Station"
小倉城.access_car: About a 2-minute drive from the urban expressway "Otemachi IC"
小倉城.tagline: Experience the majestic cherry blossoms at Kokura Castle, a stunning blend of history and nature.
小倉城.desc:
Kokura Castle, built during the Keicho period, is one of Japan's top 100 castles, renowned for its unique "Namban-style" architecture featuring a massive, eaves-less keep that commands national attention. Each year, from late March to early April, the famous old moat and Honmaru Plaza within the castle grounds burst into bloom with around 1,000 cherry trees, including the iconic Somei Yoshino and mountain cherry blossoms.

The core highlight is the "grand multi-tiered keep, robust stone walls, and classical cherry blossom scenery." Lines of pink flower walls stretch alongside the gentle waterways, and when the calm moat reflects the white castle tower and the full bloom of cherry trees, the surface is adorned with floating cherry blossom rafts, showcasing the magnificent charm of this historic city in Kitakyushu.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained ancient pathways, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. Inside the keep, you'll find a rich exhibition on the history and culture of Kokura, complete with an elevator. Set aside an hour for a leisurely stroll; visiting on weekday mornings offers the least crowds, making it the perfect time for capturing stunning photos of this historic castle's mirror-like reflections.

大濠公園.address: 福岡県福岡市中央区大濠公園
大濠公園.access_train: 1 minute walk from the subway "Ōhori Kōen Station"
大濠公園.access_car: About 10 minutes drive from Fukuoka city center
大濠公園.tagline: Discover serene beauty at a historic castle moat, where cherry blossoms and classic Japanese gardens await.
大濠公園.desc:
Ōhori Kōen is a magnificent urban park that cleverly utilizes the old moat of Fukuoka Castle, perfectly embodying the charm of West Lake. Each year, from late March to early April, the park bursts into bloom as hundreds of ancient Somei Yoshino cherry trees reach their peak along the 2-kilometer lakeside walking path and adjacent Kannon Pond.

The park's visual highlight lies in the "expansive lake, wooden willow island bridge reflected in the water, and walls of pink blossoms intertwined." Rows of pink flower walls extend alongside the gentle waterside boardwalk, and when the calm, mirror-like surface reflects the distant city skyline and vibrant flower clusters, the space feels vast and refreshing, exuding an elegant Eastern Zen atmosphere.

The park's paths are fully renovated and accessible, featuring well-maintained jogging trails and walkways that are friendly for strollers and seniors. You can also rent pedal boats to enjoy cherry blossom viewing from the water. Set aside an hour for a leisurely stroll; the lake is most tranquil in the early morning on weekdays, making it perfect for capturing stunning mirrored landscapes.

甘木公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from Amagi Station on the Amagi Railway
甘木公園.access_car: About a 10-minute drive from the Amagi IC on the Oita Expressway
甘木公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of 4,000 cherry blossoms at Amagi Park's vibrant oasis!
甘木公園.desc:
Amagi Park is a sprawling 20-hectare landscape destination, centered around the expansive Maruyama Pond and surrounded by natural hills. From late March to early April each year, the park bursts into color as 4,000 cherry blossoms, including the famous Somei Yoshino and Shidarezakura, blanket the lakeside in a stunning display.

The most iconic scene features the "giant colorful fountain in the lake, the vermilion drum bridge, and the pink flower wall creating a mirrored masterpiece." Pink flower trails stretch along the gentle water's edge, and when the calm, mirror-like surface reflects the vibrant bridge and the full bloom of cherry trees, it creates an expansive sense of space that showcases the ultimate in Japanese aesthetic beauty.

The park's flat terrain and well-maintained paths make it very accessible for seniors and strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, lively evening illuminations and food stalls add to the excitement. For the best experience, visit early in the morning on weekdays when crowds are minimal, perfect for capturing stunning reflections on the water.

秋月杉之馬場.address: 福岡県朝倉市秋月野鳥
秋月杉之馬場.access_train: Take a bus from Amagi Railway's "Amagi Station" to "Akizuki," then walk about 10 minutes.
秋月杉之馬場.access_car: Approximately a 20-minute drive from the Oita Expressway's "Amagi IC."
秋月杉之馬場.tagline: Step back in time at Akizuki, where cherry blossoms and history bloom together.
秋月杉之馬場.desc:
Akizuki Sugino Baba is a beautifully preserved historical area that showcases the ancient horse racing grounds of the Edo period in Chikuzen Akizuki. In the late Edo period, the original cedar trees were replaced with cherry blossoms, creating a stunning 500-meter-long avenue lined with over 200 ancient Somei Yoshino cherry trees that burst into a sea of pink from late March to early April.

The core visual highlight is the "dialogue between the imposing black wooden gate, the samurai residence gate, and the pink cherry blossom tunnel." Vibrant pink blooms arch overhead, intertwining with the solemn Akizuki Castle's "Kuro-mon" and the serene moss-covered surroundings, showcasing a refined aesthetic of historical beauty and rustic landscapes.

The historical street is completely flat, with wide and well-maintained pathways, making it very friendly for elderly visitors and those with strollers. During the cherry blossom festival, countless lanterns are hung at night, creating a magical atmosphere. For the best experience, visit early in the morning on weekdays when the crowds are minimal, perfect for capturing the beauty of this historic castle without anyone in sight.

小城公園.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from JR "Ogi Station"
小城公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Saga Yamato IC on the Nagasaki Expressway
小城公園.tagline: Discover the timeless beauty of cherry blossoms at this historic Edo-period garden.
小城公園.desc:
小城公園 is a historical garden crafted by the first and second lords of the 小城 domain, Nabeshima Motonobu and Nabeshima Naozane. It perfectly embodies the grandeur of the Edo period and is listed among Japan's top 100 cherry blossom spots. Each year, from late March to early April, the park bursts into bloom with around 3,000 ancient Somei Yoshino cherry trees, creating a magnificent spectacle.

The park's core feature is the exquisite interplay of a classical pond, ancient moss, and walls of pink blossoms. Colorful clusters of flowers stretch along the paths beside the “Shinji Pond” and the “O-Kusu,” while the tranquil, mirror-like surface reflects the vibrant red torii gate of 岡山神社 and the full canopy of cherry blossoms, showcasing the ultimate in Japanese aesthetic beauty.

The park's flat terrain and well-maintained paths make it very accessible for seniors and strollers. There is a large lawn area where countless snow lanterns are hung during the cherry blossom festival, creating a lively atmosphere at night. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, making it the perfect time to capture the serene essence of this classical garden.

神野公園.access_car: About a 10-minute drive from Nagasaki Expressway Saga Yamato IC
神野公園.tagline: Experience the beauty of cherry blossoms at a historic samurai garden.
神野公園.desc:
神野公園 is a stunning stroll garden built around the ruins of the Edo period Saga clan lord Nabeshima Naomasa's retreat, "神野茶屋." Every year from late March to early April, countless Somei Yoshino cherry trees bloom around the central pond, with around 1,000 trees transforming this sacred space into a vibrant tapestry of colors.

The visual highlight is the "exquisite classical wooden bridge, Japanese tea house, and the mirrored reflection of cherry blossoms." Colorful clusters of flowers bloom along the gently winding gravel paths, and when the calm, mirror-like pond perfectly reflects the famous "隔林亭" Edo tea house and the full cherry blossom canopy, the expansive space radiates an elegant Eastern Zen ambiance.

The garden's layout is flat and well-planned, making the main gravel paths very accessible for strollers and elderly visitors. There’s also a small playground for children and a large grassy area, perfect for families to enjoy a leisurely picnic. Visiting early in the morning on weekdays offers the least crowds, making it ideal for capturing stunning mirror-like views.

御船山樂園.fee: 大人 600 円（隨季節調整）
御船山樂園.address: 佐賀県武雄市武雄町大字武雄4100
御船山樂園.access_train: About 5 minutes by taxi from JR Takeo Onsen Station
御船山樂園.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes from Nagasaki Expressway Takeo-Kitagata IC
御船山樂園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of 2,000 cherry blossoms against the stunning backdrop of Mount Mifuneyama.
御船山樂園.desc:
Mifuneyama Rakuen is a vast, traditional Japanese garden spanning 150,000 square meters, created over many years by the lord of Takeo, Nabeshima Shigeaki. Each year from late March to early April, the garden comes alive with a spectacular display of 2,000 cherry trees, including Somei Yoshino and Oshima sakura, painting the landscape in vibrant colors.

The highlight of this enchanting spot is undoubtedly the top-tier "Sakura Night Illumination" in all of Japan. Standing by the tranquil waters of the pond, you'll be mesmerized by the illuminated pink blossoms, set against the rugged cliffs of Mount Mifuneyama. The reflection of this breathtaking scene on the calm water creates a truly magical experience.

The park is spacious and well-designed, with gentle slopes that make it accessible for strollers and those with mobility challenges. Within the grounds, you'll find the famous "Chikurin-tei" and contemporary art exhibitions. Arriving around 4 PM is ideal, allowing you to capture the vibrant colors of the sunset and the enchanting night views.

鏡山公園.access_train: Walk from JR 'Niji no Matsubara Station' to the trailhead and hike up.
鏡山公園.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from the Nishikyushu Expressway 'Karatsu IC' to the summit.
鏡山公園.tagline: Experience breathtaking views of Karatsu Bay and 500 cherry blossoms at the summit of Kagamiyama Park.
鏡山公園.desc:
Kagamiyama Park is perched at an elevation of 284 meters on the summit of Kagamiyama, offering a panoramic view of nationally designated scenic spots and the historical myths of Manyoshu. From late March to early April each year, the park's famous slopes and trails burst into bloom with around 500 Somei Yoshino cherry trees and mountain cherry blossoms in synchrony.

The most stunning sight here is the unbeatable combination of the Karatsu Bay seascape, the renowned 'Niji no Matsubara,' and a three-dimensional pink flower carpet. Standing at the wooden observation deck, you can gaze upon the winding blue coastline, millions of lush black pines, and the colorful carpet of yellow and red flowers at your feet, creating a magnificent scene.

While you can drive along the scenic road to the mountaintop parking lot, be aware that there are some natural gravel paths between the observation points. For cherry blossom viewing and photography, it's highly recommended to wear comfortable athletic shoes. The summit features a spacious grassy area and historical monuments. Early morning is the best time for clear air, making it ideal for capturing the most visible scenic views.

浦之崎站.address: 佐賀県伊万里市山代町立岩
浦之崎站.access_train: Arrive at Matsura Railway's Uranosaki Station
浦之崎站.access_car: Approximately 20 minutes drive from Imari city center
浦之崎站.tagline: Discover a hidden gem where vintage trains glide through a breathtaking cherry blossom tunnel.
浦之崎站.desc:
Uranosaki Station, located along the coast of Imari Bay, is a legendary railway spot known as "Sakura no Eki" (Cherry Blossom Station) due to its platform being enveloped by dense cherry blossoms on both sides. Every year from late March to early April, around 90 ancient Somei Yoshino cherry trees, planted by local residents during the Taisho era, burst into full bloom.  
  
The most stunning visual experience is the "romantic encounter of a vintage local train and a pink floral wall." Standing on the safety viewing platform, as the brightly painted Matsura Railway vintage train slowly pulls into the cherry blossom platform, the train is surrounded by an immersive wall of flowers, creating a dynamic and nostalgic scene.  
  
This spot is an operational public railway station, so please be mindful of platform safety and avoid crossing the yellow line while exploring and capturing angles. As a favorite among train enthusiasts, train services are limited, so be sure to check the timetable before your visit. Early morning visits on weekdays offer the best chance for ethereal, expansive shots.

大村公園.address: 長崎県大村市玖島1丁目45
大村公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Omura Station to 'Shiyakusho-mae'
大村公園.access_car: About a 10-minute drive from Nagasaki Expressway 'Omura IC'
大村公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of cherry blossoms at Omura Park, a historical gem of Japan!
大村公園.desc:
Omura Park is built on the historic site of the Edo period's Omura clan's Koshima Castle, making it the only location in Nagasaki Prefecture recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots. From late March to mid-April, the park bursts into bloom with around 2,000 cherry trees. In addition to the popular Somei Yoshino variety, you can also find the rare Omura-zakura, a national natural monument with up to 300 trees boasting petals ranging from sixty to two hundred.  

The park's core feature is its classical scenery, with the white walls of the castle tower, sturdy stone walls, and the old moat adorned with cherry blossoms. The vibrant blooms flourish along the gentle waterways, and when the tranquil waters of the old moat perfectly reflect the majestic castle, the nostalgic Otemon gate, and the flower rafts formed by falling petals, it creates an expansive visual experience showcasing the unique Japanese aesthetic of a feudal lord's residence.  

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained ancient pathways, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. Nearby, there are historical exhibition halls related to the Christian daimyō, perfect for travelers looking to enjoy a leisurely self-drive day trip. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, allowing you to fully capture the fresh air and the perfect mirror-like scenery.

風頭公園.address: 長崎県長崎市伊良林2丁目
風頭公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Nagasaki Station to 'Kazeato Mountain' and walk for about 5 minutes.
風頭公園.access_car: There is no parking available at the park; public transportation is recommended.
風頭公園.tagline: Ascend to the heights of Kazeato Park, where cherry blossoms and Sakamoto Ryoma's statue create a breathtaking scene.
風頭公園.desc:
Kazeato Park is perched atop Kazeato Mountain (152 meters above sea level) in the eastern part of Nagasaki City. This expansive mountainous park is renowned for its panoramic views of the stunning Nagasaki harbor and the historical statue of Sakamoto Ryoma. From late March to early April, the park's natural hillside terrain bursts into bloom with around 350 cherry blossom trees, specifically the Somei Yoshino variety.

The main visual highlight is the legendary statue of Sakamoto Ryoma framed by the Nagasaki harbor skyline and a vibrant pink floral carpet. Stroll along the well-maintained walking paths, where colorful blossoms contrast beautifully with the majestic Megami Ohashi bridge and the serene green sea. During the cherry blossom festival, up to 3,000 vintage snow lanterns illuminate the park at night, creating a truly magical atmosphere.

Since the park is located on a natural hillside, reaching the main observation deck from the bus stop requires a walk along a sloped gravel path. It is highly recommended to wear comfortable athletic shoes for cherry blossom photography. The elevated area is well-shaded and feels pleasantly cool. Visiting on weekday mornings offers the best visibility for capturing the breathtaking scenery.

平和公園.access_train: About a 3-minute walk from Nagasaki Tram "Heiwa Koen Station"
平和公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Nagasaki IC
平和公園.tagline: A serene oasis at the heart of Nagasaki, where hope blooms amidst the cherry blossoms and peace statues.
平和公園.desc:
Heiwa Koen is a world-class historical memorial park built around the epicenter of the Nagasaki atomic bomb, dedicated to proclaiming peace to the world. Each year, from late March to early April, the park bursts into life with hundreds of Somei Yoshino cherry trees in full bloom along the gentle pathways surrounding the central buildings and fountain.

The most striking modern composition features the "Bronze Giant Peace Statue, the Fountain of Peace, and the pink flower wall" in a breathtaking tableau. Strolling along the smooth stone paths, you’ll find lush clusters of blossoms enveloping you like clouds. When the clear fountain water perfectly reflects the blue sky, the solemn statue, and the expansive cherry blossom canopy, the scene is both grand and deeply moving.

The park's terrain is completely flat, equipped with excellent escalators and accessible pathways, making it very friendly for elderly visitors. With great amenities nearby, it's perfect for independent travelers looking to leisurely connect with the nearby Urakami Cathedral. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest visitors, making it the ideal time for capturing the pristine historical and cultural landscape without crowds.

長崎公園.address: 長崎県長崎市立山1丁目
長崎公園.access_train: 10-minute walk from Sakuramachi Station on the tram
長崎公園.access_car: About 5 minutes by car from Nagasaki Station
長崎公園.tagline: Discover history and beauty in Nagasaki's oldest urban park, where cherry blossoms and tranquility await.
長崎公園.desc:
Nagasaki Park, established in 1873, is the oldest public urban park in Nagasaki City, adjacent to the famous Suwa Shrine. Every year from late March to early April, hundreds of cherry blossoms, particularly the Somei Yoshino variety, burst into bloom around the classical fountain and historic stone lanterns, creating a serene and picturesque atmosphere.

The park's visual highlights include the grand vermilion torii gate, the Taisho-era brick fountain, and the stunning pink flower wall. Colorful blooms surround the ancient wooden shrine, and as you stroll along the gentle gravel paths, the calm water perfectly reflects the vintage Western-style fountain and the vibrant foliage, embodying Nagasaki's unique blend of Japanese and Western aesthetics.

The park's layout is flat and well-planned, making it easy to navigate, although the path to the shrine's highest point features some historic stone steps. A free small animal area is also available, making it perfect for family outings. We highly recommend visiting on weekday mornings to capture the park's ethereal Zen ambiance.

橘公園.address: 長崎県雲仙市千々石町己
橘公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Isahaya Station to Tachibana Shrine Mae.
橘公園.access_car: About a 40-minute drive from Nagasaki Expressway Isahaya IC.
橘公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of Tachibana Park, where vibrant cherry blossoms meet historic charm.
橘公園.desc:
Tachibana Park is a sprawling waterside oasis surrounding the famous birthplace of the historical general Tachibana Shuta at Tachibana Shrine. From late March to early April, the park features a serene, several-kilometer-long pathway along the clear Chichigasagawa River, where around 1,000 stunning Somei Yoshino cherry trees burst into bloom.

The park's visual centerpiece is the striking contrast between the national-level giant torii gate, the gentle flowing stream, and the dynamic lines of the pink cherry blossom tunnel. As you stroll along the smooth riverside path, the lush branches above intertwine beautifully in the sky. When the two hundred snow cave lanterns illuminate the area at night, the calm water perfectly reflects the magnificent architecture, creating a powerful visual impact.

The terrain is completely flat and well-maintained, making it very accessible for strollers and elderly visitors. During the cherry blossom festival, lively military deity rituals take place, making it ideal for a self-drive day trip. The early morning hours offer the freshest air and the calmest water, perfect for capturing stunning reflective photographs.

熊本城.address: 熊本県熊本市中央区本丸1-1
熊本城.access_train: A 10-minute walk from the tram stop 'Kumamotojo・Shiyakusho-mae'
熊本城.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from the Kyushu Expressway 'Kumamoto IC'
熊本城.desc:
Kumamoto Castle, built by the renowned general Kato Kiyomasa, is one of Japan's three great castles and a top-tier cherry blossom viewing spot, recognized among Japan's 100 best cherry blossom sites. From late March to early April, around 800 cherry trees, including Somei Yoshino and Higoh cherry blossoms, burst into bloom around the majestic old moat and the grand donjon.

The castle's core feature is the stunning contrast of the black and white layered donjon, the dramatic cliffside stone walls known as 'Musha Kaeshi,' and the classical landscape of cherry blossoms. Vibrant clusters of flowers bloom alongside the gentle waterways, and when the calm surface of the inner moat perfectly reflects the towering white walls and the expansive cherry blossom canopy, the scene is a harmonious blend of strength and grace, creating a majestic atmosphere.

The castle has been fully restored after the earthquake, featuring a new barrier-free aerial walkway that is very friendly for strollers and elderly visitors. Inside the donjon, there is a new digital history exhibition and elevators. On regular days, the gates open at 9 AM, allowing you to capture the perfect mirror-like views of this historic castle without crowds.
熊本城.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of cherry blossoms at one of Japan's top three castles!

一心行大櫻.address: 熊本県阿蘇郡南阿蘇村中松3226-1
一心行大櫻.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from Nakasatsu Station on the Minami Aso Railway
一心行大櫻.access_car: Approximately a 50-minute drive from Kumamoto IC on the Kyushu Expressway
一心行大櫻.tagline: Experience the majestic 400-year-old cherry blossom and vibrant canola fields in the heart of Aso.
一心行大櫻.desc:
Isshinkyō Ōzakura is a stunning cherry tree over 400 years old, believed to have been planted by the family of the fallen samurai from the Warring States period, including Lord Nakamura Hōki of Yazaki Castle. Each year, from late March to early April, this towering giant, reaching 14 meters high and spanning 32 meters wide, transforms into a breathtaking spectacle.

The most striking visual experience is the "dialogue between the majestic Aso Five Peaks, the golden blanket of canola flowers, and the ancient giant tree." In the rustic fields, this solitary giant stands tall, its lush pale pink blossoms enveloping it like clouds. When the peak bloom falls, it carpets the ground with petals, creating a vibrant contrast with the golden flower sea at your feet.

To protect the delicate roots of this ancient tree, a well-constructed wooden fence and smooth pathways have been established around the cherry blossom, making it easy to explore. As one of Japan's most popular single giant tree attractions, expect significant traffic congestion during the blooming season. The highland area can feel a bit cool, so be sure to bring a warm jacket for your visit.

市房大壩.address: 熊本県球磨郡水上村湯山
市房大壩.access_train: About a 15-minute bus ride from Kumagawa Railway's Yumemae Station
市房大壩.access_car: About a 50-minute drive from Kyushu Expressway's Hitoyoshi IC
市房大壩.tagline: Experience a breathtaking 13-kilometer cherry blossom corridor at this hidden oasis in Kumamoto.
市房大壩.desc:
Ichibō Dam (Ichibō Dam Lake) is located upstream of the Kumagawa River and features a massive reservoir formed by a giant dam. It is also recognized as one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots in Kyushu. Every year from late March to early April, over 10,000 Yoshino cherry trees burst into bloom along the gentle lakeside road that stretches for more than 13 kilometers.

The most stunning visual experience is the interplay of the 80-meter tall fountain, the emerald lake, and the pink flower walls. Rows of pink blossoms bloom alongside the smooth gravel embankment, and when the calm, mirror-like water reflects the majestic dam, the cruising sightseeing boats, and vibrant flower clusters, the scene is truly breathtaking.

The lakeside trail is gently undulating, with well-designed wooden paths that are very friendly for seniors and strollers. The area around the dam is equipped with excellent camping sites and hot springs. For the best experience, visit on a clear, windless morning to capture the perfect reflection of the colorful foliage on the still water.

立岡自然公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Uto Station
立岡自然公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Matsubashi IC on the Kyushu Expressway
立岡自然公園.tagline: Discover a serene oasis of 2,000 Yoshino cherries reflecting in the tranquil waters of Tachioka Park.
立岡自然公園.desc:
Tachioka Shizen Koen is a comprehensive natural ecological park centered around a historical irrigation reservoir established by Kato Kiyomasa during the Edo period. From late March to early April each year, the park comes alive with up to 2,000 Somei Yoshino cherry trees blooming spectacularly along the shores of the expansive Tachioka Pond and Garden Pond.

The visual highlights include the classic three-arched wooden drum bridge, the serene pond, and the enchanting pink tunnel reflecting in the water. Stroll along the smooth riverbank paths as the lush blossoms intertwine above, and witness the stunning reflection of the historic red bridge and the cherry blossom canopy on the mirror-like water, showcasing refined Japanese aesthetics.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained ancient pathways, making it very accessible for seniors and strollers. The spacious lawns are perfect for families looking to enjoy a leisurely picnic amidst nature. Set aside an hour for a stroll; the lake is calmest in the early morning, providing the best conditions for capturing perfect mirror-like scenery.

菊池公園.types: 22 varieties including Somei Yoshino and Yamazakura
菊池公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR "Kumamoto Station" to "Kikuchi Onsen" and walk about 10 minutes.
菊池公園.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from the Kyushu Expressway "Ueki IC."
菊池公園.tagline: Discover the breathtaking beauty of cherry blossoms at the historic Kikuchi Park!
菊池公園.desc:
Kikuchi Park is built on the historic site of the Kikuchi clan's "Kikuchi Castle," offering a panoramic view of the entire Kikuchi Plain. From late March to early April each year, the park bursts into bloom with around 3,000 cherry trees, featuring 22 varieties, including the famous Somei Yoshino and Yamazakura, especially along the scenic slopes and pathways.

The park's core visual highlight is the stunning interplay of the ancient wooden Kannon Hall, lush green fields, and a vibrant carpet of colorful flowers. Pink flower walls stretch alongside the natural rolling hills, and as the morning light filters through the treetops onto the stone path, the majestic Tahōtō tower creates a beautifully layered scene filled with a sense of history.

The main summit area is well-maintained and accessible, featuring large, wheelchair-friendly slopes that are perfect for strollers and seniors. Located near the famous Kikuchi Onsen, it’s an ideal spot for a day trip. Visiting in the early morning on weekdays means fewer crowds, making it the perfect time to capture the serene beauty of this sacred space and the colorful autumn foliage.

岡城阯.address: 大分県竹田市大字竹田2761
岡城阯.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Bungo-Takeda Station
岡城阯.access_car: Approximately 1 hour drive from Oita City
岡城阯.tagline: Discover the enchanting realm of Takitaro's "Kojou no Tsuki" amidst breathtaking cherry blossoms and majestic mountains.
岡城阯.desc:
Okajou (Okajou Park) is a magnificent medieval castle built on a steep cliff at an elevation of 325 meters, commissioned by Minamoto no Yoshitsune. It gained national fame as the birthplace of composer Takitaro's renowned piece, "Kojou no Tsuki." Every year in early April, around a thousand mountain cherry trees burst into bloom against the dramatic backdrop of rugged basalt rock formations.

The most striking aspect of this place lies in its breathtaking aesthetics: the heavy stone walls, the nine peaks, and the vibrant tapestry of flowers create a stunning visual feast. As you ascend the ancient, winding stone path, the white walls and tiled roofs have vanished, leaving only the solemn, rustic stone structures. When the afternoon mist envelops the mountains, the cherry blossoms and ancient ruins intertwine, evoking a profound sense of wabi-sabi zen.

Due to the castle's location on steep mountainous terrain, reaching the main keep from the entrance requires climbing a series of steep, ancient stone steps. It is highly recommended to wear sturdy hiking shoes for cherry blossom viewing and photography. The early morning air is especially ethereal, making it the perfect time to capture the story-rich landscapes.

別府公園.address: 大分県別府市野口元町12
別府公園.access_car: 15-minute drive from Beppu IC on the Oita Expressway
別府公園.tagline: Discover the serene beauty of Beppu Park, where ancient pines and vibrant cherry blossoms unite.
別府公園.desc:
Beppu Park is located in the heart of the famous Beppu Onsen area, spanning an impressive 27 hectares. This expansive urban oasis focuses on temperate plant landscapes and offers a relaxing retreat for locals and visitors alike. Every year, from late March to early April, the park reaches its peak bloom, showcasing over 200 stunning Shidarezakura and Oshima cherry trees amidst towering black pines.

The park's main feature is the striking contrast between the century-old rugged black pines, clear waterways, and vibrant pink flower walls. Stroll along the smooth stone pathways, where the vivid pink blossoms intertwine with the ancient green needles of the pines, complemented by the colorful tulip fields stretching beneath your feet. The spacious layout and rich color palette create a breathtaking scene.

The park's terrain is mostly flat, with well-maintained, accessible pathways and grassy areas, making it very friendly for seniors and strollers. There’s also a cozy wooden café within the park. For the best experience, visit early on weekdays to enjoy the pure, unspoiled beauty of nature without the crowds.

平和市民公園.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR "Maki Station"
平和市民公園.access_car: Approximately a 15-minute drive from Oita Expressway "Oita-Miyara IC"
平和市民公園.tagline: Stroll along the banks of the Oita River and be mesmerized by a two-kilometer pink tunnel of Yoshino cherry blossoms.
平和市民公園.desc:
Heiwa Shimin Koen is a sprawling modern urban park that stretches for several kilometers along the banks of the Oita River's tributary. From late March to early April each year, over 800 cherry blossom trees, mainly the Somei Yoshino variety, burst into bloom around the park's iconic Japanese garden and samurai residence-style buildings, transforming the area into a stunning pink tunnel.

The visual highlight is the "classic wooden bridge, tranquil pond, and the mirrored reflection of the pink flower wall." Colorful blossoms line the gentle waterways, and when the calm, mirror-like surface perfectly reflects the park's famous Noh theater, classic architecture, greenery, and expansive cherry blossom groves, it creates a spacious scene that showcases elegant Japanese aesthetics.

The riverside promenade is completely flat and well-paved, making it very friendly for strollers and joggers. It's open to the public for free all day. The park's grassy areas are plentiful. For the best chance to capture a perfect, ripple-free water mirror and clear sky, local photographers highly recommend visiting on weekday mornings.

一心寺.types: 15 varieties including double cherry and kanzan cherry
一心寺.count: about 1,000 trees (famous for its botan cherry)
一心寺.address: 大分県大分市大字廻栖野1305
一心寺.access_train: Take a bus from JR Oita Station to Higashi-Makizono, then walk about 15 minutes.
一心寺.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes drive from Oita Expressway Oita Hikiyoshi IC.
一心寺.tagline: Discover the breathtaking beauty of Oita's hidden cherry blossom valley, where 20,000 'Kyushu's finest' double cherry trees bloom.
一心寺.desc:
Fudōson Isshinji Temple is nestled in a deep mountain basin in southern Oita City, revered as the 'Number One Cherry Blossom Valley in Japan.' Due to its higher elevation, the blooming season here is later than in the plains, reaching its peak in mid-April, with 20,000 double cherry trees of 15 varieties bursting into bloom across the expansive grounds.

The most stunning visual impact comes from the 'majestic mountain ranges framed against the grand white-walled main hall, complemented by a vibrant wall of flowers.' Strolling along the winding scenic path, you’ll find yourself enveloped in a pink tunnel formed by towering cherry trees. When the ethereal sea of clouds drifts through the valley in the early morning, the scene is incredibly three-dimensional.

The entire temple is deeply embedded in the steep valley terrain, with a clearly undulating path mostly made of natural gravel. For those planning to take photos, it’s highly recommended to wear professional running shoes with good grip and carry a lightweight, windproof jacket. The temple charges an entrance donation. Visiting on a weekday morning is the best way to capture the stunning, ethereal sea of clouds that will leave you in awe.

不動尊一心寺.address: 大分県大分市大字廻栖野1305
不動尊一心寺.access_train: About 25 minutes by taxi from JR Oita Station
不動尊一心寺.access_car: About 15 minutes from Oita Kouguchi IC towards Route 442
不動尊一心寺.tagline: Discover the breathtaking beauty of Kyushu's premier cherry blossom haven!
不動尊一心寺.desc:
Fudouson Isshinji, nestled in the deep mountains of southern Oita City, is revered as the "Best Yaezakura in Japan." Located in a high-altitude valley, the blossoms bloom later, with around 20,000 Yaezakura trees bursting into color by mid-April.

The most stunning sight here is the dramatic backdrop of towering mountains framing the temple's white walls, complemented by a vibrant sea of blossoms. As you stroll along the winding path, the towering cherry trees create a dense pink tunnel. When the ethereal sea of clouds drifts through the valley in the early morning, it feels like stepping into a fairyland.

The temple is deeply embedded in the steep valley, with a path that is uneven and often rocky. It's highly recommended to wear good grip running shoes and bring a lightweight, windproof jacket. A small entrance fee is collected for visiting the temple. For the best experience, visit on a weekday morning to capture the stunning, otherworldly views of the cloud sea.

西都原古墳群.types: Somei Yoshino cherry (paired with 300,000 canola flowers)
西都原古墳群.access_train: Take a bus from JR Miyazaki Station to Nishito Bus Center, then walk about 15 minutes.
西都原古墳群.access_car: About a 10-minute drive from Nishito IC on the Higashi Kyushu Expressway.
西都原古墳群.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of ancient royal tombs surrounded by a sea of cherry blossoms and canola flowers.
西都原古墳群.desc:
Nishitohara Kofungun is a national designated special historic site comprising over 300 ancient royal tombs. From late March to early April, the cherry blossoms reach their peak, with 2,000 Somei Yoshino cherry trees and 300,000 canola flowers bursting into bloom around the majestic "Oni no Kutsu Kofun".

The must-see spectacle features a stunning composition of a "golden canola flower blanket, a pink cherry blossom forest, and a deep blue sky." Stroll along the gentle gravel paths, where colorful flower clusters blend seamlessly with the vast fields of bright yellow flowers stretching to the horizon. As the morning sunlight pours down, the scene becomes a harmonious blend of softness and vibrancy, creating an endless sense of space.

The entire archaeological site is mostly flat, equipped with well-maintained accessible wooden walkways and a large free parking area, making it very friendly for elderly visitors and strollers. An archaeological museum is also located within the park. For the best experience, visit on a clear, windless morning when the colors of the flower fields are at their most vibrant.

垂水公園.address: 宮崎県宮崎市大字瓜生野
垂水公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Miyazaki Station to Tairui Station.
垂水公園.access_car: About a 25-minute drive from Miyazaki City.
垂水公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of 3,500 cherry blossoms along the iconic Shimonokata Sakura Road.
垂水公園.desc:
Tairui Park is nestled in the hilly highlands of northern Miyazaki Plain, making it one of the most representative natural parks in Miyazaki City. Each year, from late March to early April, the park reaches its peak bloom, with 3,500 stunning Somei Yoshino cherry trees bursting into color along a straight scenic road that stretches for several kilometers from the foot of the mountain to the summit of the park.

The most striking feature here is the renowned "Sakura Grand Tunnel" that captivates visitors. Strolling along the gently paved stone path, you'll find vibrant pink walls of blossoms on both sides, creating an enchanting atmosphere. Climb to the highest observation deck for a panoramic view that captures the entire pink floral blanket while also overlooking the shimmering Hyuga Beach in the distance.

The park's pathways are well-maintained and flat, featuring a large grassy area perfect for families to enjoy a leisurely picnic. During the cherry blossom festival, countless lanterns illuminate the park at night, creating a magical ambiance. Visit on a clear morning to capture the most vivid and three-dimensional floral scenes in the air.

母智丘公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR "Miyakonojo Station" to "Mochio".
母智丘公園.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from Miyazaki Expressway "Miyakonojo IC".
母智丘公園.tagline: Stroll through a breathtaking pink tunnel of Yoshino cherry blossoms in the historic approach of Mochio Park.
母智丘公園.desc:
Mochio Park is a premier cherry blossom viewing spot in South Kyushu, extending along the ancient approach to Mochio Shrine. It is recognized as one of Japan's top 100 cherry blossom sites. Every year, from late March to early April, the 2-kilometer-long walking path on both sides bursts into bloom with 2,600 magnificent ancient Somei Yoshino and Yaezakura cherry trees.

The park's most striking feature is the "endless, double-row cherry tree tunnel" that creates a grand pink archway. As you leisurely walk along the smooth stone-paved approach, the lush branches intertwine above, beautifully contrasting with the traditional stone lanterns that have stood the test of time. During the cherry blossom fall, the ground becomes a stunning pink carpet, creating a powerful visual impact.

The approach is mostly flat and well-maintained, making it very accessible for seniors and those with strollers. From the shrine at the top of the hill, you can overlook the entire pink dragon of blossoms. During the cherry blossom festival, enchanting night illuminations are held. For the best experience, arrive early on weekdays before dawn to capture the fresh morning air and the serene, crowd-free scenery.

綾馬事公苑.address: 宮崎県東諸県郡綾町大字北俣2638
綾馬事公苑.access_train: Take the JR train to Miyazaki Station and catch a bus to Ayacho until the final stop.
綾馬事公苑.access_car: Approximately a 40-minute drive from Miyazaki City.
綾馬事公苑.tagline: A serene oasis of equestrian history beneath the lush canopy of Ayacho, where majestic bronze horses meet blooming cherry blossoms.
綾馬事公苑.desc:
Ayama Jikoen is nestled in the heart of Ayacho, renowned for its top-tier evergreen forests in Japan. This comprehensive landscape park beautifully merges traditional equestrian culture with nature. From late March to early April each year, the park's expansive racetrack and gentle pathways burst into bloom with up to 1,000 cherry blossoms of the Somei Yoshino variety.

The park's standout feature is the "giant bronze horse statue, verdant fields, and pink floral walls in harmonious contrast." Rows of pink flower walls stretch alongside the gentle lawns, and as the morning light filters through the treetops onto the racetrack, it creates an elegant rural aesthetic, framed by the majestic Ayame Bridge in the distance.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with wide, well-maintained paths that are very friendly for strollers and leisurely travelers. There are also interactive facilities where visitors can engage with the horses. Mornings on weekdays see fewer visitors, making it the perfect time to capture the pristine pastoral scenery and vibrant autumnal views.

觀音池公園.address: 宮崎県都城市高城町石山4195
觀音池公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR 'Miyakonojo Station' to 'Kannon Pond Park Entrance'
觀音池公園.access_car: Approximately a 10-minute drive from Miyazaki Expressway 'Miyakonojo IC'
觀音池公園.tagline: Discover the serene beauty of Kannon Pond Park, where history meets nature in a stunning floral display.
觀音池公園.desc:
Kannon Pond Park is a vast natural ecological park built around the expansive Kannon Pond and the historic Kannon-do, covering several dozen hectares. From late March to early April each year, the park's famous waterway and pathways burst into bloom with around 3,000 Shidare Yoshino cherry trees.  
  
The visual highlight is the giant vermilion suspension bridge, the tranquil pond, and the classical backdrop of pink flower walls. Colorful blossoms bloom alongside the gentle waterways, and when the mirror-like surface of the water perfectly reflects the vibrant bridge, vintage rowboats, and the colorful forest, the scene exudes an elegant Eastern aesthetic.  
  
The park features gently rolling terrain, with well-maintained, accessible pathways and wooden boardwalks that are very friendly for seniors and strollers. Facilities include hot spring amenities, large playground equipment, and camping areas. Take an hour to stroll, especially in the early morning when the lake is at its calmest, perfect for capturing stunning mirrored landscapes.

仙巖園.types: 寒緋桜, 染井吉野桜, 山桜
仙巖園.address: 鹿児島県鹿児島市吉野町9700-1
仙巖園.access_train: Take the sightseeing bus from JR Kagoshima Chuo Station and get off at Sen-gan-en Mae.
仙巖園.access_car: Approximately a 20-minute drive from Satsuma Yoshida IC on the Kyushu Expressway.
仙巖園.tagline: Discover a breathtaking garden where history meets nature, framed by the stunning Sakurajima volcano and Kinko Bay.
仙巖園.desc:
Sen-gan-en (Iso-teien) is a magnificent historical garden, meticulously crafted by Satsuma lord Shimazu Mitsuhisa in the early Edo period, and recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage site. From early February to early April, the garden bursts into color as寒緋桜 (kanhi zakura), 山桜 (yamazakura), and染井吉野桜 (somei yoshino) bloom in a stunning display.

What sets this garden apart is its unparalleled view, featuring the "breathtaking scenery of Kinko Bay, the majestic active volcano Sakurajima, and the delicate pink weeping cherry blossoms all in one frame." Standing before the elegant wooden corridor of the御殿 (Goten), the tranquil mirror-like sea and the distant, smoke-emitting volcano create a dramatic interplay, showcasing the grandeur of Satsuma aesthetics.

The garden's terrain is mostly flat, with well-maintained stone paths and grassy areas, making it very wheelchair and stroller-friendly. It also features a rich exhibition on Satsuma's modern industrial history and a high-end Shimazu tea house. Arriving early on weekdays is ideal for capturing the serene essence of this classical garden.

忠元公園.address: 鹿児島県伊佐市大口原田2484-193
忠元公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR "Shinmatsue Station" for about 1 hour to "Ōkuchi."
忠元公園.access_car: Approximately 25 minutes from Kyushu Expressway "Kurino IC."
忠元公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking pink tunnel of cherry blossoms at the historic site of Chūgen Park.
忠元公園.desc:
Chūgen Park, established on the historical site of the castle of the famous Sengoku period general, Niinō Chūgen, is one of Kagoshima Prefecture's most iconic cherry blossom viewing spots, recognized as one of Japan's top 100 cherry blossom locations. From late March to early April, the park's straight 2-kilometer approach is lined with over a thousand stunning Somei Yoshino cherry trees in full bloom.

The most striking visual experience is the "two-kilometer-long, massive pink tunnel formed by cherry trees." As you stroll along the smooth stone path, the lush clusters of blossoms intertwine above you, creating a mesmerizing canopy. At night, thousands of vintage snow lanterns illuminate the scene, beautifully complementing the wooden shrine of Chūgen Jinja, evoking a deep sense of history.

The park's terrain is completely flat, with well-maintained ancient pathways, making it very accessible for seniors and families with strollers. There is a large grassy area perfect for families to enjoy together. Take an hour to wander, especially in the early morning when the crowds are minimal, and the sunlight captures the ethereal Zen atmosphere in the air.

甲突川綠地.address: 鹿児島県鹿児島市加治屋町周辺
甲突川綠地.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from JR Kagoshima Chūō Station
甲突川綠地.access_car: Recommended to park at paid lots around the station.
甲突川綠地.tagline: Stroll along Kagoshima's riverside, enchanted by a stunning 5-kilometer corridor of Yoshino cherry blossoms.
甲突川綠地.desc:
Kōtsukigawa Ryokuchi stretches along both banks of the Kōtsukigawa River in the heart of Kagoshima City, serving as a waterside urban oasis adjacent to the birthplace of historical figures like Saigō Takamori and Ōkubo Toshimichi. From late March to early April, the 5-kilometer-long gentle embankment pathways burst into bloom with around 500 cherry trees of the Somei Yoshino variety.

The visual highlight is the breathtaking juxtaposition of the vintage stone arch bridge, the modern city skyline, and the vibrant pink flower wall. As you leisurely stroll along the smooth wooden promenade, the lush branches on either side gracefully droop towards the river. When the calm water perfectly reflects the white-walled buildings, the historic Nishida Bridge from the Edo period, and the full canopy of cherry blossoms, the sense of space expands beautifully.
